title: The admission application form must be filled online by following the provided instructions
content: The admission application form must be filled online by following the provided instructions. Applications received through post or by hand are not accepted or entertained. The online system assigns a unique Application Reference Number to each applicant which must be noted for later use.

title: Applicants must confirm that all information entered in the admission application form is correct
content: Applicants must confirm that all information entered in the admission application form is correct. In case of incorrect information, the university reserves the right to cancel the application or admission without any fee refund.

title: The application fee must be deposited in the designated bank
content: The application fee must be deposited in the designated bank. This fee is non-refundable and non-adjustable.

title: Upon payment of the application processing fee and admission test fee, the applicant can print the admit card bearing th
applicable_campus: The applicant should visit the test campus
content: Upon payment of the application processing fee and admission test fee, the applicant can print the admit card bearing the test roll number. The applicant should visit the test campus on the specified date and time mentioned on the admit card.

title: Admission of a student automatically stands cancelled if the student fails in a required exam whose result was not avail
content: Admission of a student automatically stands cancelled if the student fails in a required exam whose result was not available at the time of admission application.

title: All fee structure details are available on the university website
content: All fee structure details are available on the university website. FAST does not have a fixed semester fee as it depends on the number of courses taken. For example, if a student enrolls in fifteen credits per semester, the fee is calculated as fifteen times the per credit fee plus the student activity fund.

title: At the time of admission, students are charged the admission fee, security deposit, tuition fee for the first semester,
content: At the time of admission, students are charged the admission fee, security deposit, tuition fee for the first semester, and student activity fund. For subsequent semesters, students are only charged the tuition fee and student activity fund. Taxes are applicable as per government rules.

title: FAST is a non-profit organization and charges subsidized fees from all students
content: FAST is a non-profit organization and charges subsidized fees from all students. The university arranges financial assistance in the form of loans for students who cannot afford the subsidized fee. This assistance is subject to renewal every semester based on academic performance and financial need.

title: Financial assistance is limited to tuition fees only
content: Financial assistance is limited to tuition fees only. The assistance is discontinued if the student's CGPA falls below two point zero for undergraduate degrees or two point five for graduate degrees.

title: Loan repayment begins three months after graduation or after securing employment, whichever occurs earlier
content: Loan repayment begins three months after graduation or after securing employment, whichever occurs earlier. The total loan amount must be repaid within four years after graduation. Students are required to sign an agreement acknowledging these terms.

title: Students who receive university loans but cannot afford the first semester payment must pay the admission fee, security
applicable_campus: For the tuition fee, students may apply for installment plans through the campus
content: Students who receive university loans but cannot afford the first semester payment must pay the admission fee, security fee, and student activity fund. For the tuition fee, students may apply for installment plans through the campus director.

title: The admission fee is non-refundable
content: The admission fee is non-refundable. The security deposit is refundable upon completion of the degree or closure of admission. Tuition fee refunds are processed in accordance with Higher Education Commission guidelines.

title: The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus can be contacted at admissions
applicable_campus: The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus
content: The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus can be contacted at admissions.cfd@nu.edu.pk. The Islamabad campus can be contacted at admissions.isb@nu.edu.pk. The Karachi campus can be contacted at admissions.khi@nu.edu.pk.

title: The Lahore campus can be contacted at admissions
applicable_campus: The Lahore campus
content: The Lahore campus can be contacted at admissions.lhr@nu.edu.pk. The Peshawar campus can be contacted at admissions.pwr@nu.edu.pk. The Multan campus can be contacted at admissions.mul@nu.edu.pk.

title: Islamabad campus phone numbers are 051-111-128-128 and 051-831-4100 through 4129
applicable_campus: Islamabad campus
content: Islamabad campus phone numbers are 051-111-128-128 and 051-831-4100 through 4129. Lahore campus phone numbers are 042-111-128-128 and 042-516-5680 through 5683. Karachi city campus phone number is 021-343-90941 through 90945.

title: Karachi main campus phone numbers are 021-111-128-128 and 021-341-00541 through 00546
applicable_campus: Karachi main campus
content: Karachi main campus phone numbers are 021-111-128-128 and 021-341-00541 through 00546. Peshawar campus phone number is 091-111-128-128. Chiniot-Faisalabad campus phone number is 041-111-128-128. Multan campus phone number is 061-111-128-128.

title: Campus working hours are generally Monday to Friday from 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM
applicable_campus: hone calls are not answered, students should email their queries to the relevant campus
content: Campus working hours are generally Monday to Friday from 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM. If phone calls are not answered, students should email their queries to the relevant campus.

title: Documents uploaded during the online application must be in JPG format and less than one megabyte in size
content: Documents uploaded during the online application must be in JPG format and less than one megabyte in size. Required documents include the national identity card, detailed marks certificate, transcript, equivalence certificate, and photograph. For assistance, students can email admissions@nu.edu.pk.

title: Students who upload incorrect documents should send the correct files to the relevant campus for correction
applicable_campus: nts who upload incorrect documents should send the correct files to the relevant campus
content: Students who upload incorrect documents should send the correct files to the relevant campus for correction. The university provides online payment facility through Kuickpay for students experiencing issues with bank payments.

title: Test patterns and sample tests are available on the official university website
content: Test patterns and sample tests are available on the official university website. Information on various admission tests is also available on the university admission website.

title: The online admission test is taken on a computer
content: The online admission test is taken on a computer. Candidates should be familiar with the use of mouse and keyboard. Each candidate receives a different examination with sections such as English, analytical skills, and mathematics appearing in random sequence. Questions related to different topics also appear in random order.

title: In each section of the test, candidates can move forward and backward to review their selected responses
content: In each section of the test, candidates can move forward and backward to review their selected responses. Candidates can change their response to any question within the current section. Each section has a fixed duration and is timed independently. Time saved in one section is not carried forward to subsequent sections.

title: The duration of the online admission test is between two and three hours
content: The duration of the online admission test is between two and three hours. At the end of the test, a summary of results is displayed on screen.

title: Applicants should reach the test center on the date and time specified on the admit card
applicable_campus: The admission test is held at all campuses
content: Applicants should reach the test center on the date and time specified on the admit card. Latecomers are not allowed to take the test. Applicants should not bring anything to the test center except their admit cards. No applicant will be allowed to take the test without an endorsed admit card. The admission test is held at all campuses of the university.

title: Students can check their test results by logging into the admission portal
content: Students can check their test results by logging into the admission portal. If delays occur on result announcement day due to server load, students should retry after a few hours. Selected candidates are informed through SMS and email. Candidates should check their admission status on the university admission website.

title: Merit is calculated based on entry test marks and past academic record
content: Merit is calculated based on entry test marks and past academic record. Detailed selection criteria information is available on the university website.

title: Foreign students can register for admission using their passport number
content: Foreign students can register for admission using their passport number. Students from Afghanistan may use their POR number. Foreign students requiring information or assistance should email intoffice@nu.edu.pk.

title: At the time of admission, foreign nationals must submit a No Objection Certificate from the Ministry of Education, Gover
content: At the time of admission, foreign nationals must submit a No Objection Certificate from the Ministry of Education, Government of Pakistan.

title: Migration from one campus to another is difficult if the student did not meet the merit requirements of the desired camp
applicable_campus: Migration from one campus
content: Migration from one campus to another is difficult if the student did not meet the merit requirements of the desired campus. In exceptional circumstances, consideration may be given as detailed in the prospectus.

title: Students transferring from another university should apply for admission as regular applicants
content: Students transferring from another university should apply for admission as regular applicants. Upon admission, they should apply for credit transfer with their department. The exact courses transferred depend on the committee's decision. As per Higher Education Commission rules, a maximum of fifty percent of credits can be transferred.

title: FAST accepts students from associate degree programs
applicable_program: FAST accepts students from associate degree programs
content: FAST accepts students from associate degree programs. These students should apply as regular applicants and upon admission apply for credit transfer with their department. The exact courses transferred depend on the committee's decision. As per Higher Education Commission rules, a maximum of fifty percent of credits can be transferred.

title: The Faisalabad, Islamabad, Karachi, and Peshawar campuses operate buses along selected routes in their respective cities
applicable_campus: The Faisalabad, Islamabad, Karachi, and Peshawar campuses
content: The Faisalabad, Islamabad, Karachi, and Peshawar campuses operate buses along selected routes in their respective cities.

title: Faisalabad and Peshawar campuses have hostel facilities available for male students
applicable_campus: Faisalabad and Peshawar campuses
content: Faisalabad and Peshawar campuses have hostel facilities available for male students. For female students, only Faisalabad campus has a university-managed hostel. For other campuses, students can access private hostel accommodations or private university-managed hostels. All campuses maintain lists of reputable hostels available upon request.

title: Each campus follows its own schedule regarding weekend classes
applicable_campus: Each campus
applicable_program: Karachi campus offers a weekend program
content: Each campus follows its own schedule regarding weekend classes. Karachi campus offers a weekend program. Other campuses may schedule selected subjects on Saturdays with student and faculty consent. Regular classes are held on weekdays, while makeup classes may occasionally be scheduled on weekends.

title: Except for Faisalabad campus, all other campuses may schedule classes in the evening
applicable_campus: Except for Faisalabad campus
content: Except for Faisalabad campus, all other campuses may schedule classes in the evening. Some Master's degree programs are conducted in the evening, with details available on the website.

title: Students receive their class timetable on orientation day
content: Students receive their class timetable on orientation day. Students with timetable concerns should discuss them with the academic office.

title: No seats are reserved specifically for NTS test takers
content: No seats are reserved specifically for NTS test takers. Cut-off scores for NTS are determined when the merit list is announced.

title: Students who failed a subject in HSSC or A-Levels and reappeared with Part Two exams can apply and attempt the admission
content: Students who failed a subject in HSSC or A-Levels and reappeared with Part Two exams can apply and attempt the admission test. If selected, they receive provisional admission contingent upon submitting all results. Admission is cancelled if they fail a subject required to meet eligibility criteria.

title: Students whose HSSC Part Two result is pending can apply based on Part One exam results
content: Students whose HSSC Part Two result is pending can apply based on Part One exam results. If they meet merit criteria, they receive provisional admission. Upon submitting Part Two results, provisional admission converts to confirmed admission if eligibility and selection criteria are met. Otherwise, admission is closed.

title: Merit is calculated based on entry test marks and past academic record
content: Merit is calculated based on entry test marks and past academic record. Detailed information is available in the eligibility and selection criteria documentation.

title: Merit for SAT students is calculated using the same method as for the university test
content: Merit for SAT students is calculated using the same method as for the university test. No minimum SAT score is required for SAT-based applicants.

title: A credit hour represents the number of teaching hours per week during the semester
content: A credit hour represents the number of teaching hours per week during the semester. For theory courses, one credit hour equals one hour of teaching activities. For lab courses, one credit hour equals three hours of teaching activities. For example, a three plus one course includes three hours of classes and three hours of labs per week. In bachelor of science programs, three hours of classes typically spread across two ninety-minute sessions.

title: The admission process follows a structured timeline published on the university website
content: The admission process follows a structured timeline published on the university website. Admission application submission typically opens in mid-May and continues until early July. Admission tests are conducted over approximately two weeks following the application deadline.

title: The merit list announcing selected candidates and test-qualified applicants is released approximately one week after com
content: The merit list announcing selected candidates and test-qualified applicants is released approximately one week after completion of admission tests. For graduate programs, interviews are scheduled shortly after the merit list announcement. Post-graduate program admission tests are conducted following the interview phase.

title: Admission formalities for selected candidates typically span a period of approximately three weeks following the merit l
content: Admission formalities for selected candidates typically span a period of approximately three weeks following the merit list announcement. This period allows students to complete registration, fee payment, and document submission. Classes for the new academic session generally commence in mid-August.

title: Current admission schedules with specific dates are published on the official announcement page of the university websit
content: Current admission schedules with specific dates are published on the official announcement page of the university website. Updates and modifications are posted on the university's social media pages.

title: Students enrolled in any semester at one campus can take summer courses at another campus for subjects they failed in th
applicable_campus: Students enrolled in any semester at one campus
content: Students enrolled in any semester at one campus can take summer courses at another campus for subjects they failed in the spring semester. They can then return to their original campus for the fall semester. Students should consult their department for lists of courses offered at other campuses or contact the summer campus department directly.

title: Foreign students requiring a visa for the admission test should apply to FAST as early as possible
applicable_campus: They should immediately inform campus
content: Foreign students requiring a visa for the admission test should apply to FAST as early as possible. They should immediately inform campus administration about their travel needs to receive a letter of support for the Pakistan embassy or consulate. If the visa is received after the test date, a substitute test can be arranged as a special case.

title: Students wishing to transfer their PhD enrollment from another university to FAST should apply as regular students
applicable_campus: They should inform the campus
content: Students wishing to transfer their PhD enrollment from another university to FAST should apply as regular students. They should inform the campus about their intent for credit transfer. The campus graduate studies committee evaluates each case. As per Higher Education Commission rules, a maximum of fifty percent of credits are transferable. Students join FAST in the coursework phase, and research credits are not considered for transfer.

title: Students eligible for admission to multiple campuses should consider location, facilities, faculty, and program reputati
applicable_campus: Students eligible for admission to multiple campuses
content: Students eligible for admission to multiple campuses should consider location, facilities, faculty, and program reputation when deciding. Visiting campuses and speaking with current students or alumni can provide valuable insights into each environment.

title: National and international rankings provide information about the standing of degree programs offered at FAST
applicable_program: l rankings provide information about the standing of degree programs
content: National and international rankings provide information about the standing of degree programs offered at FAST. The entry test assesses readiness for the program. Students should prepare by reviewing relevant material and practicing similar questions.

title: Different campuses offer different degree programs
applicable_campus: Different campuses
applicable_program: Different campuses offer different degree programs
content: Different campuses offer different degree programs. The university website provides comprehensive information about available programs at each campus.

title: NTS GAT is accepted for admission in MS Data Science programs
applicable_program: NTS GAT is accepted for admission in MS Data Science programs
content: NTS GAT is accepted for admission in MS Data Science programs.

title: Pre-medical students who have passed additional mathematics or are awaiting results are eligible to apply for all comput
applicable_program: re awaiting results are eligible to apply for all computing programs
content: Pre-medical students who have passed additional mathematics or are awaiting results are eligible to apply for all computing programs. If a student fails to submit the additional mathematics result after admission, their admission is cancelled.

title: Study hour refers to time spent in structured learning activities such as lectures, seminars, or lab sessions
content: Study hour refers to time spent in structured learning activities such as lectures, seminars, or lab sessions. One credit hour means teaching a theory course for one classroom hour each week throughout the semester.

title: O Level marks are not multiplied by any factor
content: O Level marks are not multiplied by any factor. Merit is calculated based on entry test results and past academic record, which includes SSC and HSSC marks.

title: Electrical engineers can apply for computing programs
applicable_program: Electrical engineers can apply for computing programs
content: Electrical engineers can apply for computing programs. If admitted after clearing the admission test, they must complete at least four deficiency courses alongside regular program courses.

title: ECAT test results are accepted for admission to engineering programs
applicable_program: ECAT test results are accepted for admission to engineering programs
content: ECAT test results are accepted for admission to engineering programs. Results must be submitted before the admission cycle closes.

title: Merit cut-off marks are revised periodically for each degree and campus
applicable_campus: Merit cut-off marks are revised periodically for each degree and campus
content: Merit cut-off marks are revised periodically for each degree and campus.

title: The entry test fee is three thousand rupees for all applicants, including those seeking admission through NTS tests
content: The entry test fee is three thousand rupees for all applicants, including those seeking admission through NTS tests. Applicants taking more than one admission test pay an additional five hundred rupees for each additional test. Payment of test fees is mandatory.

title: Students applying with NTS NAT results must ensure their results are available before the submission deadline
content: Students applying with NTS NAT results must ensure their results are available before the submission deadline. No minimum marks guarantee admission, as NTS cut-offs are determined by the university. Selection criteria details are available on the university website.

title: All NAT tests are acceptable for computing and management sciences programs
applicable_program: tests are acceptable for computing and management sciences programs
content: All NAT tests are acceptable for computing and management sciences programs. For engineering programs, only NAT-IE is acceptable.

title: Students needing to correct academic records should email the admissions office before the deadline
content: Students needing to correct academic records should email the admissions office before the deadline. Scholarship information is available on the university website.

title: Students who completed O Levels and A Levels must submit equivalencies from the Inter Board Committee of Chairmen
content: Students who completed O Levels and A Levels must submit equivalencies from the Inter Board Committee of Chairmen. A Levels equivalency can be submitted one week after result declaration.

title: Students can take the entry test at their nearest campus regardless of which campus they plan to attend
applicable_campus: Students can take the entry test at their nearest campus
content: Students can take the entry test at their nearest campus regardless of which campus they plan to attend. Students unable to take the test on the scheduled date due to urgent reasons must submit proof and an authentic explanation. Management may reschedule the test if the deadline has not passed.

title: Students should contact the relevant campus for information about Master's class timings
applicable_campus: Students should contact the relevant campus
content: Students should contact the relevant campus for information about Master's class timings.

title: Students should contact the relevant campus for information about hostel charges, transport charges, and transport route
applicable_campus: Students should contact the relevant campus
content: Students should contact the relevant campus for information about hostel charges, transport charges, and transport routes.

title: FAST holds accreditation from relevant regulatory bodies and maintains partnerships with industry organizations
content: FAST holds accreditation from relevant regulatory bodies and maintains partnerships with industry organizations.

title: Computer science graduates have diverse career opportunities including software developer, data scientist, cybersecurity
content: Computer science graduates have diverse career opportunities including software developer, data scientist, cybersecurity analyst, web developer, mobile app developer, systems analyst, network engineer, database administrator, machine learning engineer, and IT project manager.

title: Computer science career paths are available across technology, finance, healthcare, and entertainment industries
content: Computer science career paths are available across technology, finance, healthcare, and entertainment industries.

title: The university website provides details about courses offered by the school of management
applicable_campus: Not all degrees are offered at all campuses
content: The university website provides details about courses offered by the school of management. Not all degrees are offered at all campuses. Students should contact the computing academic office for information about course electives in computing programs.

title: Students can apply to more than one campus by paying five hundred rupees for each additional campus along with the stand
applicable_campus: Students can apply to more than one campus
content: Students can apply to more than one campus by paying five hundred rupees for each additional campus along with the standard application fee.

title: Students who apply for one program but are not selected may qualify for admission to other programs if they meet the eli
applicable_program: Students who apply for one program
content: Students who apply for one program but are not selected may qualify for admission to other programs if they meet the eligibility criteria and merit score of the targeted program.

title: All engineering programs are accredited with the Pakistan Engineering Council under Level Two of the Washington Accord
content: All engineering programs are accredited with the Pakistan Engineering Council under Level Two of the Washington Accord.

title: Students can choose a specialization stream at the start of the fifth semester of the bachelor of science in electrical
content: Students can choose a specialization stream at the start of the fifth semester of the bachelor of science in electrical engineering. Available specializations include computer engineering, electronics engineering, and power engineering.

title: The MBA program is designed for students from both business and non-business backgrounds
content: The MBA program is designed for students from both business and non-business backgrounds. It is a two-year, sixty credit hour program. Students with business-related degrees such as BBA, bachelor of science in accounting and finance, or master's in commerce may receive up to thirty credit hours of exemption as permitted by the Higher Education Commission.

title: Information about specialized research areas in graduate programs, research labs, and experimental labs in the electrica
applicable_campus: n the electrical engineering department is available by contacting the concerned campus
applicable_program: Information about specialized research areas in graduate programs
content: Information about specialized research areas in graduate programs, research labs, and experimental labs in the electrical engineering department is available by contacting the concerned campus.

title: Research assistantships and graduate fellowships are available for PhD programs
applicable_program: sistantships and graduate fellowships are available for PhD programs
content: Research assistantships and graduate fellowships are available for PhD programs. Six months of international research support exchange is available under Higher Education Commission funding.

title: The university loan is completely interest-free
content: The university loan is completely interest-free. Upon recommendation by the financial aid committee, deserving students may receive approval for installment payments for hostel and transport fees.

title: Admission tests include negative marking
content: Admission tests include negative marking. The use of calculators is not allowed.

title: For BBA and BS programs including accounting and finance, business analytics, and financial technology, the test compris
content: For BBA and BS programs including accounting and finance, business analytics, and financial technology, the test comprises four sections. The English section has a weightage of ten percent. The Essay Writing section has a weightage of fifteen percent. The Analytical Skills and IQ section has a weightage of twenty-five percent. The Basic Math section has a weightage of fifty percent.

title: For BS programs in Computer Science and Engineering, the test comprises four sections
content: For BS programs in Computer Science and Engineering, the test comprises four sections. The English section has a weightage of ten percent. The Analytical Skills and IQ section has a weightage of twenty percent. The Basic Math section has a weightage of twenty percent. The Advanced Math section has a weightage of fifty percent.

title: The MBA admission test includes specific sections tailored for business graduates
content: The MBA admission test includes specific sections tailored for business graduates. The English section accounts for ten percent. Essay Writing accounts for thirty percent. Analytical Skills and IQ account for twenty percent. Quantitative Methods account for twenty percent. Business-related Knowledge accounts for twenty percent.

title: For the MS in Applied Linguistics program, the test structure emphasizes language skills
applicable_program: For the MS in Applied Linguistics program
content: For the MS in Applied Linguistics program, the test structure emphasizes language skills. The English section carries a fifteen percent weightage. Essay Writing carries a twenty-five percent weightage. Analytical Skills and IQ carry a fifteen percent weightage. The Specialization Area section carries a significant forty-five percent weightage.

title: For MS in Computing programs, the test focuses on technical and analytical abilities
content: For MS in Computing programs, the test focuses on technical and analytical abilities. Essay Writing has a ten percent weightage. Analytical Skills and IQ have a thirty percent weightage. Quantitative Methods have a thirty percent weightage. The Specialization Area section has a thirty percent weightage.

title: For other MS programs not specified above, the test follows a general pattern
content: For other MS programs not specified above, the test follows a general pattern. The English section accounts for five percent. Essay Writing accounts for ten percent. Analytical Skills and IQ account for twenty percent. Advanced Math accounts for ten percent. Quantitative Methods account for fifteen percent. The Specialization Area section accounts for forty percent.

title: Students who have taken the HSSC or an equivalent examination and are awaiting results are eligible to apply for undergr
applicable_program: re awaiting results are eligible to apply for undergraduate programs
content: Students who have taken the HSSC or an equivalent examination and are awaiting results are eligible to apply for undergraduate programs. The four-year undergraduate programs of full-time study are divided into eight semesters.

title: The university offers four bachelor's degree programs in business administration: bachelor of business administration, b
content: The university offers four bachelor's degree programs in business administration: bachelor of business administration, bachelor of science in accounting and finance, bachelor of science in business analytics, and bachelor of science in financial technology.

title: Four admission test options are available for business administration programs
applicable_program: sion test options are available for business administration programs
content: Four admission test options are available for business administration programs. Applicants may take the FAST admission test, the SAT, or the NTS test. Applicants must select one admission test from the available options. For NTS, NAT-IE, NAT-ICS, NAT-ICOM, and NAT-IGS are acceptable. NTS tests from specific universities are not acceptable for business administration program admissions.

title: Applicants must have a minimum of sixty percent marks in SSC or matriculation examination
content: Applicants must have a minimum of sixty percent marks in SSC or matriculation examination. Applicants must have a minimum of fifty percent marks in HSSC or intermediate examination.

title: There are no specific course requirements at HSSC or equivalent level for business administration programs
applicable_program: nts at HSSC or equivalent level for business administration programs
content: There are no specific course requirements at HSSC or equivalent level for business administration programs. Students from all academic backgrounds are eligible to apply.

title: The selection criteria for business administration programs uses a weighted merit calculation
applicable_program: The selection criteria for business administration programs
content: The selection criteria for business administration programs uses a weighted merit calculation. Admission test marks carry a weightage of fifty percent. HSSC or equivalent examination marks carry a weightage of forty percent. SSC or equivalent examination marks carry a weightage of ten percent.

title: The weightage of HSSC marks is calculated based on available results at the time of merit list compilation
content: The weightage of HSSC marks is calculated based on available results at the time of merit list compilation. If both HSSC Part One and Part Two results are available, marks from both parts are used. If HSSC Part Two result is not available, only marks from HSSC Part One are used. For students with A-Level qualifications, the marks or percentage given in the IBCC equivalence certificate of A-Level are used. For students whose only qualification is O-Level, the marks or percentage given in the IBCC equivalence certificate of O-Level are used.

Applicants must select one admission test from the available options. NTS tests from specific universities are not acceptable for engineering program admissions.

title: Applicants must have a minimum of sixty percent marks in both the SSC (matriculation) and HSSC (intermediate) examinatio
content: Applicants must have a minimum of sixty percent marks in both the SSC (matriculation) and HSSC (intermediate) examinations.

title: Applicants must have studied specific courses at HSSC or equivalent level
content: Applicants must have studied specific courses at HSSC or equivalent level. Acceptable combinations include pre-engineering with chemistry, mathematics, and physics. Alternatively, students may have studied ICS with computer science, mathematics, and physics.

title: The selection criteria for engineering programs uses a weighted merit calculation
applicable_program: The selection criteria for engineering programs
content: The selection criteria for engineering programs uses a weighted merit calculation. Admission test marks carry a weightage of thirty-three percent. HSSC or equivalent examination marks carry a weightage of fifty percent. SSC or equivalent examination marks carry a weightage of seventeen percent.

title: The weightage of HSSC marks is calculated based on available results at the time of merit list compilation
content: The weightage of HSSC marks is calculated based on available results at the time of merit list compilation. If both HSSC Part One and Part Two results are available, marks from both parts are used. If HSSC Part Two result is not available, only marks from HSSC Part One are used. For students with A-Level qualifications, the marks or percentage given in the IBCC equivalence certificate of A-Level are used. For students whose only qualification is O-Level, the marks or percentage given in the IBCC equivalence certificate of O-Level are used.

title: Merit cut-off marks are determined by the university based on the overall applicant pool and program capacity
applicable_program: d by the university based on the overall applicant pool and program
content: Merit cut-off marks are determined by the university based on the overall applicant pool and program capacity.

title: All documents and transcripts are verified at the time of admission
content: All documents and transcripts are verified at the time of admission. Any incorrect or false information submitted by applicants or any attempt to hide information will lead to disqualification of the candidate for admission.

title: FAST-NUCES uses a 4
applicable_campus: The grading policy applies uniformly to all programs across all campuses
content: FAST-NUCES uses a 4.0 grade point scale for all undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral programs. Letter grades are assigned to each course at the end of the semester, and each letter grade corresponds to a specific grade point value on this scale. The grading policy applies uniformly to all programs across all campuses of the university.

title: The university computes two types of grade averages for each student: the Semester Grade Point Average and the Cumulativ
content: The university computes two types of grade averages for each student: the Semester Grade Point Average and the Cumulative Grade Point Average. The Semester Grade Point Average, referred to as SGPA, is the weighted average of grade points earned in a single semester. The Cumulative Grade Point Average, referred to as CGPA, is the weighted average of all courses a student has completed across all semesters.

title: In calculating the CGPA at FAST-NUCES, the weights assigned to each course are based on the credit hours of that course
content: In calculating the CGPA at FAST-NUCES, the weights assigned to each course are based on the credit hours of that course relative to the total credit hours attempted. If a student repeats a course, only the most recent grade is used in CGPA calculation, even if the earlier attempt resulted in a higher grade. The earlier grade is dropped from the CGPA computation but remains visible on the student's transcript.

title: Special grade codes used at FAST-NUCES include W for Withdrawn, I for Incomplete, CR for Credit, S for Satisfactory, and
content: Special grade codes used at FAST-NUCES include W for Withdrawn, I for Incomplete, CR for Credit, S for Satisfactory, and U for Unsatisfactory. Grades of W, I, and CR are not included in the calculation of SGPA or CGPA. The S and U grades are used exclusively for PhD thesis courses and are similarly excluded from CGPA calculations.

title: An Incomplete grade, denoted as I, is issued when a student is unable to complete the final examination due to a genuine
content: An Incomplete grade, denoted as I, is issued when a student is unable to complete the final examination due to a genuine reason while having satisfactorily met all other course requirements. If the Incomplete grade is not resolved within the time set by the competent authority, it is automatically converted to an F grade and is then included in the CGPA.

title: A W or Withdrawn grade is assigned when a student officially withdraws from a course after the withdrawal deadline
content: A W or Withdrawn grade is assigned when a student officially withdraws from a course after the withdrawal deadline. The W grade appears on the transcript but does not affect the CGPA. Fees are not refunded for a withdrawn course, and the withdrawal must be approved by the Head of Department. Students cannot drop courses during the summer semester.

title: The minimum CGPA required to avoid academic warning differs by program level at FAST-NUCES
applicable_program: minimum CGPA required to avoid academic warning differs by program
content: The minimum CGPA required to avoid academic warning differs by program level at FAST-NUCES. Undergraduate students must maintain a CGPA of at least 2.0 out of 4.0. MS students must maintain a minimum CGPA of 2.5. PhD students must maintain a minimum CGPA of 3.0. Falling below these thresholds at the end of any semester results in the issuance of an academic warning.

title: At the end of every semester, including the first semester and summer semesters, a warning is issued to any student whos
applicable_program: hose CGPA falls below the minimum required for their degree program
content: At the end of every semester, including the first semester and summer semesters, a warning is issued to any student whose CGPA falls below the minimum required for their degree program. A student who has received a warning cannot register for the following semester without the approval of the relevant Head of Department or a senior faculty member nominated by the Head of Department.

title: Each time a student's CGPA falls below the required minimum at the end of a semester, the warning count increases by one
content: Each time a student's CGPA falls below the required minimum at the end of a semester, the warning count increases by one. If the warning count reaches three, the student's admission at FAST-NUCES is closed. If the CGPA meets or exceeds the required minimum after any semester, the warning count is reset to zero. The admission of any student is also automatically cancelled upon exceeding the maximum allowed duration for completing the degree.

title: Students who are under an academic warning at FAST-NUCES are required to repeat any previously passed courses in which t
applicable_program: r grade point is below the minimum CGPA threshold for their program
content: Students who are under an academic warning at FAST-NUCES are required to repeat any previously passed courses in which their grade point is below the minimum CGPA threshold for their program. For example, an MS student on warning with a CGPA below 2.5 is required to repeat any course in which a grade below C was received. This requirement applies in addition to any failed courses.

title: The normal course load for undergraduate students at FAST-NUCES is five courses per semester
applicable_program: ents may have a slightly different course load depending on program
content: The normal course load for undergraduate students at FAST-NUCES is five courses per semester. Final-year students may have a slightly different course load depending on program requirements. A student who is under disciplinary action, has outstanding dues, has a pending disciplinary case, or does not meet CGPA requirements cannot register for the next semester without satisfying the relevant conditions first.

title: FAST-NUCES has a clear policy on the use of generative artificial intelligence in academic work
content: FAST-NUCES has a clear policy on the use of generative artificial intelligence in academic work. The university allows certain responsible and educationally purposeful uses of generative AI tools, such as using them to understand course content or summarize material for learning purposes. However, submitting AI-generated content as one's own original academic work is considered a violation of academic integrity and is not permitted.

title: The university's academic integrity standards prohibit all forms of dishonest academic conduct
content: The university's academic integrity standards prohibit all forms of dishonest academic conduct. This includes copying from other students during examinations, plagiarism in assignments and reports, submitting work done by others, and bribery or paid services for academic benefit. Sharing assignment answers or test solutions with others is also considered a violation of the university's academic code.

title: The total number of credit hours required to complete a four-year undergraduate degree at FAST-NUCES is approximately 13
content: The total number of credit hours required to complete a four-year undergraduate degree at FAST-NUCES is approximately 130 credit hours across eight semesters. Each semester typically contains five courses, each carrying three credit hours. Laboratory and project courses may carry different credit hour weights. Final Year Project courses also contribute credit hours that count toward the degree requirement.

title: The Final Year Project, commonly known as FYP, is a mandatory component of all undergraduate programs at FAST-NUCES
applicable_program: is a mandatory component of all undergraduate programs
content: The Final Year Project, commonly known as FYP, is a mandatory component of all undergraduate programs at FAST-NUCES. It is a two-semester project completed in the seventh and eighth semesters of the program. The FYP is divided into FYP-I in the first semester and FYP-II in the second semester. Credits earned in the FYP are not transferable to other programs.

title: FYP groups at FAST-NUCES must consist of either two or three members
content: FYP groups at FAST-NUCES must consist of either two or three members. A group size of three is preferred. Cross-departmental groups are not permitted. Each group must have an internal supervisor who is a faculty member of the relevant department. If a group undertakes a project with an external supervisor from industry, an internal faculty supervisor is still mandatory.

title: To register for the Final Year Project at FAST-NUCES, a student must have earned at least 100 credit hours without any c
applicable_campus: Students with two course deficiencies may register with approval from the Campus
content: To register for the Final Year Project at FAST-NUCES, a student must have earned at least 100 credit hours without any course deficiency. Students with one deficiency course may register with approval from the Head of Department, provided they have at least 96 earned credit hours. Students with two course deficiencies may register with approval from the Campus Director, provided they have earned at least 94 credit hours.

title: Final Year Project groups that require special hardware or software subscriptions for their project must submit a Hardwa
content: Final Year Project groups that require special hardware or software subscriptions for their project must submit a Hardware and Software Requisition Form to the academic office within two weeks of the FYP registration deadline. The FYP registration form must be signed by the internal supervisor. Evaluations in both FYP-I and FYP-II are conducted on an individual student basis, even though the project is carried out as a group.

title: FAST-NUCES does not offer undergraduate admissions in the Spring semester
applicable_campus: This rule applies to all campuses
applicable_program: All students who wish to enroll in an undergraduate program
content: FAST-NUCES does not offer undergraduate admissions in the Spring semester. All students who wish to enroll in an undergraduate program must apply during the Fall intake cycle. This rule applies to all campuses and all undergraduate degree programs offered by the university.

title: The maximum duration allowed to complete an undergraduate degree at FAST-NUCES is set by the university and typically ex
content: The maximum duration allowed to complete an undergraduate degree at FAST-NUCES is set by the university and typically extends to a specified number of years beyond the standard four-year program duration. A student who does not complete degree requirements within the maximum allowed time will have their admission automatically cancelled. Specific maximum durations are outlined in the academic rules and regulations of the university.

title: Top-performing students at FAST-NUCES who demonstrate consistent academic excellence are recognized through prize awards
applicable_program: This recognition is separate from scholarship programs
content: Top-performing students at FAST-NUCES who demonstrate consistent academic excellence are recognized through prize awards. The top three position holders in each semester are awarded prize money and a certificate of achievement. This recognition is separate from scholarship programs and is based on semester-level academic performance.

title: Students at FAST-NUCES are required to clear all outstanding fees and dues before they can proceed with course registrat
content: Students at FAST-NUCES are required to clear all outstanding fees and dues before they can proceed with course registration for any semester. Registration is conducted online through the university's Flex portal. Students who fail to pay their fees by the due date or who have unresolved disciplinary matters may be denied registration until all conditions are resolved.

title: The university's loan repayment policy for need-based financial assistance requires that repayment begins three months a
content: The university's loan repayment policy for need-based financial assistance requires that repayment begins three months after graduation or after the student secures employment, whichever comes first. The total loan amount must be repaid within four years of graduation. Students are required to formally acknowledge this repayment obligation at the time the loan is granted.

title: Financial assistance provided by FAST-NUCES in the form of need-based loans is limited strictly to tuition fees
content: Financial assistance provided by FAST-NUCES in the form of need-based loans is limited strictly to tuition fees. It does not cover the admission fee, security deposit, or student activity fund. Assistance is discontinued if the student's CGPA falls below 2.0 for undergraduate programs or below 2.5 for graduate programs. Assistance may be reinstated after the student exits academic warning status.

title: FAST-NUCES offers a Student Exchange Program that allows enrolled students to study at partner universities abroad for o
applicable_program: FAST-NUCES offers a Student Exchange Program
content: FAST-NUCES offers a Student Exchange Program that allows enrolled students to study at partner universities abroad for one semester or one academic year. This program provides students with global academic exposure and the opportunity to earn credits that may be recognized toward their degree at FAST-NUCES. The university maintains partnerships with institutions internationally for this purpose.

title: Research assistantship and graduate fellowship positions are available for PhD students at FAST-NUCES
content: Research assistantship and graduate fellowship positions are available for PhD students at FAST-NUCES. These positions provide financial support to doctoral students in exchange for their assistance in research activities or teaching support. Students in engineering and computing programs may also be eligible for six-month international research exchange funding under HEC programs.

title: The MBA program at FAST-NUCES is a two-year program totaling 60 credit hours
applicable_program: The MBA program at FAST-NUCES is a two-year program
content: The MBA program at FAST-NUCES is a two-year program totaling 60 credit hours. Students who hold a BBA, BS Accounting and Finance, or a Master's in Commerce may be eligible for up to 30 credit hours of course exemption under HEC guidelines, effectively reducing their required coursework. The MBA program is designed to accommodate students from both business and non-business undergraduate backgrounds.

title: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences, commonly known as FAST-NUCES or FAST-NU, is a private federa
applicable_campus: The university was the first multi-campus
content: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences, commonly known as FAST-NUCES or FAST-NU, is a private federally chartered research university in Pakistan. It was inaugurated in July 2000 by the Federal Charter granted under Ordinance No. XXIII of 2000. The university was the first multi-campus private university established in Pakistan.

title: FAST-NU operates six campuses across Pakistan, located in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Chiniot-Faisalabad, and
applicable_campus: FAST-NU operates six campuses
applicable_program: admission policies and academic regulations while offering programs
content: FAST-NU operates six campuses across Pakistan, located in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Chiniot-Faisalabad, and Multan. The main headquarters of the university is located in Islamabad. Each campus follows the same admission policies and academic regulations while offering programs based on regional demand and available facilities.

title: The university offers undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral programs in the fields of computer science, software enginee
content: The university offers undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral programs in the fields of computer science, software engineering, electrical engineering, civil engineering, business administration, accounting and finance, artificial intelligence, and related disciplines. FAST-NU is consistently ranked among the top institutions for computing and information technology in Pakistan by the Higher Education Commission.

title: Admissions at FAST-NU are conducted twice a year, in Fall and Spring semesters
applicable_program: The Fall semester is the primary intake for undergraduate programs
content: Admissions at FAST-NU are conducted twice a year, in Fall and Spring semesters. The Fall semester is the primary intake for undergraduate programs. Applications must be submitted online through the official admissions portal at admissions.nu.edu.pk. Applications received by post or submitted by hand are not accepted.

title: The online application system assigns each applicant a unique Application Reference Number, commonly referred to as the
content: The online application system assigns each applicant a unique Application Reference Number, commonly referred to as the ARN. Applicants must note this number for use in all future correspondence and status checks related to their admission.

title: To apply for undergraduate programs at FAST-NU, applicants must fill out the online admission form, pay the application
applicable_campus: The applicant must then appear at the designated campus
content: To apply for undergraduate programs at FAST-NU, applicants must fill out the online admission form, pay the application processing fee, and print the admit card bearing the test roll number. The applicant must then appear at the designated campus on the specified date and time stated on the admit card. Latecomers are not permitted to sit for the test.

title: The application processing fee for undergraduate programs is required from all applicants, including those applying on t
applicable_program: The application processing fee for undergraduate programs
content: The application processing fee for undergraduate programs is required from all applicants, including those applying on the basis of NTS scores. This fee is non-refundable and non-adjustable under any circumstances. Applicants must deposit this fee in the designated bank before printing the admit card.

title: Applicants are required to bring only their endorsed admit card to the test centre
content: Applicants are required to bring only their endorsed admit card to the test centre. No other documents, mobile phones, calculators, smartwatches, or electronic devices are allowed inside the examination hall. Any applicant without a valid endorsed admit card will not be allowed to take the test.

title: The FAST-NU Admission Test is a computer-based test conducted at all campuses of the university, including Islamabad, La
applicable_campus: The FAST-NU Admission Test is a computer-based test conducted at all campuses
content: The FAST-NU Admission Test is a computer-based test conducted at all campuses of the university, including Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, and Peshawar. The test is typically two hours in duration and consists entirely of multiple-choice questions. Each correct answer awards one mark, and each incorrect answer deducts 0.25 marks. Calculators are not permitted during the test.

title: The FAST Admission Test for undergraduate computing and engineering programs covers four main sections: Advanced Mathema
applicable_program: Admission Test for undergraduate computing and engineering programs
content: The FAST Admission Test for undergraduate computing and engineering programs covers four main sections: Advanced Mathematics, Basic Mathematics, Analytical and IQ reasoning, and English language. The Advanced Mathematics section includes topics from FSc Part 1 and Part 2, such as matrices, algebra, trigonometry, permutation and combination, probability, analytic geometry, and calculus.

title: Applicants who have taken the SAT examination may apply for undergraduate programs, specifically BBA or BS degrees, with
applicable_program: have taken the SAT examination may apply for undergraduate programs
content: Applicants who have taken the SAT examination may apply for undergraduate programs, specifically BBA or BS degrees, without appearing in the FAST Admission Test. The minimum SAT score required for BBA, BS Accounting and Finance, BS Business Administration, and BS FinTech programs is 1000. For all other computing and engineering programs, the minimum required SAT score is 1200.

title: Applicants who reside abroad and are unable to appear for the FAST university admission test may apply on the basis of S
content: Applicants who reside abroad and are unable to appear for the FAST university admission test may apply on the basis of SAT scores. This option is available for international or overseas Pakistani applicants. Such applicants must submit their SAT results through the official admissions portal.

title: Applicants who have taken the NTS NAT examination may also apply for undergraduate admission at FAST-NU
applicable_campus: Cut-off marks for NTS NAT are determined by FAST-NU and vary by campus
applicable_program: or NTS NAT are determined by FAST-NU and vary by campus and program
content: Applicants who have taken the NTS NAT examination may also apply for undergraduate admission at FAST-NU. The university accepts NTS NAT scores as an alternative to the FAST entry test. Cut-off marks for NTS NAT are determined by FAST-NU and vary by campus and program. For competitive programs, closing merits on NTS NAT can reach between 80 and 85 percentile.

title: The merit formula for undergraduate BS and BBA programs at FAST-NU assigns 50 percent weightage to the HSSC or equivalen
applicable_program: The merit formula for undergraduate BS and BBA programs
content: The merit formula for undergraduate BS and BBA programs at FAST-NU assigns 50 percent weightage to the HSSC or equivalent marks and 50 percent weightage to the marks obtained in the FAST Admission Test. There is no interview component in the undergraduate admissions process. Final selection is based entirely on the calculated merit score.

title: In cases where the Intermediate result is not yet available at the time of application, Matriculation marks are used as
content: In cases where the Intermediate result is not yet available at the time of application, Matriculation marks are used as a substitute and multiplied by a factor of 0.9 to equate them to average Intermediate marks. Admission offered on the basis of pending results is automatically cancelled if the candidate fails to pass the required examination once results are announced.

title: For admission to Engineering programs, candidates must have at least 60 percent marks in SSC or Matric and at least 60 p
content: For admission to Engineering programs, candidates must have at least 60 percent marks in SSC or Matric and at least 60 percent marks in FSc Pre-Engineering or an equivalent examination. For Computing programs such as BS Computer Science and BS Software Engineering, candidates must have at least 60 percent in SSC and at least 50 percent in FSc Pre-Engineering, ICS, or equivalent. For BBA, Accounting and Finance, and similar business programs, candidates must have at least 60 percent in SSC and at least 50 percent in FSc or ICS or equivalent.

title: Candidates who have appeared in the HSSC examination and are awaiting their results are eligible to apply for undergradu
content: Candidates who have appeared in the HSSC examination and are awaiting their results are eligible to apply for undergraduate admission at FAST-NU. They may provisionally sit the admission test and apply during the active admissions cycle. However, their admission will be subject to verification of final results once announced.

title: FAST-NU uses a competitive and merit-based selection process for all undergraduate admissions
content: FAST-NU uses a competitive and merit-based selection process for all undergraduate admissions. The university has an acceptance rate of approximately 13 percent, making it one of the more selective private universities in Pakistan. A score of approximately 70 percent in the FAST admission test is generally considered a safe threshold, with closing merits across programs ranging from 55 to 70 percent.

title: The selected candidates are informed of their admission status via SMS on their registered mobile numbers and via email
content: The selected candidates are informed of their admission status via SMS on their registered mobile numbers and via email. Candidates can also check the status of their admission online through the official university website at nu.edu.pk or the admissions portal at admissions.nu.edu.pk.

title: Foreign nationals applying for admission at FAST-NU must submit a No Objection Certificate from the Ministry of Educatio
applicable_program: ies to all non-Pakistani citizens seeking enrollment in any program
content: Foreign nationals applying for admission at FAST-NU must submit a No Objection Certificate from the Ministry of Education, Government of Pakistan, at the time of admission. This requirement applies to all non-Pakistani citizens seeking enrollment in any program at the university.

title: All information provided in the admission application form must be accurate
content: All information provided in the admission application form must be accurate. The university reserves the right to cancel the application or admission of any candidate who submits incorrect, false, or misleading information, without providing any fee refund. All documents and transcripts submitted are verified at the time of admission.

title: The admission test for undergraduate programs is held at all FAST campuses simultaneously during the announced testing w
applicable_campus: The admission test for undergraduate programs is held at all FAST campuses
applicable_program: The admission test for undergraduate programs
content: The admission test for undergraduate programs is held at all FAST campuses simultaneously during the announced testing window. For Fall 2025 admissions, the test was scheduled starting from July 20, 2025. Exact dates and test centre assignments are printed on the admit card issued after payment of the application fee.

title: FAST-NU results for the admission test are typically announced approximately one week after the completion of the examin
applicable_campus: g result announcement, the university publishes merit lists for each program and campus
applicable_program: the university publishes merit lists for each program
content: FAST-NU results for the admission test are typically announced approximately one week after the completion of the examination. Following result announcement, the university publishes merit lists for each program and campus. Candidates can view their position and admission status through the online portal.

title: For graduate programs at the MS level, FAST-NU requires candidates to hold a relevant bachelor's degree from a recognize
content: For graduate programs at the MS level, FAST-NU requires candidates to hold a relevant bachelor's degree from a recognized university with a minimum CGPA of 2.00 out of 4.00. Candidates must also have valid GRE subject test scores or NTS GAT General scores as per HEC guidelines. Specific programs may have additional prerequisite course requirements.

title: Candidates for MS Computer Science programs who lack certain prerequisite courses may be required to complete up to four
applicable_program: Candidates for MS Computer Science programs
content: Candidates for MS Computer Science programs who lack certain prerequisite courses may be required to complete up to four deficiency courses. These courses must be completed and passed within the first two semesters of the MS program. The prerequisite subjects typically include computer programming, data structures, operating systems, database systems, algorithms, computer networks, automata theory, and software engineering.

title: For admission to the PhD program at FAST-NU, candidates must hold a Master's degree in a relevant subject from a recogni
applicable_program: For admission to the PhD program
content: For admission to the PhD program at FAST-NU, candidates must hold a Master's degree in a relevant subject from a recognized university, earned after a minimum of 18 years of education, with a CGPA of at least 3.0 out of 4.0 or equivalent. Candidates must also achieve a minimum of 60 percent marks and at least 50 percentile rank in the relevant NTS GAT Subject test. The GAT Subject score must be valid at the time of admission.

title: PhD admissions at FAST-NU are also assessed through a panel evaluation
content: PhD admissions at FAST-NU are also assessed through a panel evaluation. Candidates must obtain a minimum of 60 percent marks in the admission test, after which selection is based 60 percent on admission test performance and 40 percent on panel evaluation. Candidates are also required to submit a research proposal and appear before the Doctoral Admissions Committee for an interview.

title: As of March 1, 2025, a research publication requirement has been introduced for PhD degree completion at FAST-NU
applicable_campus: The requirement varies slightly by campus
content: As of March 1, 2025, a research publication requirement has been introduced for PhD degree completion at FAST-NU. Candidates must publish one research article in an HEC approved W category journal, or two research articles in HEC approved X category journals, as a mandatory requirement for the award of the PhD degree. The requirement varies slightly by campus and department.

title: The PhD dissertation at FAST-NU must contain original research work
content: The PhD dissertation at FAST-NU must contain original research work. Before submission to foreign evaluators, a plagiarism test is conducted on the dissertation. The dissertation is then evaluated by at least two PhD experts from academically or technologically advanced foreign countries. A local committee comprising two local experts and one internal evaluator subsequently reviews the dissertation and conducts an open defence session.

title: FAST-NU offers the PhD program in Management Sciences with specializations in Management, Marketing, Finance, Human Reso
applicable_campus: These programs are offered at the Islamabad and Lahore campuses
content: FAST-NU offers the PhD program in Management Sciences with specializations in Management, Marketing, Finance, Human Resource Management, and Technology Management. These programs are offered at the Islamabad and Lahore campuses. PhD admission in Management Sciences additionally requires a valid GMAT score or equivalent as per HEC rules.

title: Students whose CGPA is between 2
content: Students whose CGPA is between 2.90 and 3.00 out of 4.00 in the semester system, or between 58 and 60 percent in the annual system, may be provisionally admitted to the PhD program. Such students are required to complete a Zero Semester, which is a regular semester in which they study additional courses of 9 credit hours at level 7 and maintain a minimum GPA of 3.00. Transition to the PhD program is subject to approval by the Graduate Studies Committee.

title: The contact information for FAST-NU central admissions is as follows
content: The contact information for FAST-NU central admissions is as follows. The postal address is FAST House, Rohtas Road, G-9/4, Islamabad. The helpline number is 111-128-128. The official admissions website is admissions.nu.edu.pk and the general university website is nu.edu.pk.

title: Each applicant is responsible for verifying the accuracy of information on their admit card before the test date
content: Each applicant is responsible for verifying the accuracy of information on their admit card before the test date. In the event of discrepancies in personal details, applicants should contact the admissions office immediately. Admit cards with errors that are not reported before the test date may cause issues during the admission process.

title: FAST-NU does not conduct interviews for undergraduate program admissions
applicable_campus: This ensures a transparent and standardized selection process across all campuses
applicable_program: FAST-NU does not conduct interviews for undergraduate program
content: FAST-NU does not conduct interviews for undergraduate program admissions. Selection at the undergraduate level is entirely based on academic merit calculated from HSSC results and the FAST admission test score. This ensures a transparent and standardized selection process across all campuses.

title: Candidates applying to FAST-NU through the NTS NAT route must have valid and current NTS scores
content: Candidates applying to FAST-NU through the NTS NAT route must have valid and current NTS scores. The university determines its own cut-off marks for NTS NAT, which may differ from NTS general standards. Applicants who choose this route are placed on separate merit lists and compete within their respective test category.

title: FAST-NU publishes separate merit lists for each test type, including the FAST Entry Test, SAT, and NTS NAT
applicable_campus: termine the number of seats allocated to each test category for each program and campus
applicable_program: he number of seats allocated to each test category for each program
content: FAST-NU publishes separate merit lists for each test type, including the FAST Entry Test, SAT, and NTS NAT. Candidates selected from each merit list are informed separately. The university reserves the right to determine the number of seats allocated to each test category for each program and campus.

title: Candidates who are offered admission must confirm their seat by submitting the required fee and documents within the spe
content: Candidates who are offered admission must confirm their seat by submitting the required fee and documents within the specified deadline. Failure to confirm admission within the given timeframe results in automatic cancellation of the seat. No extension is typically granted for this deadline.

title: Once a candidate has confirmed a seat at FAST-NU, the university verifies all submitted academic documents and certifica
content: Once a candidate has confirmed a seat at FAST-NU, the university verifies all submitted academic documents and certificates. Any document found to be forged, altered, or misrepresented results in the immediate cancellation of admission without any fee refund. Legal action may also be taken by the university in such cases.

title: FAST-NU does not offer on-campus hostel facilities at all campuses
applicable_campus: FAST-NU does not offer on-campus
content: FAST-NU does not offer on-campus hostel facilities at all campuses. The Islamabad campus has limited on-campus accommodation, while campuses such as Lahore do not provide on-campus hostel facilities. Students from other cities typically arrange private accommodation in nearby areas or hostels.

title: The Peshawar campus of FAST-NU provides a hostel allocation process for students requiring accommodation
applicable_campus: The Peshawar campus
content: The Peshawar campus of FAST-NU provides a hostel allocation process for students requiring accommodation. Students must submit a completed Hostel Allocation Request Form along with a copy of their CNIC, a bank draft, and a passport-size photograph. Seat allocation is made on a first-come, first-served basis based on available quota for each degree and batch.

title: The university's admissions cycle for Fall semester typically begins in April or May with the announcement of test dates
content: The university's admissions cycle for Fall semester typically begins in April or May with the announcement of test dates, followed by the test conducted in July, and admissions confirmation in August. Spring admissions follow a separate and shorter schedule. Exact dates are announced on the official university website each year.

title: For the most current admission schedule, fee structure, eligibility criteria, and test dates, applicants are advised to
content: For the most current admission schedule, fee structure, eligibility criteria, and test dates, applicants are advised to consult the official FAST-NU admissions website at admissions.nu.edu.pk or contact the university helpline. Information on third-party websites may not always reflect the latest updates made by the university.

title: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences is a private research university established in Pakistan in t
applicable_campus: It operates under a Federal Charter and was the first multi-campus
content: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences is a private research university established in Pakistan in the year 2000. It operates under a Federal Charter and was the first multi-campus private university in the country. The university is sponsored by the Foundation for Advancement of Science and Technology, which was registered by the Government of Pakistan as a charitable institution in 1980.

title: FAST-NU was a pioneer in Pakistan's information technology sector and was the first institution to introduce an undergra
content: FAST-NU was a pioneer in Pakistan's information technology sector and was the first institution to introduce an undergraduate computer science curriculum in the country. Its headquarters are located in Islamabad, and the university has maintained a strong emphasis on research, computing, and emerging technologies since its founding.

title: The university currently operates campuses in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Chiniot-Faisalabad, and Multan
applicable_campus: The university currently operates campuses
content: The university currently operates campuses in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Chiniot-Faisalabad, and Multan. Each campus serves students from its surrounding region while maintaining the same academic standards, curriculum structure, and university regulations established at the central level. The total student enrollment across all campuses is approximately 11,000.

title: The Islamabad campus is the oldest and largest campus of FAST-NU
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
content: The Islamabad campus is the oldest and largest campus of FAST-NU. It is located in H-11/4, Islamabad, and offers a wide range of undergraduate, graduate, and PhD programs in computing, engineering, management sciences, and humanities. The campus is equipped with modern classrooms, computer laboratories, a library, sports facilities, and student activity spaces.

title: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NU is located near Nescom Hospital and is accessible from Srinagar Highway, the Rawalpindi-
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
content: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NU is located near Nescom Hospital and is accessible from Srinagar Highway, the Rawalpindi-Peshawar GT Road, and the Islamabad Metro Bus Service. The campus has hosted numerous convocations and academic events and continues to serve as the primary administrative and academic centre of the university.

title: The Lahore campus of FAST-NU is situated in Faisal Town, spread over approximately 12
applicable_campus: The Lahore campus
applicable_program: It is known for its strong programs
content: The Lahore campus of FAST-NU is situated in Faisal Town, spread over approximately 12.5 acres. It is known for its strong programs in software engineering, computer science, electrical engineering, civil engineering, and mechanical engineering. The campus has modern facilities and maintains active student societies and extracurricular programs.

title: The Karachi campus of FAST-NU is located in a major commercial and industrial city and offers specialized programs inclu
applicable_campus: The Karachi campus
applicable_program: major commercial and industrial city and offers specialized programs
content: The Karachi campus of FAST-NU is located in a major commercial and industrial city and offers specialized programs including Actuarial Science, Business Analytics, and MS Data Science. The campus has strong industry connections with the technology and engineering sectors of Karachi, and provides students with access to internship and placement opportunities at leading companies.

title: The Peshawar campus of FAST-NU offers programs in computing and engineering tailored to meet the educational demands of
applicable_campus: The Peshawar campus
applicable_program: The Peshawar campus of FAST-NU offers programs
content: The Peshawar campus of FAST-NU offers programs in computing and engineering tailored to meet the educational demands of the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa region. The campus provides a hostel facility for students, along with academic and extracurricular support. Students from Peshawar campus have priority in hostel seat allocation over students from other campuses.

title: The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus of FAST-NU serves the central Punjab region and offers programs primarily in computing and
applicable_campus: The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus
applicable_program: mpus of FAST-NU serves the central Punjab region and offers programs
content: The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus of FAST-NU serves the central Punjab region and offers programs primarily in computing and engineering. The campus benefits from its location in Pakistan's textile and industrial hub and maintains academic quality aligned with the main FAST standards. Students from this campus feed into both local industry and the broader national technology sector.

title: The Multan campus is the newest campus of FAST-NU, established to serve the southern Punjab region
applicable_campus: The Multan campus
content: The Multan campus is the newest campus of FAST-NU, established to serve the southern Punjab region. It is located in DHA Multan and is being developed with modern infrastructure and facilities.
