The campus began offering BS programs in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, and Software Engineering from Fall 2025 onward.

title: FAST-NU offers undergraduate programs at the bachelor's level in several disciplines
applicable_campus: The availability of specific programs depends on the campus
content: FAST-NU offers undergraduate programs at the bachelor's level in several disciplines. These include BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Artificial Intelligence, BS Electrical Engineering, BS Civil Engineering, BS Mechanical Engineering, BS Data Science, BS Cyber Security, BBA, and BS Accounting and Finance. The availability of specific programs depends on the campus.

title: BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, and BS Robotics are offered at all FAST campuses
applicable_campus: mputer Science, BS Software Engineering, and BS Robotics are offered at all FAST campuses
content: BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, and BS Robotics are offered at all FAST campuses. Other programs such as Mechanical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Actuarial Science, and Business Analytics may be offered at select campuses only. Students are advised to check the specific program offerings of their preferred campus before applying.

title: Each undergraduate bachelor's program at FAST-NU is a four-year, eight-semester program
content: Each undergraduate bachelor's program at FAST-NU is a four-year, eight-semester program. The curriculum combines core theoretical coursework with practical laboratory work, project-based learning, and industry internships. Students are expected to complete a final-year project as part of their degree requirements.

title: FAST-NU offers graduate-level programs at the MS level in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Electrical Engineering
content: FAST-NU offers graduate-level programs at the MS level in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Data Science, Cyber Security, Management Sciences, and other related fields. These programs are designed for students who hold a relevant bachelor's degree and wish to specialize further or pursue academic research.

title: The MS programs at FAST-NU require students to complete a specified number of credit hours of coursework and either writ
content: The MS programs at FAST-NU require students to complete a specified number of credit hours of coursework and either write a thesis or complete a project, depending on the chosen track. The duration of the MS program is typically one and a half to two years. Students with deficiencies in core undergraduate subjects may be required to complete additional prerequisite courses.

title: FAST-NU also offers MBA and MCS programs as part of its graduate portfolio
applicable_program: FAST-NU also offers MBA and MCS programs
content: FAST-NU also offers MBA and MCS programs as part of its graduate portfolio. These programs are designed for working professionals and recent graduates who wish to build competencies in management, information technology, or computing. Program structures may include evening classes to accommodate professional students.

title: At the doctoral level, FAST-NU offers PhD programs in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Management Sciences, and
content: At the doctoral level, FAST-NU offers PhD programs in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Management Sciences, and related disciplines. PhD candidates are required to complete graduate-level coursework, pass a qualifying examination, and then conduct independent original research culminating in a dissertation.

title: The PhD program at FAST-NU requires candidates to conduct original research and publish their findings in recognized aca
content: The PhD program at FAST-NU requires candidates to conduct original research and publish their findings in recognized academic journals as a condition of degree completion. As of March 2025, the publication requirement for PhD degrees involves one research article in a W-category HEC approved journal, or two research articles in X-category journals, depending on the specific program.

title: FAST-NU maintains a semester-based academic system
content: FAST-NU maintains a semester-based academic system. The academic year consists of two primary semesters, namely Fall and Spring, and a summer session may be offered for supplementary coursework. Each semester typically spans around 16 to 18 weeks of instruction followed by a final examination period.

title: The university provides an active campus life with numerous student-led clubs and societies covering areas such as compu
applicable_campus: The university provides an active campus
content: The university provides an active campus life with numerous student-led clubs and societies covering areas such as computing, entrepreneurship, arts, sports, and community service. Students are encouraged to participate in extracurricular activities to develop leadership, teamwork, and communication skills alongside their academic studies.

title: FAST-NU has strong industry linkages that provide students with opportunities for internships, industry visits, and care
content: FAST-NU has strong industry linkages that provide students with opportunities for internships, industry visits, and career placements. Graduates of FAST-NU are widely employed in the technology, engineering, and business sectors both within Pakistan and internationally. The university's alumni network spans multiple countries and industries.

title: FAST-NU is consistently ranked among the top universities in Pakistan by the Higher Education Commission
content: FAST-NU is consistently ranked among the top universities in Pakistan by the Higher Education Commission. In the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2025, FAST was ranked in the 351 to 400 band, reflecting its international academic standing in computing and technology disciplines.

title: The library at FAST-NU campuses provides students and faculty access to a wide range of academic books, research journal
applicable_campus: The library at FAST-NU campuses
content: The library at FAST-NU campuses provides students and faculty access to a wide range of academic books, research journals, and digital resources. The Islamabad campus has a newly developed library building that serves as a central resource for academic research and study. Digital library access is also available for enrolled students.

title: FAST-NU campuses are equipped with modern computer laboratories that are regularly updated with current hardware and sof
applicable_campus: FAST-NU campuses
content: FAST-NU campuses are equipped with modern computer laboratories that are regularly updated with current hardware and software. These labs support practical coursework, final-year projects, and research activities. Students have access to lab facilities during and beyond regular class hours to support their academic work.

title: The university operates an online student portal that allows students to register for courses, view academic results, tr
content: The university operates an online student portal that allows students to register for courses, view academic results, track CGPA, access fee information, and manage other administrative tasks. Faculty and students communicate through this system as well as through the official university email infrastructure.

title: FAST-NU has an established Office of Research and Graduate Studies that oversees postgraduate admissions, research super
applicable_campus: ensure that academic standards for MS and PhD programs are maintained across all campuses
applicable_program: Committee to ensure that academic standards for MS and PhD programs
content: FAST-NU has an established Office of Research and Graduate Studies that oversees postgraduate admissions, research supervision, and doctoral dissertation management. The office coordinates with the Graduate Studies Committee to ensure that academic standards for MS and PhD programs are maintained across all campuses.

title: Sports and recreational facilities are available at FAST-NU campuses to support student well-being and physical activity
applicable_campus: Sports and recreational facilities are available at FAST-NU campuses
content: Sports and recreational facilities are available at FAST-NU campuses to support student well-being and physical activity. Students participate in inter-campus and national-level sports competitions. Various cultural and technical festivals are also organized throughout the academic year, providing students with platforms to showcase their skills.

title: The contact number for FAST-NU is 111-128-128
applicable_campus: Campus-specific information can be accessed through the individual campus
content: The contact number for FAST-NU is 111-128-128. The official university website is nu.edu.pk. Campus-specific information can be accessed through the individual campus web portals, such as isb.nu.edu.pk for the Islamabad campus and pwr.nu.edu.pk for the Peshawar campus. Admissions-related information is managed through admissions.nu.edu.pk.

title: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences charges a one-time admission fee at the time of initial enrol
content: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences charges a one-time admission fee at the time of initial enrollment. This fee is non-refundable and must be paid before a student can confirm their seat. If a graduate of FAST-NU is admitted to a higher degree program at the same university, the admission fee is not charged again.

title: A refundable security deposit is payable by all new students at the time of admission
content: A refundable security deposit is payable by all new students at the time of admission. This deposit is held by the university throughout the duration of the student's enrollment and is returned upon graduation or official withdrawal, subject to the university's terms and conditions.

title: The tuition fee at FAST-NU is charged on a per-credit-hour basis
content: The tuition fee at FAST-NU is charged on a per-credit-hour basis. A standard undergraduate semester typically consists of five to six courses. The total per-semester tuition cost varies depending on the number of credit hours registered in that semester. Fee structures are subject to revision each academic year.

title: An application processing fee is required from all applicants, including those applying on the basis of NTS NAT or GAT t
content: An application processing fee is required from all applicants, including those applying on the basis of NTS NAT or GAT test scores. This fee must be deposited in the designated bank before the admit card can be printed. The processing fee is non-refundable regardless of whether the applicant appears for the test or is granted admission.

title: The fee structure at FAST-NU may vary across campuses and programs
applicable_campus: The fee structure at FAST-NU may vary across campuses
applicable_program: The fee structure at FAST-NU may vary across campuses and programs
content: The fee structure at FAST-NU may vary across campuses and programs. Students are advised to verify the exact fee schedule for their specific campus and program through the official university website or the relevant campus admissions office. The university updates its fee information periodically.

title: For MS and PhD programs, the tuition fee structure differs from undergraduate programs
applicable_campus: ecific fee amounts for postgraduate programs can be obtained from the respective campus
content: For MS and PhD programs, the tuition fee structure differs from undergraduate programs. Graduate students are charged tuition fees per course or per semester depending on the program structure. Specific fee amounts for postgraduate programs can be obtained from the respective campus or the university's admissions portal.

title: The total fees payable by a student during their enrollment include tuition fees, student activity fund contributions, a
content: The total fees payable by a student during their enrollment include tuition fees, student activity fund contributions, any applicable laboratory fees, and charges for specific course resources. Textbooks and additional academic resources are not covered by the tuition fee and are the responsibility of the student.

title: FAST-NU provides merit-based scholarships to exceptional students
applicable_campus: merit scholarships are offered to the top three position holders in the FAST-NU campus
content: FAST-NU provides merit-based scholarships to exceptional students. Merit scholarships are awarded to the top three position holders of each provincial examination board in the year of admission. Additionally, merit scholarships are offered to the top three position holders in the FAST-NU campus-wise admission merit list. These scholarships cover full tuition fees for eight regular semesters.

title: To retain the merit scholarship, a student must maintain a semester GPA of 3
content: To retain the merit scholarship, a student must maintain a semester GPA of 3.0 or higher in every semester without interruption. The scholarship is awarded for continuous, full-course-load studies and is renewable annually subject to academic performance. If a student's GPA falls below 3.0 in any semester, the scholarship may be discontinued.

title: The Higher Education Commission of Pakistan provides scholarships to students enrolled in MS and PhD programs at FAST-NU
applicable_program: an provides scholarships to students enrolled in MS and PhD programs
content: The Higher Education Commission of Pakistan provides scholarships to students enrolled in MS and PhD programs at FAST-NU. These scholarships cover tuition fees, a monthly stipend, and a book allowance. PhD students selected on HEC fellowship receive a monthly stipend of approximately Rs 60,000 along with a full tuition fee waiver.

title: The Government of Punjab offers scholarships to indigent students who hold a Punjab domicile
applicable_campus: Newly admitted students at any campus
content: The Government of Punjab offers scholarships to indigent students who hold a Punjab domicile. A fixed number of these scholarships are available for four-year undergraduate studies and cover a portion of the tuition fee. Newly admitted students at any campus may apply for this scholarship during the admission process.

title: The Balochistan Government offers scholarships specifically for students enrolled at the Karachi campus of FAST-NU, on a
applicable_campus: Government offers scholarships specifically for students enrolled at the Karachi campus
applicable_program: olarships are available for both undergraduate and graduate programs
content: The Balochistan Government offers scholarships specifically for students enrolled at the Karachi campus of FAST-NU, on a need-cum-merit basis. These scholarships are available for both undergraduate and graduate programs and cover the full tuition fee for the entire duration of the program. The scholarship is renewable annually.

title: FAST-NU offers need-based financial assistance to students who cannot afford to pay their tuition fees
content: FAST-NU offers need-based financial assistance to students who cannot afford to pay their tuition fees. This assistance is provided in the form of interest-free study loans for academically capable but financially constrained students. The financial assistance is limited to tuition fees only and does not cover other academic or living expenses.

title: Financial assistance at FAST-NU is reviewed and renewed every semester based on the student's ongoing academic performan
content: Financial assistance at FAST-NU is reviewed and renewed every semester based on the student's ongoing academic performance. Assistance is suspended if a student's CGPA falls below the minimum required to avoid academic warning. Once the student improves their CGPA and exits warning status, the assistance may be reinstated.

title: The OSAF, or Old Students Association of FAST, provides financial assistance to students who are unable to afford the fu
applicable_campus: tly offers loans to eligible students in the undergraduate program at the Lahore campus
content: The OSAF, or Old Students Association of FAST, provides financial assistance to students who are unable to afford the full tuition fee. This program currently offers loans to eligible students in the undergraduate program at the Lahore campus during both Fall and Spring semesters, excluding the first semester. Allocation is based on financial need as assessed by a panel of interviewers each semester.

title: FAST Alumni of North America provides funds for financial assistance to students who cannot afford their tuition
applicable_campus: terest-free loans, known as Qarz-e-Hasna, to needy and deserving students at any campus
content: FAST Alumni of North America provides funds for financial assistance to students who cannot afford their tuition. The Ihsan Trust also provides interest-free loans, known as Qarz-e-Hasna, to needy and deserving students at any campus of FAST-NU. These funds are allocated on a need-cum-merit basis to help students complete their higher education.

title: Several external community-based scholarships are also available to FAST-NU students
applicable_campus: For the Karachi campus
content: Several external community-based scholarships are also available to FAST-NU students. These include scholarships from the Memon Community, Bohra Community, Agha Khan Foundation, INFAQ Foundation, Ihsan Trust, and General Refaqat Memorial Scholarship. For the Karachi campus, scholarships are also available from the Alumni Association and the Hindu Community.

title: Students may also work as teaching assistants or in other university support roles in exchange for compensation
content: Students may also work as teaching assistants or in other university support roles in exchange for compensation. Students selected for such roles can receive a fixed amount per course for their services during the semester. These opportunities are available as a form of supplementary financial support.

title: Several commercial banks offer student loan programs that FAST-NU students may access
applicable_program: Several commercial banks offer student loan programs
content: Several commercial banks offer student loan programs that FAST-NU students may access. These include loan facilities from institutions such as MCB, Bank Alfalah, Bank Islami, and others. These are external loan arrangements and are subject to the respective bank's terms and eligibility criteria.

title: All students admitted to FAST-NU who require financial assistance are required to submit a financial aid application on
applicable_campus: A notice regarding the application window is posted on the campus
content: All students admitted to FAST-NU who require financial assistance are required to submit a financial aid application on the prescribed form. A notice regarding the application window is posted on the campus notice board at the start of each semester. Students must apply within the specified time to be considered for that semester's assistance.

title: FAST-NU does not guarantee financial assistance to all applying students
content: FAST-NU does not guarantee financial assistance to all applying students. The university has a limited pool of funds allocated for need-based support each semester. Priority is given to students with the most severe financial need as verified through the assessment process, combined with satisfactory academic performance.

title: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences offers a comprehensive range of undergraduate, graduate, and
applicable_campus: ensive range of undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral degree programs across its campuses
content: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences offers a comprehensive range of undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral degree programs across its campuses. The full and current list of all degree programs offered by the university, along with information about which programs are available at each campus, can be found at https://nu.edu.pk/Degree-Programs. Not all programs are offered at all campuses, so students are strongly advised to consult this page before applying.

title: FAST-NUCES operates six campuses in Pakistan, located in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Chiniot-Faisalabad, and M
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES operates six campuses
content: FAST-NUCES operates six campuses in Pakistan, located in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Chiniot-Faisalabad, and Multan. The Islamabad campus serves as the central administrative hub of the university. The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus is the largest campus of FAST-NUCES in terms of physical land area, spanning 22 acres along the Faisalabad-Chiniot Road, approximately 9 kilometers from the Faisalabad Motorway interchange.

title: The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus of FAST-NUCES was established in 2012
applicable_campus: The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus
content: The Chiniot-Faisalabad campus of FAST-NUCES was established in 2012. It is commonly referred to as the CFD campus. The campus is the fifth in the FAST-NUCES network and is strategically located at the crossroads of Faisalabad, which is Pakistan's leading industrial city, and the historically significant city of Chiniot. The campus is identified by the address: FAST Square, 9 km from Faisalabad Motorway Interchange towards Chiniot.

title: The CFD campus offers a wide range of undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral programs
applicable_campus: The CFD campus
content: The CFD campus offers a wide range of undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral programs. Undergraduate programs include BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Electrical Engineering, BS Artificial Intelligence, BS Business Analytics, BS Internet of Things, BS Robotics, and BBA. Graduate programs include MS Computer Science, MS Electrical Engineering, and MBA. Doctoral programs include PhD Computer Science and PhD Electrical Engineering.

title: The CFD campus houses three main academic schools and departments
applicable_campus: The CFD campus
content: The CFD campus houses three main academic schools and departments. The FAST School of Computing covers the departments of Computer Science, Software Engineering, and Artificial Intelligence and Data Science. The School of Engineering covers the Department of Electrical Engineering and the Department of Computer Engineering. The FAST School of Management offers BBA, BSBA, and MBA programs. The Department of Sciences and Humanities supports foundation courses across all programs.

title: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NUCES is situated at A
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
content: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NUCES is situated at A.K. Brohi Road, H-11/4, Islamabad. It is near the Nescom Hospital and is accessible via Srinagar Highway, the GT Road, and the Islamabad Metro Bus Service. The Islamabad campus offers programs through the FAST School of Computing, the School of Engineering, and the FAST School of Management. It is one of the oldest and most research-active campuses of the university.

title: The FAST School of Computing at the Islamabad campus offers programs in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artifici
applicable_campus: The FAST School of Computing at the Islamabad campus
applicable_program: The FAST School of Computing at the Islamabad campus offers programs
content: The FAST School of Computing at the Islamabad campus offers programs in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, and Cyber Security. The School of Engineering at Islamabad offers Electrical Engineering. The FAST School of Management at Islamabad offers BBA and programs in Management Sciences. The Islamabad campus is also home to several research laboratories focused on artificial intelligence, software engineering, cyber security, and computer networks.

title: The Lahore campus of FAST-NUCES is situated in Faisal Town and spans approximately 12
applicable_campus: The Lahore campus
content: The Lahore campus of FAST-NUCES is situated in Faisal Town and spans approximately 12.5 acres of land. It offers programs through the FAST School of Computing, the Department of Electrical Engineering, the Department of Civil Engineering, and the FAST School of Management. The Lahore campus is known for its active student societies and its strong linkages with Pakistan's technology and software industry.

title: The Lahore campus engineering laboratories include the Electronics Lab, Digital Systems Lab, Embedded Controls Lab, Ante
applicable_campus: The Lahore campus
content: The Lahore campus engineering laboratories include the Electronics Lab, Digital Systems Lab, Embedded Controls Lab, Antenna and Microwave Lab, Microprocessors Lab, and Communications Lab. The computing laboratories consist of database labs, programming labs, software engineering labs, and computer application labs. The entire Lahore campus is connected through a high-speed Local Area Network and is equipped with wireless hotspots throughout the facility.

title: The Karachi campus of FAST-NUCES operates two facilities
applicable_campus: The Karachi campus
applicable_program: PM to accommodate evening and weekend program
content: The Karachi campus of FAST-NUCES operates two facilities. The Main Campus is located in sector 17-D of Shah Latif Town on the National Highway, approximately 30 kilometers east of the Karachi Airport. The City Campus is located in Block 6 of PECHS on Sharah-e-Faisal. The Main Campus operates Monday to Friday from 8 AM to 4 PM. The City Campus operates six days a week and remains open until 9 PM to accommodate evening and weekend program students.

title: The Karachi campus offers a distinctive set of programs compared to other FAST campuses, including BS Actuarial Sciences
applicable_campus: The Karachi campus
applicable_program: The Karachi campus offers a distinctive set of programs
content: The Karachi campus offers a distinctive set of programs compared to other FAST campuses, including BS Actuarial Sciences and specialized MS programs in Data Science and Business Analytics. The campus has strong ties to Karachi's financial, technology, and engineering industries and provides students with access to internship and employment opportunities at leading companies based in the city.

title: The Peshawar campus of FAST-NUCES serves the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa region and offers programs primarily in computing and en
applicable_campus: The Peshawar campus
applicable_program: FAST-NUCES serves the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa region and offers programs
content: The Peshawar campus of FAST-NUCES serves the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa region and offers programs primarily in computing and engineering. The campus provides an on-campus hostel facility for male students. Students from outside Peshawar who are admitted to this campus can apply for hostel allocation by submitting the required form, CNIC copy, bank draft, and passport photograph. Seat allocation is done on a first-come, first-served basis based on available campus quota.

title: The Multan campus is the newest addition to the FAST-NUCES network
applicable_campus: The Multan campus
applicable_program: t is located in DHA Multan and began offering undergraduate programs
content: The Multan campus is the newest addition to the FAST-NUCES network. It is located in DHA Multan and began offering undergraduate programs in Fall 2025. The Multan campus currently focuses on programs in computing and emerging sciences and is being developed with modern academic and support infrastructure. Students from southern Punjab and surrounding areas are the primary target community for this campus.

title: The FAST-NUCES CFD campus is the only campus in the FAST-NUCES network that operates a university-managed hostel facilit
applicable_campus: The FAST-NUCES CFD campus
content: The FAST-NUCES CFD campus is the only campus in the FAST-NUCES network that operates a university-managed hostel facility for female students. All other campuses that provide hostel facilities, including Faisalabad and Peshawar, only manage hostels for male students. At campuses where no on-campus hostel is available, such as Lahore, students are directed to private hostels in the surrounding areas. All campuses maintain a list of reputable private hostels near the campus, which students can request from the campus administration.

title: The on-campus hostel at the CFD campus provides furnished rooms with scenic views of the campus lawns
applicable_campus: The on-campus
content: The on-campus hostel at the CFD campus provides furnished rooms with scenic views of the campus lawns. The hostel building is equipped with wireless internet access, though signals may vary depending on location within the building. Indoor and outdoor sports facilities are available within or adjacent to the hostel, including a futsal court, a football ground, a cricket ground, a lawn tennis court, a badminton court, a snooker table, a table tennis facility, and a stretching and fitness area.

title: The CFD campus hostel operates a mess facility that provides meals to resident students throughout the day
applicable_campus: The CFD campus
content: The CFD campus hostel operates a mess facility that provides meals to resident students throughout the day. The average monthly cost of the mess at the CFD campus hostel is approximately Rs 8,000 per month. This figure covers three daily meals and is subject to revision based on food costs. Hostel residents are required to participate in the mess plan as arranged by the hostel administration. Students have noted that the mess predominantly serves egg-based and basic food items, and food quality is a commonly discussed aspect of hostel life among current residents.

title: The hostel at the CFD campus uses automated management systems for attendance tracking, mess management, and hostel admi
applicable_campus: The hostel at the CFD campus
content: The hostel at the CFD campus uses automated management systems for attendance tracking, mess management, and hostel administration. The hostel is managed under formal Standard Operating Procedures that govern conduct, visiting hours, room maintenance, and disciplinary matters. A delegation from Government College University Faisalabad visited the CFD hostel in July 2025 and was briefed on these systems as a model for hostel management. The visit was led by Prof. Dr. Shahzad Sarfraz, Director of the CFD campus.

title: FAST-NUCES campuses operate bus services along selected city routes to assist students who commute daily from residentia
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES campuses
content: FAST-NUCES campuses operate bus services along selected city routes to assist students who commute daily from residential areas. Bus transport is currently available at the Faisalabad, Islamabad, Karachi, and Peshawar campuses. The Lahore campus does not currently provide bus transport. Routes and timings are determined by each campus based on student demand and city geography. Students should contact the campus administration for current route and fare information.

title: All FAST-NUCES campuses follow a five-day academic week from Monday to Friday
applicable_campus: All FAST-NUCES campuses
content: All FAST-NUCES campuses follow a five-day academic week from Monday to Friday. Classes at the CFD campus are held exclusively on weekdays and no Saturday classes are scheduled under normal circumstances. Makeup classes may occasionally be scheduled on Saturdays with the consent of the students and faculty involved. The Karachi campus has a weekend program specifically for working professionals and continuing education students. Other campuses do not run regular weekend programs but may schedule a few courses on Saturdays if needed.

title: FAST-NUCES is affiliated with the Pakistan Engineering Council, which has accredited the engineering programs at the uni
applicable_program: which has accredited the engineering programs
content: FAST-NUCES is affiliated with the Pakistan Engineering Council, which has accredited the engineering programs at the university. Undergraduate engineering degrees in Electrical Engineering, Civil Engineering, and Mechanical Engineering are PEC-accredited. This accreditation is important for graduates who wish to register as Professional Engineers in Pakistan or pursue further qualifications that require PEC-recognised degrees.

title: The ACM student chapter at FAST-NUCES was established on April 12, 2001, at the Karachi campus
applicable_campus: student chapter at FAST-NUCES was established on April 12, 2001, at the Karachi campus
content: The ACM student chapter at FAST-NUCES was established on April 12, 2001, at the Karachi campus. It operates under the Association for Computing Machinery, an international professional organization for the computing community. ACM chapters are now active at multiple FAST campuses. The chapters organize programming contests, software competitions, gaming events, quiz competitions, short courses, workshops, and seminars throughout the academic year.

title: SOFTEC, which stands for Software Exhibition and Competition, is the flagship annual event of the FAST-NUCES Lahore camp
applicable_campus: xhibition and Competition, is the flagship annual event of the FAST-NUCES Lahore campus
content: SOFTEC, which stands for Software Exhibition and Competition, is the flagship annual event of the FAST-NUCES Lahore campus. It has been organized since 1995 and is among the oldest student-run technology events in Pakistan. SOFTEC is a two-day event that includes a software project competition, a programming contest run in collaboration with the ACM chapter, an engineering project competition in collaboration with the NCS society, a microcontroller interfacing competition, a gaming competition, and a book festival. The event draws participants from universities across Pakistan and attracts industry professionals and judges who assess student work.

title: PROCOM is the flagship annual event of the FAST-NUCES Karachi campus and has been organized since 1998
applicable_campus: PROCOM is the flagship annual event of the FAST-NUCES Karachi campus
content: PROCOM is the flagship annual event of the FAST-NUCES Karachi campus and has been organized since 1998. It is a two-day event that serves as both a technology competition platform and a large-scale career fair. PROCOM 2025 featured over 40 competitions in fields including artificial intelligence, software development, gaming, cybersecurity, and data science. It also hosted a job fair featuring 40 to 50 local and international companies providing recruitment opportunities to students and recent graduates. Total prize money at recent editions of PROCOM has reached Rs 1.2 million.

title: NaSCon, which stands for National Students Convention, is the flagship annual event of the FAST-NUCES Islamabad campus
applicable_campus: al Students Convention, is the flagship annual event of the FAST-NUCES Islamabad campus
content: NaSCon, which stands for National Students Convention, is the flagship annual event of the FAST-NUCES Islamabad campus. It has been organized since 2001 and has grown to become one of the largest student-run technology festivals in Pakistan. NaSCon is a three-day event typically held in March or April at the Islamabad campus. The event features over 80 competitions spanning categories including computing, engineering, business, sports, and social events. NaSCon 2024 was held from March 1 to 3 at the Islamabad campus with a prize pool of over Rs 3 million and more than 10,000 participants from universities across the country.

title: NaSCon draws student delegations from universities across Pakistan, including large contingents from other FAST campuses
applicable_campus: s from universities across Pakistan, including large contingents from other FAST campuses
content: NaSCon draws student delegations from universities across Pakistan, including large contingents from other FAST campuses. At NaSCon 2025, held from April 18 to 20, a delegation of 118 students from the FAST-NUCES Lahore campus participated in events ranging from AI, cybersecurity, and software engineering to business challenges and sports. The event is run primarily by a student organizing committee supported by FAST Islamabad alumni and faculty mentors. Registration and event information for NaSCon is available at nascon.com.pk.

title: DAIRA is the flagship annual Olympiad event of the FAST-NUCES Chiniot-Faisalabad campus
applicable_campus: DAIRA is the flagship annual Olympiad event of the FAST-NUCES Chiniot-Faisalabad campus
content: DAIRA is the flagship annual Olympiad event of the FAST-NUCES Chiniot-Faisalabad campus. It is described as the largest Olympiad held in the Faisalabad region. DAIRA is a three-day event that features competitions in technology, computing, business, and cultural activities. DAIRA 2024 was held from April 26 to 28 at the CFD campus and attracted participants and sponsors from across Punjab. The event is organized by student societies and supported by the CFD campus administration.

title: FAST-NUCES Lahore hosts Geek Week, which is an annual computing and technology event that has attracted more than 1,000
content: FAST-NUCES Lahore hosts Geek Week, which is an annual computing and technology event that has attracted more than 1,000 student participants from universities across Pakistan. The event promotes technology awareness and provides a platform for students to compete in computing, programming, and technical challenges.

title: NUTEC is the annual flagship event of the FAST-NUCES Peshawar campus
applicable_campus: NUTEC is the annual flagship event of the FAST-NUCES Peshawar campus
content: NUTEC is the annual flagship event of the FAST-NUCES Peshawar campus. It is organized by students of the Peshawar campus and covers competitions in computing, engineering, and related technical fields. The event showcases student projects and encourages inter-university academic competition in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa region.

title: FAST-NUCES students participate actively in the International Collegiate Programming Contest, commonly known as the ICPC
applicable_campus: FAST teams from multiple campuses
applicable_program: udents participate actively in the International Collegiate Program
content: FAST-NUCES students participate actively in the International Collegiate Programming Contest, commonly known as the ICPC. The ICPC is considered the world's most prestigious programming competition for university students. Teams of three students represent their university at regional and national rounds. FAST-NUCES Lahore has achieved notable results in ICPC Asia Regional competitions. At one edition of the ICPC Asia Regional held at GIKI, a FAST Lahore team from the MAJORS group secured sixth position out of 70 teams from across Pakistan. FAST teams from multiple campuses regularly qualify for national rounds.

title: The Sportics NUCES society organizes a national-level competition called the Olympiad, which is an annual event that bri
applicable_campus: annual event that brings together competitive programmers from across different campuses
applicable_program: which is an annual event that brings together competitive program
content: The Sportics NUCES society organizes a national-level competition called the Olympiad, which is an annual event that brings together competitive programmers from across different campuses and backgrounds. The Olympiad is open to coders of all skill levels and serves as a platform to promote competitive programming culture within the FAST-NUCES student community. Details about the Sportics Olympiad are available at fastolympiad.com.

title: FAST-NUCES campuses host IEEE Student Branch chapters at the Karachi, Lahore, and Islamabad campuses
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES campuses
content: FAST-NUCES campuses host IEEE Student Branch chapters at the Karachi, Lahore, and Islamabad campuses. The IEEE Student Branch organizes seminars, conferences, workshops, and industry-academic interaction events. The Karachi campus IEEE branch has organized IEEE PSC events and similar technical conferences. These chapters connect students with international professional networks and provide opportunities to present research and projects at IEEE-affiliated events.

title: The FAST School of Management at the CFD campus operates an Executive Development Centre, commonly known as the EDC
applicable_campus: The FAST School of Management at the CFD campus
content: The FAST School of Management at the CFD campus operates an Executive Development Centre, commonly known as the EDC. The EDC organizes professional development workshops, training sessions, and industry conferences open to faculty, professionals, and working students. Recent EDC programs at CFD have included workshops on Full Stack Web Development, Business Analytics using Excel and Power BI, Accounting with QuickBooks, AI-based programming for non-programmers, and case writing for business education.

title: The Quality Enhancement Cell, referred to as QEC, operates at each FAST-NUCES campus to evaluate and improve academic an
applicable_campus: The Quality Enhancement Cell, referred to as QEC, operates at each FAST-NUCES campus
content: The Quality Enhancement Cell, referred to as QEC, operates at each FAST-NUCES campus to evaluate and improve academic and institutional performance. The QEC conducts periodic Institutional Performance Evaluation visits to assess faculty performance, program quality, student outcomes, and administrative efficiency. The CFD campus QEC organized a three-day IPE visit in March 2024. These evaluations are a requirement under HEC accreditation and quality assurance frameworks.

title: The Career Services Office at FAST-NUCES campuses facilitates connections between students, alumni, and industry employe
applicable_campus: The Career Services Office at FAST-NUCES campuses
content: The Career Services Office at FAST-NUCES campuses facilitates connections between students, alumni, and industry employers. Each campus holds periodic on-campus recruitment drives where technology companies conduct interviews and hire graduating students. The CSO at the Lahore campus organized an on-campus recruitment drive for GoSaaS in April 2025, targeting graduating batches in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, and Electrical Engineering.

title: The CFD campus organizes an annual Job Fair that invites companies from across Punjab to recruit students and recent gra
applicable_campus: The CFD campus
content: The CFD campus organizes an annual Job Fair that invites companies from across Punjab to recruit students and recent graduates. The Job Fair 2024 at CFD was held on April 17, 2024, and was attended by numerous industry sponsors including companies such as KATco. The Job Fair provides students with direct access to hiring managers and career counselors from technology, engineering, and business sectors.

title: FAST-NUCES campuses provide access to the university's digital library system, which is accessible through nu
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES campuses
content: FAST-NUCES campuses provide access to the university's digital library system, which is accessible through nu.insigniails.com/Library/Home. This system allows enrolled students and faculty to access academic books, research journals, and digital resources from any location. Physical libraries at each campus also hold printed collections that support course-related and research reading needs.

title: Student academic and administrative management at FAST-NUCES is handled through two primary online platforms
content: Student academic and administrative management at FAST-NUCES is handled through two primary online platforms. The FLEX portal, accessible at flexstudent.nu.edu.pk, is used for course registration, grade access, fee management, and other student-related administrative functions. The SLATE platform, accessible at slate.nu.edu.pk, is used for academic resource management. Students are assigned login credentials at the time of enrollment.

title: The Gatepass Application system at the CFD campus is a digital tool that students can use to formally request entry or e
applicable_campus: The Gatepass Application system at the CFD campus
content: The Gatepass Application system at the CFD campus is a digital tool that students can use to formally request entry or exit permissions beyond standard campus hours. This system digitizes a process that is otherwise managed manually at many institutions. Access to the gatepass application is available through the CFD campus website at cfd.nu.edu.pk.

title: FAST-NUCES has a formal Disability and Accessibility Committee at the CFD campus
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES has a formal Disability and Accessibility Committee at the CFD campus
content: FAST-NUCES has a formal Disability and Accessibility Committee at the CFD campus. This committee works to ensure that students with physical or other disabilities receive appropriate academic and campus support. The committee addresses accessibility issues within the physical campus environment and academic process and is a formal institutional body listed on the CFD campus website.

title: The GEIAH committee at FAST-NUCES stands for Gender Equity, Inclusion, and Anti-Harassment
applicable_campus: It was formally established at the CFD campus
content: The GEIAH committee at FAST-NUCES stands for Gender Equity, Inclusion, and Anti-Harassment. It was formally established at the CFD campus in September 2025 through an official notification. The committee is responsible for creating a safe, inclusive, and respectful campus environment for all students, staff, and faculty. Contact and policy information for GEIAH is available on the CFD campus website.

title: FAST-NUCES alumni are organized through two main bodies
content: FAST-NUCES alumni are organized through two main bodies. The FAST Alumni Network, known as FAN, is accessible at fan.nu.edu.pk. The Old Students Association of FAST, known as OSAF, is accessible at osaf.org.pk. These platforms allow graduates to maintain connections with the university, participate in alumni activities, and contribute to financial assistance programs for current students.

title: FAST-NUCES holds its Board of Advanced Studies and Research meetings, referred to as BASR meetings, at regular intervals
applicable_campus: online by the Registrar, Directors, Deans, and Heads of Department from all five campuses
content: FAST-NUCES holds its Board of Advanced Studies and Research meetings, referred to as BASR meetings, at regular intervals to review and approve PhD degrees for qualifying candidates. The 50th BASR meeting was held on March 19, 2024, chaired by the Rector of FAST-NUCES, Dr. Aftab Ahmad. The meeting was attended online by the Registrar, Directors, Deans, and Heads of Department from all five campuses at the time, namely CFD, Islamabad, Karachi, Lahore, and Peshawar. Six PhD degrees were approved at that meeting across Management Sciences, Software Engineering, and Computer Science disciplines.

title: FAST-NUCES faculty are research-active professionals with doctoral qualifications from national and international univer
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
content: FAST-NUCES faculty are research-active professionals with doctoral qualifications from national and international universities. The Islamabad campus faculty includes researchers with postdoctoral qualifications from institutions including Princeton University and the University of Nottingham. Faculty members publish research in HEC-recognized journals and participate in funded projects from national and international funding bodies.

title: Notable research areas at FAST-NUCES Islamabad include the Internet of Things, artificial intelligence, cyber security,
applicable_campus: The MARS Research Lab at the Islamabad campus
content: Notable research areas at FAST-NUCES Islamabad include the Internet of Things, artificial intelligence, cyber security, computer vision, underwater wireless communication, and software engineering. The MARS Research Lab at the Islamabad campus is directed by Dr. Niaz Ahmed and focuses on underwater communication systems, embedded systems, and sensor networks. The campus also has a dedicated Department of Cyber Security offering both BS and graduate level programs, which is among the few such dedicated departments in a Pakistani university.

title: FAST-NUCES has produced graduates who have gone on to achieve prominent positions in technology and business globally
applicable_campus: Alumni from the Lahore campus
content: FAST-NUCES has produced graduates who have gone on to achieve prominent positions in technology and business globally. Alumni from the Lahore campus include Arif Iqbal, co-founder and CEO of LAAM, a South Asian fashion technology platform, who raised 5.5 million USD in seed funding. He graduated from FAST-NUCES Lahore in Batch 2001 and later worked at Facebook, Pinterest, and eBay before founding LAAM. The Karachi campus alumni include prominent figures in the visual effects industry, including a graduate associated with Oscar-winning projects.

title: The CFD campus has a total of over 17 active student societies
applicable_campus: The CFD campus
content: The CFD campus has a total of over 17 active student societies. These include technical societies such as the ACM chapter, the Google Developer Student Clubs, the Microsoft Learn Student Ambassadors, the IEEE Student Branch, and the FAST Club of App Development and Programming. Creative and cultural societies include the FAST Design and Marketing Society, the FAST Photography Society, the FAST Symphonics Society, the Rang arts society, and the Sukhan literary society. Other societies include the BOL Debating Society, the FAST English Club, the FAST Goonj community welfare society, the Fast Corporate Society, the Sports Guild Society, the FAST E-Gaming Society, the Silent Steps welfare society, and the Decentralized Asset Trading Society.

title: The FAST Goonj society at CFD is a community welfare organization that runs charitable initiatives and social outreach p
applicable_program: zation that runs charitable initiatives and social outreach programs
content: The FAST Goonj society at CFD is a community welfare organization that runs charitable initiatives and social outreach programs. Silent Steps is a society that focuses on silent service and social responsibility. These non-technical societies give students an opportunity to engage with the broader community and develop their social and humanitarian sensibilities alongside their technical education.

title: The Sukhan literary society at the CFD campus is dedicated to promoting Urdu and English literature, creative writing, a
applicable_campus: The Sukhan literary society at the CFD campus
content: The Sukhan literary society at the CFD campus is dedicated to promoting Urdu and English literature, creative writing, and intellectual discussion. The BOL Debating Society organizes parliamentary-style debates, intra-FAST debates, and public speaking events. These societies contribute to the holistic development of students and provide platforms for expression, civic engagement, and intellectual growth.

title: FAST-NUCES campuses are equipped with auditoriums that serve as venues for guest lectures, seminar events, convocations,
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES campuses
content: FAST-NUCES campuses are equipped with auditoriums that serve as venues for guest lectures, seminar events, convocations, and society programs. The Islamabad campus has a significant history of hosting over 85 convocation ceremonies since its founding. Convocations at FAST campuses are major events that formally recognize graduates from BS, BBA, MBA, MS, and PhD programs and are attended by university leadership, faculty, families, and distinguished guests.

title: FAST-NUCES is a non-profit organization whose campus operations are funded by tuition fees collected from students
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES is a non-profit organization whose campus
content: FAST-NUCES is a non-profit organization whose campus operations are funded by tuition fees collected from students. The university's stated mission includes producing scientists, engineers, and technology professionals of the highest caliber who contribute to the social, economic, and technological development of Pakistan. The university was the first private sector institution in Pakistan to offer an undergraduate Computer Science degree, and its graduates have played a foundational role in building Pakistan's information technology industry.

title: The contact details for each FAST-NUCES campus are as follows
applicable_campus: The contact details for each FAST-NUCES campus
content: The contact details for each FAST-NUCES campus are as follows. The Islamabad campus is at A.K. Brohi Road, H-11/4, Islamabad, reachable through the main helpline 111-128-128 and at isb.nu.edu.pk. The Lahore campus is in Faisal Town, Lahore, reachable at lhr.nu.edu.pk. The Karachi campus is at Shah Latif Town, National Highway, Karachi, reachable at khi.nu.edu.pk. The CFD campus is at FAST Square, 9 km from the Faisalabad Motorway Interchange towards Chiniot, reachable at (041) 111-128-128 and at cfd.nu.edu.pk, email info.cfd@nu.edu.pk. The Peshawar campus is reachable at (091) 111-128-128 and at pwr.nu.edu.pk. The university's central admissions website is admissions.nu.edu.pk and the main website is nu.edu.pk.

title: FAST-NU does not provide on-campus hostel facilities at all of its campuses
applicable_campus: FAST-NU does not provide on-campus
content: FAST-NU does not provide on-campus hostel facilities at all of its campuses. The availability of hostel accommodation varies significantly from one campus to another. Students planning to study at a campus that does not offer on-campus accommodation must arrange private housing in nearby areas.

title: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NU has limited on-campus accommodation available for students
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
content: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NU has limited on-campus accommodation available for students. Due to the limited capacity of on-campus hostel spaces, many students at the Islamabad campus prefer or are required to arrange off-campus accommodations in nearby sectors of the city.

title: The Islamabad campus is located in H-11/4, which is a well-developed and peaceful residential and educational sector of
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
content: The Islamabad campus is located in H-11/4, which is a well-developed and peaceful residential and educational sector of the city. Students residing off-campus typically find accommodation in nearby sectors such as G-10, G-11, I-8, and I-9. These areas are well connected to the campus through public transport including the Islamabad Metro Bus Service.

title: The Lahore campus of FAST-NU does not offer on-campus hostel facilities
applicable_campus: The Lahore campus
content: The Lahore campus of FAST-NU does not offer on-campus hostel facilities. Students from outside Lahore who are admitted to this campus are required to arrange private accommodation in the surrounding areas. The campus is located in Faisal Town, and several private hostels and student residences exist in the vicinity of the campus.

title: The Peshawar campus of FAST-NU provides a hostel facility for students requiring accommodation
applicable_campus: The Peshawar campus
content: The Peshawar campus of FAST-NU provides a hostel facility for students requiring accommodation. Students wishing to apply for hostel allocation must submit a completed Hostel Allocation Request Form. The form must be accompanied by a copy of the applicant's CNIC, a bank draft for the required fees, and a passport-size photograph.

title: Hostel seat allocation at the Peshawar campus is carried out on a first-come, first-served basis and is subject to avail
applicable_campus: Hostel seat allocation at the Peshawar campus
content: Hostel seat allocation at the Peshawar campus is carried out on a first-come, first-served basis and is subject to availability within the degree and batch-wise quota. Students from the Peshawar campus are given priority over students from other campuses when allocating hostel seats. Applications can be submitted by post or by hand to the campus address.

title: Applications for hostel accommodation at the Peshawar campus are considered received only when they are complete in all
applicable_campus: Applications for hostel accommodation at the Peshawar campus
content: Applications for hostel accommodation at the Peshawar campus are considered received only when they are complete in all required documents. Incomplete applications are not processed. Applicants can check the status of their hostel application online through the campus web portal. A separate confirmation email is not sent to applicants.

title: The Peshawar campus hostel also maintains a list of external hostel options near the campus for students who are unable
applicable_campus: The Peshawar campus
content: The Peshawar campus hostel also maintains a list of external hostel options near the campus for students who are unable to secure on-campus accommodation. These external hostel rates are updated periodically by the campus. The university provides this list for information purposes only and does not endorse any external hostel for the quality of facilities or services.

title: Students admitted to FAST-NU are responsible for arranging their own accommodation if on-campus hostel facilities are no
applicable_campus: admitted to FAST-NU are responsible for arranging their own accommodation if on-campus
content: Students admitted to FAST-NU are responsible for arranging their own accommodation if on-campus hostel facilities are not available at their campus. The university advises students to secure accommodation before the start of the semester to avoid any disruption to their studies. Students should verify the current hostel status directly with their campus before making decisions.

title: Off-campus hostels in the vicinity of FAST campuses are available in various configurations including single, double, an
applicable_campus: Off-campus
content: Off-campus hostels in the vicinity of FAST campuses are available in various configurations including single, double, and shared rooms. Rates for such accommodations vary depending on the location, room type, and facilities provided. Students are advised to research and visit potential hostels before confirming any arrangement.

title: Transport facilities are available at FAST-NU campuses to assist students commuting from residential areas to the campus
applicable_campus: Transport facilities are available at FAST-NU campuses
content: Transport facilities are available at FAST-NU campuses to assist students commuting from residential areas to the campus. Students living off-campus can use university transport, public transport, or private arrangements to commute. The availability and routes of university transport vary by campus.

title: The campus dining facilities at FAST-NU include student cafeterias and canteens that provide meals and refreshments duri
applicable_campus: The campus
content: The campus dining facilities at FAST-NU include student cafeterias and canteens that provide meals and refreshments during the academic day. These facilities are accessible to all enrolled students. Mess facilities, where students can arrange regular meal plans, may also be available at campuses that provide hostel accommodation.

title: Students residing in university-affiliated accommodation or on-campus hostels are required to follow the hostel rules an
applicable_campus: Students residing in university-affiliated accommodation or on-campus
content: Students residing in university-affiliated accommodation or on-campus hostels are required to follow the hostel rules and regulations established by the university. These rules govern conduct, visiting hours, maintenance of facilities, and disciplinary matters. Violation of hostel rules may result in the cancellation of hostel accommodation.

title: FAST-NU encourages outstation students to explore all available housing options, including university hostel facilities,
applicable_campus: Students are advised to contact the relevant campus
content: FAST-NU encourages outstation students to explore all available housing options, including university hostel facilities, affiliated external hostels, and private rentals. Students are advised to contact the relevant campus administration directly for the most accurate and up-to-date information on hostel availability, rules, and charges before applying.

title: The FAST-NUCES Chiniot-Faisalabad campus hostel is available exclusively to enrolled students of the campus
applicable_campus: The FAST-NUCES Chiniot-Faisalabad campus
content: The FAST-NUCES Chiniot-Faisalabad campus hostel is available exclusively to enrolled students of the campus. Students who wish to reside in the hostel must apply through the prescribed hostel application form. Only students who are currently enrolled at the campus are eligible for accommodation, and the hostel is not open to external applicants or non-enrolled individuals.

title: Both the boys and girls hostels at the campus operate on a shared room arrangement
applicable_campus: Both the boys and girls hostels at the campus
content: Both the boys and girls hostels at the campus operate on a shared room arrangement. Each room accommodates exactly six students. Rooms are not allocated to fewer than six occupants. Students may submit roommate preferences, but these preferences are only considered if all six students mutually agree and submit their request together as a complete group. Partial group requests are not accommodated.

title: Priority in hostel seat allocation is given to students who reside in cities other than Chiniot and Faisalabad and who d
applicable_campus: de in cities other than Chiniot and Faisalabad and who do not have access to the campus
content: Priority in hostel seat allocation is given to students who reside in cities other than Chiniot and Faisalabad and who do not have access to the campus transport facility. Students who belong to Chiniot or Faisalabad city and can use campus transport are given the lowest priority when seats are being allocated, considering the limited total hostel capacity available.

title: Room allotments in the hostel are made by the respective Warden
content: Room allotments in the hostel are made by the respective Warden. The hostel is managed on a non-profit basis, and accommodation charges are periodically revised by the competent authority on the recommendations of the Hostel Management Committee. Revisions are made based on anticipated expenses rather than for profit.

title: A refundable security deposit of Rs 23,000 is payable at the time of hostel admission
content: A refundable security deposit of Rs 23,000 is payable at the time of hostel admission. This amount is paid only once and is refunded when the student formally vacates the hostel, provided no outstanding dues or damages exist. Hostel charges are deposited online into the designated bank account, and the payment receipt must be uploaded through the Google Form provided by the hostel management.

title: The hostel operates on a semester-based allotment cycle
content: The hostel operates on a semester-based allotment cycle. The first allotment period covers the Fall semester from August to January. The second allotment period covers the Spring semester from February to July. Allotment of students is done at the end of every semester for the following period. Students must reapply each semester through the hostel management process.

title: No refund of accommodation charges is made if a student voluntarily leaves the hostel mid-semester or is expelled from t
content: No refund of accommodation charges is made if a student voluntarily leaves the hostel mid-semester or is expelled from the hostel for disciplinary reasons. Students who leave early are not entitled to a partial refund of the fees already paid for that allotment period.

title: The university may provide generator or UPS backup in the event of electricity load shedding
content: The university may provide generator or UPS backup in the event of electricity load shedding. Fuel charges for the generator may be shared among students and met from hostel funds. This provision is subject to availability and is not guaranteed at all times.

title: The Warden has the authority to shift a student to a different room at any time during the semester without providing a
content: The Warden has the authority to shift a student to a different room at any time during the semester without providing a reason, if adjustments are required for management or disciplinary purposes. Students are expected to comply with any such room reassignment instructions. Unauthorized room occupation or room swapping without permission from the hostel administration is a serious violation that is dealt with by the Hostel Disciplinary Committee.

title: Room furniture and all electrical fittings in the hostel must be maintained in good condition by the occupants of the ro
content: Room furniture and all electrical fittings in the hostel must be maintained in good condition by the occupants of the room. Students are collectively responsible for any loss or damage to hostel property within their room. In the event of damage to university equipment, appliances, furniture, or other fixtures, the university reserves the right to charge the responsible students for the full or partial cost of repair or replacement.

title: Parents or guardians are not permitted to stay overnight in the hostel
content: Parents or guardians are not permitted to stay overnight in the hostel. Visitors must register in the visitors register maintained at the hostel gate. Guests and visitors are not allowed inside the hostel premises without prior permission from the Warden. Any undesirable activity or misconduct observed by students must be reported to the concerned Warden immediately.

title: Male students residing in the hostel must not be absent from the hostel between 10:00 PM and 6:30 AM without obtaining p
content: Male students residing in the hostel must not be absent from the hostel between 10:00 PM and 6:30 AM without obtaining prior permission from the hostel administration. Female students must be inside the hostel before 5:30 PM and are not permitted to leave the hostel after this time without prior permission. Unauthorized absence from the hostel at night is treated as an act of indiscipline and may result in disciplinary action.

title: Student attendance at the hostel is recorded electronically after the hostel gate closes each evening
content: Student attendance at the hostel is recorded electronically after the hostel gate closes each evening. Students are required to present their RFID card at the gate and scan it on the designated computer terminal when entering the hostel. This automated attendance system is used to monitor the presence of residents within the hostel premises.

title: Ragging of any kind is strictly prohibited in the hostel and will result in immediate expulsion from the hostel
content: Ragging of any kind is strictly prohibited in the hostel and will result in immediate expulsion from the hostel. Any student found engaging in ragging against fellow residents will face mandatory removal from the hostel without exception. The hostel administration treats ragging as a zero-tolerance offense.

title: Possession of any type of weapon, whether licensed or unlicensed, inside the hostel is treated as a criminal offense
applicable_campus: d within the hostel premises and may result in expulsion from the hostel and the campus
content: Possession of any type of weapon, whether licensed or unlicensed, inside the hostel is treated as a criminal offense. Such cases are reported directly to the police. Similarly, consumption, storage, or supply of liquor, narcotics, illegal drugs, or any intoxicant is strictly prohibited within the hostel premises and may result in expulsion from the hostel and the campus.

title: The FAST-NUCES CFD campus hostels are designated as complete no-smoking zones
applicable_campus: The FAST-NUCES CFD campus
content: The FAST-NUCES CFD campus hostels are designated as complete no-smoking zones. All forms of tobacco consumption, including cigarettes and e-cigarettes such as vapes, are strictly prohibited inside and outside the hostel buildings. This policy is aligned with the HEC Policy on Drug and Tobacco Abuse 2021 and the FAST Anti-Drug and Tobacco Rules.

title: Violations of the no-smoking and anti-drug policy in the hostel may result in a fine ranging from Rs 10,000 to Rs 20,000
applicable_campus: ing from Rs 10,000 to Rs 20,000, suspension from the hostel, suspension from the campus
content: Violations of the no-smoking and anti-drug policy in the hostel may result in a fine ranging from Rs 10,000 to Rs 20,000, suspension from the hostel, suspension from the campus, or all of the above combined. Cases of serious violations may be referred to the Hostel Management Committee and the Anti-Drug and Tobacco Committee for further action. The identity of students who report violations is kept confidential by the hostel management.

title: All residents are collectively responsible for ensuring that no smoking or drug-related activity occurs in their shared
content: All residents are collectively responsible for ensuring that no smoking or drug-related activity occurs in their shared room. If any smoking material or evidence of drug use is found in a room during an inspection, the Hostel Management Committee may take action against all occupants of that room. Students are therefore advised to report any violation they observe in their room immediately to the Head Warden or Hostel Supervisor.

title: Use of unauthorized electrical appliances in the hostel rooms is prohibited
content: Use of unauthorized electrical appliances in the hostel rooms is prohibited. Fans and lights must be switched off when students leave their rooms to conserve energy. Playing music or videos inside rooms in a manner that disturbs other residents is also prohibited. Running torrents on the hostel internet connection is not allowed unless explicitly permitted by the Warden.

title: Ballgames are not permitted inside the hostel building
content: Ballgames are not permitted inside the hostel building. Functions or private parties in the hostel premises require prior permission from the Warden. Personal servants are not allowed inside the hostel. Jumping over hostel walls is strictly prohibited. Grouping, lobbying, and social media campaigns targeting other residents or the hostel administration are not allowed.

title: No discrimination based on region, caste, or religion is permitted within the hostel community
content: No discrimination based on region, caste, or religion is permitted within the hostel community. Students are expected to maintain peace, respect, and harmony among all residents. Controversial discussions of a political or divisive nature are not allowed within the hostel. Students must maintain a friendly and quiet environment that is conducive to studying.

title: Valuables should not be kept in hostel rooms
content: Valuables should not be kept in hostel rooms. Room inspections may be conducted by the hostel administration at any time without prior notice. Students must cooperate fully with any room inspection carried out by the Warden or authorized hostel staff. Maintenance complaints should be submitted to the Hostel Representative, who then coordinates with the hostel management team.

title: Abusive language or behavior toward any hostel resident, staff member, or administration representative will not be tole
content: Abusive language or behavior toward any hostel resident, staff member, or administration representative will not be tolerated. Gatherings in unauthorized areas of the hostel are prohibited. Students must maintain personal hygiene and appropriate attire at all times within the hostel. Stickers must not be pasted on hostel walls or windows, and walls must not be defaced in any way.

title: Male visitors are not permitted to enter the female hostel under any circumstances
content: Male visitors are not permitted to enter the female hostel under any circumstances. Female visitors are not permitted inside the girls hostel after 5:30 PM. Female students must be back in the hostel before 5:30 PM and must obtain formal permission from the hostel administration whenever they wish to leave the hostel premises. Female visitors are also not permitted in the boys hostel.

title: Parents and guardians of female students must provide the hostel administration with a list of approved guests who are p
applicable_campus: Before leaving for the campus
content: Parents and guardians of female students must provide the hostel administration with a list of approved guests who are permitted to visit or pick up the student. Before leaving for the campus from the hostel, all female students must strictly follow the campus dress code policy in place at the time.

title: The mess facility in the hostel is optional for students
content: The mess facility in the hostel is optional for students. Students who wish to use the mess must fill out the mess Google form for the following day before 11:59 PM on the current day. Students must present or scan their RFID card in the mess hall to record their attendance for each meal. Students must indicate their meal preferences in advance through the online system.

title: Mess timings must be strictly observed by all participating students
content: Mess timings must be strictly observed by all participating students. The menu for each day is decided by the mess committee and is not individually customizable. Utensils from the mess hall are not to be taken outside the mess premises. The use of the kitchen for private cooking is not allowed. Students must dine only in the designated hostel mess and not in their rooms.

title: Mess dues must be paid before the stipulated payment date each month
content: Mess dues must be paid before the stipulated payment date each month. The average monthly cost of the mess at the hostel is approximately Rs 8,000 per month. A deep freezer and oven are available in the mess facility for students who bring cooked food from home and wish to reheat it. Students may store food items in the deep freezer, but only in containers of maximum 4 by 4 inches in size. Each student is allowed to store only one week's worth of food in the deep freezer at a time and must not displace another student's stored items.

title: Only plastic body air coolers are permitted in hostel rooms
content: Only plastic body air coolers are permitted in hostel rooms. Lahori air coolers and steel body air coolers are strictly prohibited because their usage can cause short circuits and pose a safety hazard. Students who wish to bring a plastic body air cooler must submit an online application through the designated Google Form to start or close the air cooler facility. No application submitted in physical form is entertained for this request.

title: Students who use an air cooler in their room must pay an advance monthly electricity charge as defined by the hostel man
content: Students who use an air cooler in their room must pay an advance monthly electricity charge as defined by the hostel management. The exact amount of this charge is communicated by the management at the time of approval. Before installation of any cooling appliance, students must also pay a security deposit of Rs 10,000 against the electricity bill. This amount is submitted through a request to the Hall Warden using the general request form.

title: Air conditioners may be installed in hostel rooms with prior written approval from the Warden
content: Air conditioners may be installed in hostel rooms with prior written approval from the Warden. All students sharing the room must sign the formal application before an AC can be approved. Only Split or Inverter-type air conditioners with a minimum 3-star energy efficiency rating are permitted. Window-type air conditioners and other non-compliant types are strictly not allowed in any hostel room.

title: The air conditioner must be registered in the name of one student who will act as the primary responsible party
content: The air conditioner must be registered in the name of one student who will act as the primary responsible party. This registered student is responsible for ensuring compliance with all hostel rules related to the AC, timely payment of electricity bills, maintenance and repairs, and resolving any disputes related to AC usage among roommates. The registered student must be an actively enrolled and residing occupant of the room in which the AC is installed.

title: A refundable security deposit of Rs 25,000 must be paid to the hostel office before the installation of an air condition
content: A refundable security deposit of Rs 25,000 must be paid to the hostel office before the installation of an air conditioner. This deposit may be adjusted against electricity bills or any damages caused by the installation or operation of the AC. The deposit is refunded only after all dues have been cleared and the AC has been properly removed without causing any damage to hostel property.

title: Each AC-equipped room must have exactly six students
content: Each AC-equipped room must have exactly six students. If a group of five students wishes to install an AC and the sixth roommate is unwilling, the hostel management reserves the right to allocate the sixth spot to any other suitable student in order to maintain fairness and optimal room occupancy. Individual room shifts are not permitted. Only a complete group of six occupants may request a room swap, subject to approval by the hostel administration.

title: Monthly electricity billing for air conditioners is issued in the name of the registered student and must be paid before
content: Monthly electricity billing for air conditioners is issued in the name of the registered student and must be paid before the 10th of every month. Delay in payment may result in disconnection of the AC's electricity supply, deduction from the security deposit, and disciplinary action. Maintenance of the AC unit is the sole responsibility of the student group, and any damage to walls, wiring, or other hostel infrastructure caused during installation or removal must be repaired at the students' expense.

title: Submeters are installed in hostel rooms to measure electricity consumption by individually owned appliances such as room
content: Submeters are installed in hostel rooms to measure electricity consumption by individually owned appliances such as room fridges and air conditioners. Monthly electricity bills for these appliances are calculated based on the actual meter reading and units consumed. This system ensures that each student group pays only for the electricity their personal appliances consume.

title: Students may bring their own refrigerators to the hostel room with the consent of all roommates
