content: Students may bring their own refrigerators to the hostel room with the consent of all roommates. Before installation of a room fridge, students must pay a security deposit of Rs 10,000 against the electricity bill. The request for fridge installation is submitted to the Hall Warden through the general request form. Monthly electricity charges for the fridge are then billed based on submeter readings.

title: The hostel has a Physical Fitness Center available to resident students
content: The hostel has a Physical Fitness Center available to resident students. The registration fee for the fitness center is Rs 500 and is charged only once. The semester fee for use of the fitness center is Rs 3,000 per semester. Students are required to re-rack weights and maintain cleanliness in the fitness center after each workout session. Loud music, foul language, misbehavior toward instructors or support staff, and fighting are all strictly prohibited inside the fitness center.

title: Smoking is not permitted inside the fitness center
content: Smoking is not permitted inside the fitness center. Use of supplements and anabolic substances is also not allowed within the fitness center premises. Violation of fitness center rules may result in cancellation of membership or additional disciplinary action depending on the severity of the incident. Religious or political discussions are not permitted within the fitness center premises.

title: The campus has a Medical Center that is available to hostel residents at all times
applicable_campus: The campus
content: The campus has a Medical Center that is available to hostel residents at all times. The medical staff is on duty 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. The first medical consultation and the first dose of any medication are provided to students free of charge. No consultation fee is charged to hostel residents for visits to the medical center.

title: In the event of a medical emergency where the campus doctor recommends hospitalization, a transport facility is made ava
applicable_campus: In the event of a medical emergency where the campus
content: In the event of a medical emergency where the campus doctor recommends hospitalization, a transport facility is made available by the hostel administration to shift the student to a hospital. This emergency transport service is part of the campus's commitment to student welfare and is coordinated through the hostel administration and medical center staff.

title: Complaints related to hostel matters, other students, or hostel staff can be submitted in writing to the Warden
content: Complaints related to hostel matters, other students, or hostel staff can be submitted in writing to the Warden. Complaints can also be submitted through the online complaint management portal available at the address provided by the hostel management. All formal complaints are handled through the established hostel grievance process and are not entertained verbally.

title: The Hostel Management Committee, referred to as the HMC, is the governing body responsible for overseeing all hostel pol
content: The Hostel Management Committee, referred to as the HMC, is the governing body responsible for overseeing all hostel policies, disciplinary matters, and decisions related to accommodation, electricity usage, and facility management. The Warden and hostel management reserve the right to modify, suspend, or cancel any facility or permission at any time. Updated rules and regulations are communicated to residents as and when changes are made.

title: The hostel offers three types of room occupancy arrangements to students
content: The hostel offers three types of room occupancy arrangements to students. The standard shared room accommodates six students and is the most common room type available. A semi-private room option accommodates two students. A private single-occupancy room is also available for students who prefer to live alone. Each room type carries a different per-semester fee structure based on the number of occupants.

title: The per-semester accommodation fee for a six-person shared room is Rs 44,000
content: The per-semester accommodation fee for a six-person shared room is Rs 44,000. The per-semester accommodation fee for a two-person room is Rs 60,000. The per-semester accommodation fee for a single-occupancy private room is Rs 64,000. These semester fees cover the cost of accommodation only and do not include the security deposit, mess charges, or any utility charges for personal appliances such as air coolers, air conditioners, or refrigerators.

title: The security deposit for hostel admission is charged separately from the semester accommodation fee
content: The security deposit for hostel admission is charged separately from the semester accommodation fee. This security deposit is a one-time refundable payment made at the time of initial hostel admission and is not included in the per-semester room charges. Students must ensure both the security deposit and the semester accommodation fee are paid through the designated payment process before occupying their allocated room.

title: Each hostel room, regardless of the occupancy type, is furnished with standard furniture items for every occupant
content: Each hostel room, regardless of the occupancy type, is furnished with standard furniture items for every occupant. Each student in the room is provided with a single bed, a study table, a chair, and a personal cupboard for storing belongings. These furniture items are the property of the university and must be maintained in good condition throughout the student's stay. Any damage to the provided furniture is the financial responsibility of the room's occupants.

title: FAST-NUCES maintains separate merit lists for each test type used in the admission process
applicable_campus: Candidates who qualify through both routes should contact their preferred campus
content: FAST-NUCES maintains separate merit lists for each test type used in the admission process. A candidate who applies through both the NTS NAT and the FAST NU admission test is evaluated under each test category separately. While a candidate may apply through multiple routes by paying the relevant fees, admission is ultimately granted on the basis of one test result. Candidates who qualify through both routes should contact their preferred campus directly for the most current guidance on how selection is handled in overlapping cases.

title: The NTS NAT merit cutoff at FAST-NUCES tends to be higher than the cutoff for the FAST NU test
applicable_campus: equence of the smaller number of seats allocated to the NTS NAT category at each campus
content: The NTS NAT merit cutoff at FAST-NUCES tends to be higher than the cutoff for the FAST NU test. This is not a deliberate policy to discourage NTS applicants but is a consequence of the smaller number of seats allocated to the NTS NAT category at each campus. Because fewer seats are available for NAT applicants, the competition within that pool is more intense and the resulting cutoff is naturally higher. For competitive programs such as BS Computer Science, NTS NAT closing aggregates have historically reached between 80 and 85 percentile.

title: When a candidate applies to FAST-NUCES with multiple campus preferences, priority is given to the campus that the candid
applicable_campus: When a candidate applies to FAST-NUCES with multiple campus
content: When a candidate applies to FAST-NUCES with multiple campus preferences, priority is given to the campus that the candidate listed as their first preference. Candidates who listed a specific campus as their first preference are evaluated first for that campus's merit list. If a candidate does not qualify for their first preference campus, they are then considered for their second preference campus only if they meet that campus's merit requirements. First preference applicants at each campus are given priority over candidates who listed that campus as a lower preference.

title: A candidate cannot change their campus preference list or degree program preference after they have seen their FAST NU t
applicable_campus: A candidate cannot change their campus
applicable_program: didate cannot change their campus preference list or degree program
content: A candidate cannot change their campus preference list or degree program preference after they have seen their FAST NU test score. The preference list submitted at the time of application is final. Changes to campus or program preferences are not accepted after the test is completed and before the merit list is released. Candidates are therefore strongly advised to carefully finalize their preferences before submitting the application.

title: FAST-NUCES does not officially publish a waiting list in the traditional sense
applicable_campus: process is based on seat availability and is not guaranteed for every program or campus
content: FAST-NUCES does not officially publish a waiting list in the traditional sense. If seats become available after the first merit list, the university issues a second call for admission, commonly referred to among students as the second merit list or second cutoff. Candidates who fall just below the first cutoff may receive an email notification if their merit qualifies under the second cutoff. This process is based on seat availability and is not guaranteed for every program or campus.

title: If a candidate is selected for a program that was not their first preference, they are not required to pay the full admi
applicable_campus: should monitor their admissions portal and email for any notifications from the campus
applicable_program: If a candidate is selected for a program
content: If a candidate is selected for a program that was not their first preference, they are not required to pay the full admission fee simply to maintain eligibility for a higher-preference program if a second merit list is issued. Candidates in this situation should monitor their admissions portal and email for any notifications from the campus regarding subsequent merit list calls. Specific procedural guidance on this situation should be sought directly from the relevant campus admissions office.

title: If a candidate receives admission, pays the required fees, and subsequently receives their FSc Part 2 result showing les
content: If a candidate receives admission, pays the required fees, and subsequently receives their FSc Part 2 result showing less than the required minimum percentage, the university will cancel the admission. In such a case, a full refund of the tuition fee and other applicable charges is provided to the candidate. The admission test fee, however, is non-refundable under all circumstances. The admission fee and security deposit refund process follows HEC guidelines.

title: A-Level students who apply to FAST-NUCES before their final A-Level results are available can use their O-Level equivale
content: A-Level students who apply to FAST-NUCES before their final A-Level results are available can use their O-Level equivalence percentage as calculated by IBCC in the application form. The O-Level equivalence percentage serves as a provisional substitute for the HSSC result. Once the A-Level results are declared, the candidate must obtain the IBCC A-Level equivalence certificate and submit it within the time period specified by the university. This certificate is required to confirm that the candidate meets the minimum academic eligibility criteria for their chosen program. Failure to provide the A-Level equivalence certificate by the stated deadline will result in cancellation of admission.

title: Candidates who apply to FAST-NUCES from outside Pakistan and cannot appear in the NU test in person may apply using thei
applicable_program: he minimum SAT score required for Computing and Engineering programs
content: Candidates who apply to FAST-NUCES from outside Pakistan and cannot appear in the NU test in person may apply using their SAT scores. The minimum SAT score required for Computing and Engineering programs is 1200, and for BBA and Business programs it is 1000. SAT scores are treated as a separate merit category and candidates applying on this basis compete among other SAT applicants for the seats allocated to that category.

title: The FAST-NUCES test result is typically announced approximately one week after the completion of the testing window
content: The FAST-NUCES test result is typically announced approximately one week after the completion of the testing window. Candidates can check their result by logging into the admissions portal at admissions.nu.edu.pk. On the day of result announcement, the portal may be slower than usual due to high concurrent traffic from many users. Candidates who experience delays are advised to wait a few hours and try again. Results are also communicated via SMS and email to registered contacts.

title: After results are announced, FAST-NUCES publishes merit lists for each program and campus
applicable_campus: ter results are announced, FAST-NUCES publishes merit lists for each program and campus
applicable_program: FAST-NUCES publishes merit lists for each program
content: After results are announced, FAST-NUCES publishes merit lists for each program and campus. Candidates who are selected are informed via SMS and email and are given a deadline by which they must confirm their seat by paying the required fees and submitting the necessary documents. Missing the confirmation deadline results in automatic cancellation of the offered seat with no possibility of reinstatement.

title: A candidate who is selected for admission at FAST-NUCES and confirms their seat but later decides to withdraw before cla
content: A candidate who is selected for admission at FAST-NUCES and confirms their seat but later decides to withdraw before classes begin may apply for a fee refund. The refund amount depends on the timing of the withdrawal and is governed by the Higher Education Commission refund policy. The one-time non-refundable admission fee of Rs 30,000 is not refunded under any circumstances regardless of when the withdrawal is made. The security deposit and tuition fee refund eligibility depend on the specific withdrawal date relative to the semester start date.

title: Students who have questions about specific admission scenarios, unusual circumstances, or edge cases that are not clearl
applicable_campus: rsity website are encouraged to contact the admissions office of their preferred campus
content: Students who have questions about specific admission scenarios, unusual circumstances, or edge cases that are not clearly answered by the information published on the university website are encouraged to contact the admissions office of their preferred campus directly. The university helpline is reachable at 111-128-128 and the general admissions email is admissions@nu.edu.pk. Campus-specific email addresses are also listed on each campus website for direct inquiries.

title: FAST-NUCES does not release official closing aggregate data or merit list cutoffs publicly on its website or official so
applicable_campus: Students who need historical closing merit information for a specific campus
content: FAST-NUCES does not release official closing aggregate data or merit list cutoffs publicly on its website or official social media platforms. Students who need historical closing merit information for a specific campus and program can contact the relevant campus admissions office, which will provide this information upon request. Unofficial closing merit data available on student forums or third-party websites should be treated as approximate and may not reflect the actual cutoffs for any given year.

title: The FAST admission test is the same for BS Computer Science and BS Software Engineering programs
applicable_program: he same for BS Computer Science and BS Software Engineering programs
content: The FAST admission test is the same for BS Computer Science and BS Software Engineering programs. The test pattern for all computing programs and all engineering programs is identical, covering English, Analytical Skills and IQ, Basic Mathematics, and Advanced Mathematics sections. The BBA, BS Accounting and Finance, BS Business Analytics, and BS FinTech test pattern is different and replaces the Advanced Mathematics section with an Essay Writing section. Candidates must select the correct test type corresponding to their desired program at the time of registration.

title: A candidate who applies to FAST-NUCES and is not selected for their chosen program in the first merit list is not automa
applicable_campus: am in the first merit list is not automatically redirected to another program or campus
applicable_program: applies to FAST-NUCES and is not selected for their chosen program
content: A candidate who applies to FAST-NUCES and is not selected for their chosen program in the first merit list is not automatically redirected to another program or campus. Each program and campus has its own independent merit list. A candidate who is not selected for BS Computer Science is not automatically placed on the merit list for BBA or BS Electrical Engineering. If the candidate wishes to be considered for multiple programs, they must have indicated this during the application process and paid the corresponding additional fees.

title: A candidate who wishes to apply to multiple campuses of FAST-NUCES must pay an additional fee of Rs 500 per additional c
applicable_campus: A candidate who wishes to apply to multiple campuses
content: A candidate who wishes to apply to multiple campuses of FAST-NUCES must pay an additional fee of Rs 500 per additional campus at the time of application. The single application form and test result apply across all selected campuses, but the candidate is evaluated separately against each campus's merit list. Candidates should not assume that clearing the merit for one campus automatically qualifies them for another.

title: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences, commonly known as FAST-NUCES or FAST-NU, is a federally char
applicable_campus: It was established in 2000 as the first multi-campus
content: FAST National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences, commonly known as FAST-NUCES or FAST-NU, is a federally chartered private research university in Pakistan. It was established in 2000 as the first multi-campus private university in the country. The university is sponsored by the Foundation for Advancement of Science and Technology, which has been a registered charitable body under the Government of Pakistan since 1980.

title: FAST-NUCES operates campuses in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Chiniot-Faisalabad, and Multan
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES operates campuses
content: FAST-NUCES operates campuses in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Chiniot-Faisalabad, and Multan. Each campus follows the same admission policies, academic regulations, and degree structures established centrally. The university serves over 11,000 students and is supported by a faculty of more than 500 members across all campuses.

title: The university is consistently ranked among the top institutions in Pakistan for computing, engineering, and business by
content: The university is consistently ranked among the top institutions in Pakistan for computing, engineering, and business by the Higher Education Commission of Pakistan. In the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2025, FAST-NUCES was ranked in the 351 to 400 band globally, making it one of the few Pakistani universities with an internationally recognized ranking in technology disciplines.

title: FAST-NUCES accepts undergraduate applications only during the Fall semester
applicable_campus: There is no Spring or annual intake for undergraduate programs at any campus
applicable_program: There is no Spring or annual intake for undergraduate programs
content: FAST-NUCES accepts undergraduate applications only during the Fall semester. There is no Spring or annual intake for undergraduate programs at any campus. Students who miss the Fall admission cycle must wait until the following year to apply. This policy is consistent across all campuses and all undergraduate degree programs offered by the university.

title: The admissions portal for FAST-NUCES is available at admissions
content: The admissions portal for FAST-NUCES is available at admissions.nu.edu.pk. All applications must be submitted through this online portal. The university does not accept applications by post, courier, or in-person submission. Applicants must create an account on the portal, enter personal and academic information accurately, and complete all steps of the application process before the announced deadline.

title: After completing the online registration, every applicant is automatically assigned a unique Application Reference Numbe
content: After completing the online registration, every applicant is automatically assigned a unique Application Reference Number by the admissions portal. This number is known as the ARN. The ARN must be retained by the applicant for all future inquiries, correspondence, and status checks related to their application. It cannot be retrieved if lost without contacting the admissions office directly.

title: The application fee for the FAST-NUCES admission test is non-refundable and non-adjustable under all circumstances
content: The application fee for the FAST-NUCES admission test is non-refundable and non-adjustable under all circumstances. This fee must be paid by depositing it at the designated bank before the applicant can print their admit card. The application fee is required from all applicants, including those applying on the basis of NTS NAT scores. Without payment confirmation, the admit card cannot be generated.

title: FAST-NUCES also provides an online payment facility through the Kuickpay payment gateway for paying the application fee
content: FAST-NUCES also provides an online payment facility through the Kuickpay payment gateway for paying the application fee and tuition fees. Students can use internet banking, mobile banking apps, or ATM machines from supported banks including Allied Bank, Askari Bank, Bank Al Habib, Bank Alfalah, Bank Islami, Bank of Punjab, Faysal Bank, HBL, MCB, Meezan Bank, National Bank, and UBL, among others. EasyPaisa and JazzCash can also be used for payments through Kuickpay. To complete the payment, the applicant must select Bill Payment from their banking platform, choose Kuickpay, enter the voucher number along with the Institution ID prefix as shown on the challan, and confirm the payment.

title: Once the application fee is confirmed by the bank, the applicant can log back into the portal and print their admit card
applicable_campus: s test roll number, the assigned test campus
content: Once the application fee is confirmed by the bank, the applicant can log back into the portal and print their admit card. The admit card displays the applicant's test roll number, the assigned test campus, and the scheduled date and time for the admission test. Applicants must carefully verify all details on the admit card. If any information is incorrect, the admissions office must be contacted immediately for correction.

title: Applicants are required to bring only their endorsed admit card to the test venue on the day of the examination
content: Applicants are required to bring only their endorsed admit card to the test venue on the day of the examination. No other documents, including CNIC, result cards, or any academic certificates, need to be brought to the test centre on test day. Mobile phones, calculators, smartwatches, electronic devices, and bags are not allowed inside the examination hall under any circumstances.

title: Latecomers are not permitted to enter the examination hall after the test has begun
content: Latecomers are not permitted to enter the examination hall after the test has begun. The admit card specifies both the reporting time and the start time of the test. It is the responsibility of the applicant to arrive at the correct test centre at the stated time. The university does not make exceptions for late arrivals regardless of the reason, unless a prior formal request has been accepted by the administration.

title: If an applicant is unable to appear for the admission test on the assigned date due to an urgent reason, they may reques
content: If an applicant is unable to appear for the admission test on the assigned date due to an urgent reason, they may request a rescheduling by submitting authentic proof of the reason before the deadline. The management will accommodate the request only if it is submitted with valid supporting documentation and if the deadline has not passed. Not all rescheduling requests are guaranteed to be approved.

title: The FAST-NUCES admission test is a computer-based test conducted at specialized computer laboratories at each campus sim
applicable_campus: is a computer-based test conducted at specialized computer laboratories at each campus
content: The FAST-NUCES admission test is a computer-based test conducted at specialized computer laboratories at each campus simultaneously. Each candidate is assigned an individual computer terminal for the duration of the test. The entire test takes approximately two to three hours to complete, with each section having its own individual timer. Once a section's timer runs out, the system automatically advances the candidate to the next section and does not allow them to return to the previous section.

title: The FAST admission test for BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Artificial Intelligence, BS Electrical Engi
content: The FAST admission test for BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Artificial Intelligence, BS Electrical Engineering, and other engineering and computing programs consists of four sections: English, Analytical Skills and IQ, Basic Mathematics, and Advanced Mathematics. The test is entirely multiple-choice in format and carries a total of 100 marks. Calculators are not allowed during the test.

title: The English section of the FAST admission test covers grammar, reading comprehension, vocabulary, and analogy-based ques
content: The English section of the FAST admission test covers grammar, reading comprehension, vocabulary, and analogy-based questions. The Analytical Skills and IQ section covers scenario-based reasoning, statement-based questions, number sequences, spatial patterns, and direction-based problems. The Basic Mathematics section covers quantitative reasoning and word problems from arithmetic, basic algebra, percentages, ratios, cost and profit calculations. The Advanced Mathematics section covers matrices, algebra, trigonometry, permutation and combination, probability, analytic geometry, integration, differentiation, and other topics from FSc Part 1 and Part 2 mathematics.

title: The FAST admission test for BBA, BS Accounting and Finance, BS Business Analytics, and BS FinTech programs has a differe
content: The FAST admission test for BBA, BS Accounting and Finance, BS Business Analytics, and BS FinTech programs has a different structure from the engineering and computing test. The business program test includes English, Essay Writing, Analytical Skills and IQ, and Basic Mathematics sections. The Advanced Mathematics section is replaced by Essay Writing in the business program test. Candidates applying for business programs are assessed on their written communication ability in addition to verbal and quantitative reasoning.

title: The negative marking rule in the FAST admission test is applied differently for different sections
content: The negative marking rule in the FAST admission test is applied differently for different sections. For the Analytical Skills and IQ, Basic Mathematics, and Advanced Mathematics sections, one mark is deducted for every four incorrect answers, meaning each wrong answer carries a penalty of 0.25 marks. For the English section, one mark is deducted for every twelve incorrect answers, meaning each wrong answer carries a lighter penalty of approximately 0.083 marks. Unanswered questions receive zero marks and incur no penalty.

title: The total marks of the FAST admission test is 100
content: The total marks of the FAST admission test is 100. The Advanced Mathematics section carries the highest weightage in the computing and engineering test, accounting for approximately 50 percent of the total marks. The relative weights of other sections vary. Applicants are advised to refer to the official test pattern page at nu.edu.pk for the most current section-wise breakdown, as this information is subject to update.

title: FAST-NUCES test results are typically announced approximately one week after the completion of the examination
applicable_campus: dmitted candidates also receive an SMS and email notification from the concerned campus
content: FAST-NUCES test results are typically announced approximately one week after the completion of the examination. Results can be checked by logging into the admissions portal at admissions.nu.edu.pk. On the day of result announcement, the portal may experience slow loading due to high traffic from concurrent users. Candidates are advised to retry after a few hours if the portal is not immediately accessible. Admitted candidates also receive an SMS and email notification from the concerned campus.

title: The merit formula used for calculating a candidate's final aggregate for undergraduate programs at FAST-NUCES assigns 50
applicable_campus: ely on this calculated aggregate, and the final merit lists for each program and campus
applicable_program: s final aggregate for undergraduate programs
content: The merit formula used for calculating a candidate's final aggregate for undergraduate programs at FAST-NUCES assigns 50 percent weightage to HSSC or equivalent marks and 50 percent weightage to the score obtained in the FAST admission test. There is no interview component in the undergraduate admissions process. Selection is based entirely on this calculated aggregate, and the final merit lists for each program and campus are generated accordingly.

title: For candidates applying through the NTS NAT route, the selection aggregate is calculated similarly, with 50 percent from
content: For candidates applying through the NTS NAT route, the selection aggregate is calculated similarly, with 50 percent from HSSC marks and 50 percent from the NTS NAT score. Candidates applying through SAT also follow the same 50 to 50 formula, with their SAT score substituting the admission test score. FAST-NUCES prepares separate merit lists for each test category, meaning FAST test applicants compete among themselves and NTS NAT or SAT applicants have their own separate merit lists.

title: If a candidate's HSSC result is not available at the time of merit list compilation, the marks from Matric are used as a
content: If a candidate's HSSC result is not available at the time of merit list compilation, the marks from Matric are used as a substitute and multiplied by a factor of 0.9 to estimate an equivalent HSSC score. This substitution is only temporary. If the candidate is admitted on this basis and subsequently fails to pass the required examination, their admission is automatically cancelled. No fee refund is provided in such cases.

title: A score of approximately 70 percent in the FAST admission test is generally considered a safe benchmark for securing adm
applicable_campus: Closing aggregates across programs and campuses
content: A score of approximately 70 percent in the FAST admission test is generally considered a safe benchmark for securing admission. Closing aggregates across programs and campuses typically fall between 55 and 70 percent depending on the program. For the NTS NAT route, closing aggregates for more competitive programs such as Computer Science can reach between 80 and 85 percentile. These figures are indicative trends based on previous years and may vary each cycle.

title: FAST-NUCES has an approximate acceptance rate of 13 percent, making it one of the more selective private universities in
applicable_campus: Many applicants receive admission offers but may not secure their first-choice campus
applicable_program: sion offers but may not secure their first-choice campus or program
content: FAST-NUCES has an approximate acceptance rate of 13 percent, making it one of the more selective private universities in Pakistan. Thousands of students apply each year, and only those who score highly in the admission test and hold strong academic records from SSC and HSSC can secure seats. Many applicants receive admission offers but may not secure their first-choice campus or program due to competition.

title: Separate merit lists are maintained for each campus and each program at FAST-NUCES
applicable_campus: Separate merit lists are maintained for each campus
applicable_program: eparate merit lists are maintained for each campus and each program
content: Separate merit lists are maintained for each campus and each program at FAST-NUCES. Selection at one campus does not automatically qualify a candidate for another campus. A candidate who applies to multiple campuses must pay an additional fee of Rs 500 per additional campus beyond the primary campus selection at the time of application. Results for each campus are provided separately in the admissions portal.

title: A candidate who is not selected for one program, such as BS Computer Science, is not automatically considered for anothe
applicable_campus: sidered for another program such as BBA or BS Electrical Engineering at the same campus
applicable_program: A candidate who is not selected for one program
content: A candidate who is not selected for one program, such as BS Computer Science, is not automatically considered for another program such as BBA or BS Electrical Engineering at the same campus. Each program has a separate merit list and competition pool. A student wishing to be considered for multiple programs must apply separately and pay the relevant fees for each additional application.

title: For Engineering programs such as BS Electrical Engineering, BS Civil Engineering, and BS Mechanical Engineering, candida
content: For Engineering programs such as BS Electrical Engineering, BS Civil Engineering, and BS Mechanical Engineering, candidates must have a minimum of 60 percent marks in SSC or Matric and a minimum of 60 percent marks in FSc Pre-Engineering with the subjects of Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry, or ICS with Mathematics, Physics, and Computer Science.

title: For Computing programs such as BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Artificial Intelligence, BS Data Science
content: For Computing programs such as BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Artificial Intelligence, BS Data Science, and BS Cyber Security, candidates must have a minimum of 60 percent in SSC and at least 50 percent in FSc Pre-Engineering, ICS, or an equivalent qualification. The compulsory subject condition is that the candidate must have studied Mathematics in their intermediate or equivalent program.

title: For BBA, BS Accounting and Finance, BS Business Analytics, and BS FinTech programs, candidates must have a minimum of 60
content: For BBA, BS Accounting and Finance, BS Business Analytics, and BS FinTech programs, candidates must have a minimum of 60 percent in SSC and at least 50 percent in FSc or ICS or an equivalent qualification. Students from all intermediate groups, including FA, FSc, ICS, and commerce backgrounds, are eligible to apply for business programs provided they meet the minimum percentage requirements.

title: Pre-medical students, meaning those who studied FSc with Biology, Physics, and Chemistry without Mathematics, are genera
applicable_program: are generally not eligible for Engineering or Computing programs
content: Pre-medical students, meaning those who studied FSc with Biology, Physics, and Chemistry without Mathematics, are generally not eligible for Engineering or Computing programs at FAST-NUCES. However, pre-medical students who have passed an additional Mathematics examination or are awaiting results of such an exam are eligible to apply for Computing programs. For Engineering programs, the Mathematics prerequisite is strictly required with no exceptions. Pre-medical students without additional Mathematics can only apply for BBA and business-related programs.

title: Students who have taken O Levels and A Levels can apply to FAST-NUCES using their academic credentials
content: Students who have taken O Levels and A Levels can apply to FAST-NUCES using their academic credentials. For O Level students, the equivalent marks for SSC are calculated by IBCC using the grades of eight standard subjects including Chemistry, English, Islamiat, Mathematics, Pakistan Studies, Physics, Urdu, and either Biology or Computer Studies. The IBCC calculates the equivalent percentage out of 850 marks based on this formula.

title: For A Level students applying to FAST-NUCES, the marks are equated to HSSC marks using the IBCC equivalence system
content: For A Level students applying to FAST-NUCES, the marks are equated to HSSC marks using the IBCC equivalence system. The total marks used for A Level equivalence calculation are based on the same 800-mark formula applied to IBCC-standardized subject scores. Students awaiting their A Level results may enter their O Level equivalence percentage in the application form at the time of applying. They must submit the A Level equivalence certificate from IBCC within one week of the A Level results being declared.

title: FAST-NUCES accepts the IBCC equivalence certificate as the official document for O Level and A Level qualifications
content: FAST-NUCES accepts the IBCC equivalence certificate as the official document for O Level and A Level qualifications. The university does not independently compute equivalence for foreign qualifications. All students with non-Pakistani academic backgrounds, including International Baccalaureate, American High School Diploma, and Cambridge qualifications, must obtain IBCC equivalence certificates before or at the time of admission confirmation.

title: Foreign students who wish to apply to FAST-NUCES and are unable to physically appear for the admission test due to resid
content: Foreign students who wish to apply to FAST-NUCES and are unable to physically appear for the admission test due to residing abroad may apply using valid SAT scores. The minimum SAT score required for admission to BBA, BS Accounting and Finance, BS Business Analytics, and BS FinTech programs is 1000. For all Computing and Engineering programs, the minimum SAT score required is 1200. These are minimum eligibility thresholds, not guarantees of admission.

title: Foreign students who require a visa to travel to Pakistan for the admission test should apply as early as possible and i
applicable_campus: the admission test should apply as early as possible and immediately inform the campus
content: Foreign students who require a visa to travel to Pakistan for the admission test should apply as early as possible and immediately inform the campus administration about their travel requirements. The university will provide a support letter that can be submitted to the Pakistan embassy or consulate in the student's country. If the student receives their visa after the scheduled test date due to processing delays, a substitute test may be arranged as a special case. For such requests, the email address to contact is intoffice@nu.edu.pk.

title: Foreign nationals who are applying through the FAST-NUCES online portal and encounter the CNIC field requirement can reg
content: Foreign nationals who are applying through the FAST-NUCES online portal and encounter the CNIC field requirement can register using their passport number instead of a CNIC. Applicants from Afghanistan may use their Proof of Registration number in place of a CNIC. For further guidance on foreign student applications, the email address intoffice@nu.edu.pk is the designated contact point.

title: The documents that applicants must have ready when submitting the online admission form include attested copies of the M
content: The documents that applicants must have ready when submitting the online admission form include attested copies of the Matric or SSC result, attested copies of the FSc or HSSC result or equivalent, a copy of the CNIC or B-Form, passport-size photographs, and an IBCC equivalence certificate if the applicant has O Level, A Level, or any other foreign academic qualification. For MS program applicants, a transcript of the relevant bachelor's degree must also be provided. All document images must be uploaded in JPG format and must be below 1 MB in size.

title: If an applicant uploads incorrect documents during the online application process, they should email the concerned campu
applicable_campus: documents during the online application process, they should email the concerned campus
content: If an applicant uploads incorrect documents during the online application process, they should email the concerned campus admissions office directly with the corrected documents. The admissions team will process the update manually. The relevant campus email addresses are listed on the official FAST-NUCES website. For the Chiniot-Faisalabad campus specifically, the admissions email is admissions.cfd@nu.edu.pk. For general admissions support, the email is admissions@nu.edu.pk.

title: After submitting the fee and completing the admission test, selected candidates are notified via SMS on their registered
content: After submitting the fee and completing the admission test, selected candidates are notified via SMS on their registered mobile number and via email. Selected candidates must confirm their seat by paying the required admission fee and security deposit within the specified deadline. Failure to confirm the seat within the given time results in automatic cancellation. No extension is normally granted for this confirmation deadline.

title: The one-time non-refundable admission fee at FAST-NUCES is Rs 30,000
content: The one-time non-refundable admission fee at FAST-NUCES is Rs 30,000. This fee is charged at the time of initial enrollment and is not refunded under any circumstances, including withdrawal or cancellation. Students who are already graduates of FAST-NUCES and are admitted to a higher degree program at the same university are not required to pay the admission fee again.

title: The security deposit at FAST-NUCES is Rs 20,000 and is a refundable amount held by the university throughout the student
content: The security deposit at FAST-NUCES is Rs 20,000 and is a refundable amount held by the university throughout the student's enrollment. This deposit is returned upon completion of the degree or upon official closure of the student's admission. It is not refunded during enrollment except under the official HEC refund policy guidelines.

title: The tuition fee at FAST-NUCES is charged on a per-credit-hour basis and is currently set at Rs 10,000 per credit hour fo
content: The tuition fee at FAST-NUCES is charged on a per-credit-hour basis and is currently set at Rs 10,000 per credit hour for the academic year 2025 to 2026. A standard semester with five three-credit-hour courses totals approximately Rs 150,000 in tuition. A semester with six courses would amount to approximately Rs 180,000. The Student Activities Fund contribution is Rs 2,500 per semester as of the Fall 2024 semester onward.

title: Tuition fee is payable in full before the start of each semester
content: Tuition fee is payable in full before the start of each semester. Late payment attracts a fine charged at the rate of Rs 1 per day for every Rs 1,000 of outstanding fee. If the fee remains unpaid for two months after the due date, the student's course registration is suspended. The fee structure is reviewed and may be revised every academic year.

title: Tuition fees at FAST-NUCES are refunded in accordance with the Higher Education Commission refund guidelines in cases wh
content: Tuition fees at FAST-NUCES are refunded in accordance with the Higher Education Commission refund guidelines in cases where a student withdraws after enrollment. These HEC refund rules were revised and made applicable from December 20, 2024. The amount refunded depends on the timing of the withdrawal relative to the start of the semester. Students are advised to review the HEC refund policy and the FAST fee structure page for the current applicable rules.

title: The NU Aid program at FAST-NUCES provides need-based financial assistance to students in the form of interest-free loans
content: The NU Aid program at FAST-NUCES provides need-based financial assistance to students in the form of interest-free loans. Students may apply for NU Aid at the time of admission or within one to two weeks after the start of the first semester. Applications are assessed through an interview process, after which the financial aid amount is determined based on financial need and academic merit. The aid covers 25, 50, 75, or 100 percent of the tuition fee depending on the assessed need.

title: Once NU Aid is granted, the loan repayment begins three months after graduation or three months after the student secure
content: Once NU Aid is granted, the loan repayment begins three months after graduation or three months after the student secures employment, whichever comes earlier. The full loan amount must be repaid within four years. Students acknowledge this repayment commitment at the time the loan is granted. The NU Aid financial assistance covers tuition fees only and does not cover the admission fee, security deposit, or student activities fund.

title: The FAST-NUCES admission test is conducted at all campuses simultaneously during the announced testing window
applicable_campus: The FAST-NUCES admission test is conducted at all campuses
content: The FAST-NUCES admission test is conducted at all campuses simultaneously during the announced testing window. Applicants who prefer to appear at a campus other than their primary campus of choice may appear for the test at any FAST campus. For example, a student who has applied to the Karachi campus but is currently residing in Lahore may appear for the test at the Lahore campus. The test result is the same regardless of which campus it was taken at.

title: Applicants who are selected for admission at a particular FAST campus but wish to study at a different campus may reques
applicable_campus: Applicants who are selected for admission at a particular FAST campus
content: Applicants who are selected for admission at a particular FAST campus but wish to study at a different campus may request a transfer after completing at least two semesters at the original campus. The transfer requires the student to reappear in the FAST entry test and qualify on the merit of the desired destination campus. All passed courses must carry a grade of C or above. Any course with a grade below C must be repeated at the new campus after transfer.

title: A student currently studying at another university who wishes to transfer to FAST-NUCES must apply as a new student and
content: A student currently studying at another university who wishes to transfer to FAST-NUCES must apply as a new student and go through the standard admission process. After gaining admission, the student may apply for credit exemption for relevant previously completed courses. The Head of Department reviews the transcript and recommends compatible courses for exemption, subject to final approval by the Dean of Faculty. Credit exemption cannot exceed 50 percent of the total credit hours of the FAST degree program.

title: Candidates who have been admitted to a PhD program at another university and wish to transfer to FAST-NUCES should apply
applicable_campus: They should inform the campus
applicable_program: Candidates who have been admitted to a PhD program
content: Candidates who have been admitted to a PhD program at another university and wish to transfer to FAST-NUCES should apply as a regular PhD student. They should inform the campus about their intent to transfer credits. The graduate studies committee evaluates the case. As per HEC rules, a maximum of 50 percent of PhD credits are transferable, and research credits earned at the previous institution are not recognized. The student joins FAST-NUCES in the coursework phase of the PhD program.

title: The NTS NAT test accepted at FAST-NUCES for Engineering programs is the NAT-IE
applicable_program: The NTS NAT test accepted at FAST-NUCES for Engineering programs
content: The NTS NAT test accepted at FAST-NUCES for Engineering programs is the NAT-IE. For Computing and Management Sciences programs, both NAT-IE and NAT-ICS are acceptable. There are no minimum NTS NAT marks that guarantee admission. The cut-off score is determined by FAST-NUCES based on the number of applicants and available seats each semester. Applicants must check the official eligibility criteria page for current NTS NAT cut-off information.

title: Candidates who have completed their second year of Intermediate studies and wish to apply using only their first-year ma
content: Candidates who have completed their second year of Intermediate studies and wish to apply using only their first-year marks should note that FAST-NUCES applies the highest available academic result to calculate merit. The merit list uses either the first year or the final year result, whichever is more favorable and available at the time of compilation. Students should apply with the best and most current academic record available to them.

title: The test for MS programs at FAST-NUCES differs in structure from the undergraduate admission test
content: The test for MS programs at FAST-NUCES differs in structure from the undergraduate admission test. The MS admission test covers subject-specific content relevant to the program being applied to, along with quantitative and verbal reasoning. A minimum score of 60 percent in the MS admission test is generally required to be considered for the program. Deficiency courses may also be assigned to candidates who lack prerequisite knowledge in certain core undergraduate subjects.

title: The GAT General test from NTS is the standard requirement for MS admissions in line with HEC policy
content: The GAT General test from NTS is the standard requirement for MS admissions in line with HEC policy. For PhD admissions, the NTS GAT Subject test at the relevant discipline is required, and the candidate must score at least 50 percentile in that test with a minimum of 60 percent marks. GAT scores must be valid and current at the time of application submission.

title: FAST-NUCES does not conduct interviews for undergraduate program admissions under any circumstances
applicable_campus: This process applies uniformly to all programs and all campuses
applicable_program: FAST-NUCES does not conduct interviews for undergraduate program
content: FAST-NUCES does not conduct interviews for undergraduate program admissions under any circumstances. The entire undergraduate selection process is based on the merit aggregate calculated from HSSC marks and the admission test score. This process applies uniformly to all programs and all campuses.

title: The test pattern information and sample test materials for the FAST admission test are available on the official univers
content: The test pattern information and sample test materials for the FAST admission test are available on the official university website at nu.edu.pk under the Admissions section. The Test Pattern page provides a breakdown of sections, question types, marking scheme, and time allocations for each program category. Candidates are strongly encouraged to review this page thoroughly before preparing for the test.

title: Practicing past papers from previous years is one of the most effective ways to prepare for the FAST admission test
content: Practicing past papers from previous years is one of the most effective ways to prepare for the FAST admission test. These papers help candidates understand the difficulty level, question types, and time pressure of the actual test. Several online platforms offer FAST-specific preparation materials, topic-wise MCQs, mock tests, and diagnostic assessments. The official FAST-NUCES website also provides sample tests that reflect the actual test format.

title: For the Advanced Mathematics section of the FAST engineering and computing test, candidates should focus on topics from
content: For the Advanced Mathematics section of the FAST engineering and computing test, candidates should focus on topics from FSc Part 1 and Part 2 mathematics. Key areas include matrices and determinants, quadratic equations, sequences and series, complex numbers, trigonometric identities and equations, permutation and combination, probability, analytic geometry, vectors, and calculus including integration and differentiation. These topics carry the highest weightage and require the most preparation time.

title: The Analytical Skills and IQ section of the FAST test does not follow a specific syllabus
content: The Analytical Skills and IQ section of the FAST test does not follow a specific syllabus. It tests the candidate's logical reasoning, pattern recognition, and analytical thinking ability. Regular practice with IQ question sets, sequence problems, and logical puzzles significantly improves performance in this section. Once the underlying pattern of a question type is identified, the correct answer can typically be found quickly.

title: The Basic Mathematics section of the FAST test covers arithmetic fundamentals, percentages, ratios and proportions, prof
content: The Basic Mathematics section of the FAST test covers arithmetic fundamentals, percentages, ratios and proportions, profit and loss, cost and price problems, simple and compound interest, and basic algebraic word problems. These questions are more straightforward than the Advanced Mathematics section and can be answered with strong foundational math skills.

title: The English section of the FAST test covers grammar rules, reading comprehension passages, vocabulary in context, and an
applicable_program: For the BBA and business program
content: The English section of the FAST test covers grammar rules, reading comprehension passages, vocabulary in context, and analogy-based questions. For the BBA and business program test, an Essay Writing section is also included. A well-structured essay with complex sentence constructions, varied vocabulary, and minimal grammatical errors is expected by the test evaluators. Practicing timed essay writing on general topics helps candidates improve their performance in this section.

title: The working hours of FAST-NUCES campus admissions offices are generally Monday to Friday from 08:00 to 16:00
applicable_campus: The working hours of FAST-NUCES campus
content: The working hours of FAST-NUCES campus admissions offices are generally Monday to Friday from 08:00 to 16:00. For enquiries outside business hours, candidates are advised to send an email to the relevant campus admissions address. The central helpline number for all FAST-NUCES campuses is 111-128-128. The Peshawar campus can also be reached at 091-111-128-128 and the Chiniot-Faisalabad campus at 041-111-128-128.

title: After a student pays their admission confirmation fee but has not yet received a roll number or further campus communica
applicable_campus: eir admission confirmation fee but has not yet received a roll number or further campus
content: After a student pays their admission confirmation fee but has not yet received a roll number or further campus communication, the delay may be due to the time required for the admissions office to verify and validate the submitted documents of each selected candidate. Students in this situation should contact the campus helpline directly to follow up on the status of their documentation verification.

title: The FAST-NUCES admission process for each application includes a verification step where all submitted documents and aca
content: The FAST-NUCES admission process for each application includes a verification step where all submitted documents and academic credentials are checked for authenticity. Any document found to be forged, altered, or misrepresented at any stage of the process results in immediate disqualification or cancellation of admission without any fee refund. The university also reserves the right to take legal action in such cases.

title: Students who confirm their seat at FAST-NUCES and then later wish to cancel their admission must formally inform the uni
applicable_campus: cancel their admission must formally inform the university through the relevant campus
content: Students who confirm their seat at FAST-NUCES and then later wish to cancel their admission must formally inform the university through the relevant campus. The refund of fees, if applicable, is governed by the HEC refund policy effective from December 20, 2024. The admission fee of Rs 30,000 is non-refundable under any circumstance. The security deposit of Rs 20,000 is refundable at the time of degree completion or official admission closure.

title: Students at FAST-NUCES who are admitted to an undergraduate program and later wish to change to a different degree progr
applicable_program: Students at FAST-NUCES who are admitted to an undergraduate program
content: Students at FAST-NUCES who are admitted to an undergraduate program and later wish to change to a different degree program within the university must reappear in the FAST entry test for the new program and qualify based on the merit of that program. The student is treated as a new enrollee in the new program. Their CGPA from the previous program is not carried over. Credits from courses passed with a grade of C or above may be transferred if the courses are relevant to the new program.

title: FAST-NUCES offers a broad range of undergraduate programs across its campuses
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES offers a broad range of undergraduate programs across its campuses
applicable_program: FAST-NUCES offers a broad range of undergraduate programs
content: FAST-NUCES offers a broad range of undergraduate programs across its campuses. These include BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Artificial Intelligence, BS Data Science, BS Cyber Security, BS Electrical Engineering, BS Civil Engineering, BS Mechanical Engineering, BS Robotics and Intelligent Systems, BBA, BS Accounting and Finance, BS Business Analytics, and BS FinTech. The programs available at each campus vary. BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, and BS Robotics are offered at all campuses.

title: All undergraduate programs at FAST-NUCES are structured as four-year, eight-semester programs totaling approximately 130
content: All undergraduate programs at FAST-NUCES are structured as four-year, eight-semester programs totaling approximately 130 credit hours. Each semester typically consists of five to six courses. The curriculum combines theoretical coursework with laboratory work, software or engineering projects, and a mandatory Final Year Project carried out in the seventh and eighth semesters. Industry internships are also an integral part of the undergraduate experience.

title: The career prospects for FAST-NUCES graduates are wide-ranging due to the university's strong industry linkages
content: The career prospects for FAST-NUCES graduates are wide-ranging due to the university's strong industry linkages. Computer Science and Software Engineering graduates are employed in software houses, technology companies, banking sectors, telecommunications, and government institutions. Engineering graduates work in energy, construction, electronics, and manufacturing industries. BBA and finance graduates enter banking, consulting, retail, and corporate management. FAST-NUCES alumni are also widely employed in technology roles internationally, particularly in the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, and Gulf countries.

title: FAST-NUCES offers graduate and doctoral programs at the MS and PhD level in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Elec
applicable_program: FAST-NUCES offers graduate and doctoral programs
content: FAST-NUCES offers graduate and doctoral programs at the MS and PhD level in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Data Science, Cyber Security, Management Sciences, and related fields. These programs are designed for students who hold a relevant bachelor's degree and wish to specialize further or conduct original research. PhD programs require the completion of coursework, a qualifying examination, and an original research dissertation that contributes new knowledge to the field.

title: The PhD program at FAST-NUCES includes a mandatory research publication requirement as a condition for degree completion
applicable_campus: The specific requirements may vary slightly by discipline and campus
content: The PhD program at FAST-NUCES includes a mandatory research publication requirement as a condition for degree completion. Candidates must publish at least one research article in an HEC-approved W-category journal, or two research articles in HEC-approved X-category journals, before the degree is awarded. These publication requirements have been in effect from March 1, 2025. The specific requirements may vary slightly by discipline and campus.

title: For students interested in joining FAST-NUCES as a teaching or research assistant, opportunities exist at the graduate l
content: For students interested in joining FAST-NUCES as a teaching or research assistant, opportunities exist at the graduate level. PhD students may be eligible for research assistantships that provide financial support in exchange for participation in research activities. Graduate students may also qualify for HEC Indigenous Fellowships, which provide a monthly stipend and full tuition fee coverage for the duration of the PhD program.

title: The official FAST-NUCES website for admissions is admissions
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
content: The official FAST-NUCES website for admissions is admissions.nu.edu.pk. The main university website is nu.edu.pk. The Islamabad campus website is isb.nu.edu.pk and the Peshawar campus website is pwr.nu.edu.pk. Test pattern information is available at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/TestPattern. Degree program listings are available at nu.edu.pk/Degree-Programs. Eligibility criteria are published at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/EligibilityCriteria. The fee structure is available at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/FeeStructure. The official admissions FAQ is published at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/FAQ.

title: FAST-NUCES does not publish official closing merit aggregates or merit list cut-offs on its website or official social m
applicable_campus: Students who wish to know the closing aggregate for a specific program and campus
content: FAST-NUCES does not publish official closing merit aggregates or merit list cut-offs on its website or official social media platforms. Students who wish to know the closing aggregate for a specific program and campus in a given year are advised to contact the relevant campus admissions office directly. The campus will share this information upon request. Unofficial closing merit data shared on third-party websites or student forums may not be accurate and should not be relied upon for planning purposes.

title: For the Karachi campus of FAST-NUCES, there is a Main Campus and a City Campus
applicable_campus: For the Karachi campus
applicable_program: PM to accommodate students enrolled in evening programs
content: For the Karachi campus of FAST-NUCES, there is a Main Campus and a City Campus. The Main Campus is the primary academic facility. The City Campus is located in PECHS Block 6 on Sharah-e-Faisal, Karachi, and operates six days a week with classes running until 9 PM to accommodate students enrolled in evening programs. Parking facilities are available at both campuses for students and visitors.

title: FAST-NUCES campuses in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Faisalabad, and Peshawar offer bus transport services along specific
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES campuses
content: FAST-NUCES campuses in Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Faisalabad, and Peshawar offer bus transport services along specific city routes to help students commute to and from campus. Routes and schedules are determined by each campus based on student demand. Students should contact their campus administration for the most current transport route information and applicable charges.

title: Students applying to FAST-NUCES who want to know more about the fee structure for a specific campus should note that fee
applicable_campus: lying to FAST-NUCES who want to know more about the fee structure for a specific campus
content: Students applying to FAST-NUCES who want to know more about the fee structure for a specific campus should note that fee details may vary slightly between campuses and are updated annually. The most accurate and current fee information is always available at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/FeeStructure. Students should always verify fee information directly from the official source before making financial decisions.

title: Provincial endowment scholarships are available for students admitted to FAST-NUCES from Punjab, Sindh, Khyber Pakhtunkh
content: Provincial endowment scholarships are available for students admitted to FAST-NUCES from Punjab, Sindh, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, and Balochistan. The Punjab Educational Endowment Fund, Sindh Endowment Board, and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Education Foundation each run scholarship programs that FAST-NUCES students can apply to. These programs provide tuition support for deserving students who meet the income and academic eligibility criteria set by the respective provincial bodies.

title: The Ehsaas Undergraduate Scholarship Program, run by the federal government, is also available to eligible FAST-NUCES st
applicable_campus: cedures and eligibility are available through the Ehsaas program website and the campus
content: The Ehsaas Undergraduate Scholarship Program, run by the federal government, is also available to eligible FAST-NUCES students. This program provides financial assistance to students from low-income families who are enrolled in accredited universities. FAST-NUCES students meeting the Ehsaas eligibility criteria can apply through the program's portal. Details on application procedures and eligibility are available through the Ehsaas program website and the campus financial assistance office.

title: HEC Need-Based Scholarships are available for students enrolled in MS and PhD programs at FAST-NUCES
applicable_campus: sted in HEC scholarships should apply through the HEC portal and notify the FAST campus
applicable_program: olarships are available for students enrolled in MS and PhD programs
content: HEC Need-Based Scholarships are available for students enrolled in MS and PhD programs at FAST-NUCES. These scholarships cover tuition fees and provide a monthly stipend. PhD students selected under HEC Indigenous Fellowships receive a monthly stipend of approximately Rs 60,000 in addition to full tuition fee coverage. Students interested in HEC scholarships should apply through the HEC portal and notify the FAST campus financial assistance office to ensure the scholarship is linked to their enrollment record.

title: Students admitted to FAST-NUCES are encouraged to approach the campus financial assistance office at the beginning of ea
applicable_campus: Students admitted to FAST-NUCES are encouraged to approach the campus
content: Students admitted to FAST-NUCES are encouraged to approach the campus financial assistance office at the beginning of each semester to inquire about available scholarships and loan programs. The university maintains a notice board and online announcements through which scholarship application windows are communicated. Missing the announced application window typically means the student will have to wait for the next semester to apply.

title: The FAST-NUCES admissions cycle for Fall typically begins in April or May with the announcement of test dates and the op
content: The FAST-NUCES admissions cycle for Fall typically begins in April or May with the announcement of test dates and the opening of the online application portal. The application deadline is usually in early July, the admission test is held from mid-July onward, and results are announced approximately one week after the test. Seat confirmation and fee deposit follow shortly after, with the semester commencing in late August or early September. These timelines are approximate and may vary each year. The confirmed schedule is always announced on the official website.

title: All information provided by a candidate in the FAST-NUCES admission application form is subject to verification
content: All information provided by a candidate in the FAST-NUCES admission application form is subject to verification. The university reserves the right to cancel the admission of any student whose submitted information is found to be incorrect, incomplete, or intentionally misleading at any stage of the admission process, including after enrollment. Cancellation under these circumstances does not entitle the candidate to any fee refund.

title: FAST-NUCES does not require any interview for undergraduate admissions
content: FAST-NUCES does not require any interview for undergraduate admissions. The selection is entirely merit-based and objective, derived from the weighted combination of academic results and admission test performance. This transparent process ensures equal opportunity for all applicants regardless of socioeconomic background or personal circumstances.

title: Students who have already graduated from FAST-NUCES and wish to pursue a higher degree at the university are not require
applicable_campus: to any FAST-NUCES graduate who is admitted to a postgraduate program at any FAST campus
content: Students who have already graduated from FAST-NUCES and wish to pursue a higher degree at the university are not required to pay the admission fee again. This benefit applies to any FAST-NUCES graduate who is admitted to a postgraduate program at any FAST campus. All other admission requirements, including the relevant entry test or GAT score, still apply.

title: FAST-NUCES provides sample test papers and a test pattern overview on the official website to help candidates prepare
content: FAST-NUCES provides sample test papers and a test pattern overview on the official website to help candidates prepare. The test pattern page at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/TestPattern includes program-specific formats and section-wise details. Candidates are strongly advised to review this page before finalizing their preparation plan, as the exact section structure and weightage can be confirmed there.

title: The university helpline number 111-128-128 is available for prospective students to ask questions about the admission pr
applicable_campus: For students unable to find answers through these channels, visiting the campus
content: The university helpline number 111-128-128 is available for prospective students to ask questions about the admission process, test dates, eligibility, and general program information. Inquiries can also be sent by email to admissions@nu.edu.pk. For students unable to find answers through these channels, visiting the campus admissions office in person during working hours is also an available option.

title: Campus life at FAST-NUCES is designed to offer students a balanced environment that combines academic rigor with extracu
applicable_campus: Each campus
content: Campus life at FAST-NUCES is designed to offer students a balanced environment that combines academic rigor with extracurricular development. Each campus hosts a variety of student-led societies and clubs that allow students to explore interests in computing, engineering, management, arts, culture, literature, adventure, and community service. Active participation in these societies is encouraged by the university as a means of developing well-rounded graduates.

title: The Lahore campus of FAST-NUCES has one of the most active society ecosystems in the university
applicable_campus: The Lahore campus
content: The Lahore campus of FAST-NUCES has one of the most active society ecosystems in the university. One of its flagship societies is SOFTEC, which stands for Software Exhibition and Competition. SOFTEC is the largest student body at the Lahore campus and organizes an annual technology event that serves as a platform for student innovation, industry interaction, and talent recognition. The event draws participants and industry representatives from across Pakistan.

title: The ACM chapter at FAST-NUCES Lahore is affiliated with the Association for Computing Machinery, an international profes
applicable_campus: Similar ACM chapters are active at other FAST campuses
content: The ACM chapter at FAST-NUCES Lahore is affiliated with the Association for Computing Machinery, an international professional organization for computing professionals and researchers. The chapter organizes academic seminars, programming contests, and knowledge-sharing sessions to connect students with the broader computing community. Similar ACM chapters are active at other FAST campuses as well.

title: The NUCES Media Group at the Lahore campus provides a creative outlet for students interested in photography, videograph
applicable_campus: The NUCES Media Group at the Lahore campus
content: The NUCES Media Group at the Lahore campus provides a creative outlet for students interested in photography, videography, graphic design, short films, animation, and creative writing. It serves as a media production and communication body for campus events and activities. The society also publishes creative content and represents the media presence of the campus community.

title: The FAST Literary Society, known as FLS, is active at the Lahore campus and promotes a culture of reading and creative e
applicable_campus: The FAST Literary Society, known as FLS, is active at the Lahore campus
content: The FAST Literary Society, known as FLS, is active at the Lahore campus and promotes a culture of reading and creative expression. It organizes events such as book fairs, poetry slams, book clubs, and writing competitions. The society also publishes a literary magazine known as Highway, which serves as a platform for student writing and literary work.

title: The NUCES Fun Trekkers society, known as NFT, is one of the non-technical societies at the Lahore campus and has been ac
applicable_campus: kkers society, known as NFT, is one of the non-technical societies at the Lahore campus
content: The NUCES Fun Trekkers society, known as NFT, is one of the non-technical societies at the Lahore campus and has been active since 1998. It organizes adventurous outdoor activities and trips to various destinations across Pakistan and beyond. The society aims to provide students with experiences outside the academic environment and encourages exploration and physical activity.

title: FAST-NUCES also has a chapter of the American Society of Civil Engineers, known as ASCE NUCES, which operates primarily
applicable_campus: can Society of Civil Engineers, known as ASCE NUCES, which operates primarily at campuses
applicable_program: operates primarily at campuses that offer civil engineering programs
content: FAST-NUCES also has a chapter of the American Society of Civil Engineers, known as ASCE NUCES, which operates primarily at campuses that offer civil engineering programs. The chapter promotes awareness of developments in civil engineering, provides students with professional networking opportunities, and supports their growth as future engineers.

title: A music society is active at FAST-NUCES and focuses on promoting musical talent among students
applicable_campus: It organizes and participates in musical events both on and off campus
content: A music society is active at FAST-NUCES and focuses on promoting musical talent among students. It organizes and participates in musical events both on and off campus. The society is the primary platform through which musically inclined students can develop and showcase their skills in a structured setting.

title: A Global Leadership Initiative society is active at FAST-NUCES and focuses on developing leadership skills and internati
content: A Global Leadership Initiative society is active at FAST-NUCES and focuses on developing leadership skills and international awareness among students. The society organizes panel discussions, global awareness events, and skill-building programs. Its goal is to prepare students for roles in international professional environments by bridging academic learning with real-world challenges.

title: FAST-NUCES Islamabad Campus has organized over 85 convocations since its founding, reflecting the large and consistent v
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES Islamabad Campus
content: FAST-NUCES Islamabad Campus has organized over 85 convocations since its founding, reflecting the large and consistent volume of graduates produced annually. Convocation ceremonies at the Islamabad campus celebrate graduates from BS, BBA, MBA, MS, and PhD programs. These events are held on campus and represent a formal recognition of student achievement.

title: Sports facilities are available at FAST-NUCES campuses to support student physical activity and wellness
applicable_campus: Sports facilities are available at FAST-NUCES campuses
applicable_program: of campus community among students from different academic programs
content: Sports facilities are available at FAST-NUCES campuses to support student physical activity and wellness. Students can participate in inter-campus sports tournaments and other competitive events. Sports activities at FAST-NUCES promote teamwork, discipline, and a sense of campus community among students from different academic programs.

title: Cultural festivals and creative events are held throughout the academic year at FAST-NUCES campuses
applicable_campus: estivals and creative events are held throughout the academic year at FAST-NUCES campuses
content: Cultural festivals and creative events are held throughout the academic year at FAST-NUCES campuses. These include art exhibitions, music performances, and drama productions that bring together students from various departments. Such events serve as outlets for creativity and provide an informal social dimension to campus life beyond academic studies.

title: FAST-NUCES campuses in Faisalabad, Islamabad, Karachi, and Peshawar operate bus services along selected city routes to a
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES campuses
content: FAST-NUCES campuses in Faisalabad, Islamabad, Karachi, and Peshawar operate bus services along selected city routes to assist students in commuting to and from campus. These transport services reduce the burden of daily commuting for students who live in areas not immediately adjacent to the campus. Routes and schedules are determined by each campus based on student demand and city geography.

title: The Faisalabad and Peshawar campuses of FAST-NUCES have hostel facilities available for male students
