applicable_campus: The Faisalabad and Peshawar campuses
content: The Faisalabad and Peshawar campuses of FAST-NUCES have hostel facilities available for male students. Among all FAST campuses, only the Faisalabad campus maintains a university-managed hostel that accepts female students. At campuses without on-campus hostel facilities, students are directed to private hostel accommodations or independently arranged housing in nearby areas.

title: FAST-NUCES campuses maintain canteen and cafeteria facilities where students can purchase meals and refreshments during
applicable_campus: FAST-NUCES campuses
applicable_program: PM to accommodate working students and evening program
content: FAST-NUCES campuses maintain canteen and cafeteria facilities where students can purchase meals and refreshments during campus hours. The Karachi main campus operates on weekdays from 8 AM to 4 PM. The Karachi City Campus, located in PECHS Block 6 on Sharah-e-Faisal, operates six days a week and remains open until 9 PM to accommodate working students and evening program enrollees.

title: Parking facilities are available at FAST-NUCES campuses for students who commute by private car or motorcycle
applicable_campus: Parking facilities are available at FAST-NUCES campuses
content: Parking facilities are available at FAST-NUCES campuses for students who commute by private car or motorcycle. The Karachi main campus has a dedicated parking area outside the main gate for student and visitor vehicles. The City Campus in Karachi similarly provides parking spots to accommodate visitors and commuting students.

title: FAST-NUCES places emphasis on industry engagement and career preparation as part of its institutional mission
content: FAST-NUCES places emphasis on industry engagement and career preparation as part of its institutional mission. The university's strong network with technology companies, engineering firms, and business organizations provides students with access to internship opportunities, industry visits, and career placement support. Graduates of FAST-NUCES are widely employed across Pakistan and internationally in technology, engineering, and business sectors.

title: Research at FAST-NUCES is conducted across its School of Computing, School of Engineering, and School of Management
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
content: Research at FAST-NUCES is conducted across its School of Computing, School of Engineering, and School of Management. Faculty and students publish research in national and international academic journals and participate in funded research projects. The Islamabad campus has been recognized nationally for research contributions in the fields of artificial intelligence, software engineering, and computer networks.

title: International students are welcomed at FAST-NUCES and are encouraged to participate in the full range of campus activiti
applicable_campus: re welcomed at FAST-NUCES and are encouraged to participate in the full range of campus
content: International students are welcomed at FAST-NUCES and are encouraged to participate in the full range of campus activities, including student societies, cultural events, and sports programs. The university maintains a diverse student body and strives to offer an inclusive environment for students from different backgrounds and nationalities. Foreign students are required to submit a No Objection Certificate from the Ministry of Education of Pakistan upon enrollment.

title: FAST-NUCES participates in international academic collaborations through its Student Exchange Program, which enables enr
applicable_program: tional academic collaborations through its Student Exchange Program
content: FAST-NUCES participates in international academic collaborations through its Student Exchange Program, which enables enrolled students to spend time studying at partner universities abroad. These partnerships span multiple countries and allow students to earn academic credits and broaden their professional and cultural exposure. The program is managed through the university's international affairs office.

title: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NUCES houses three academic schools: the School of Computing, the School of Engineering, an
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
applicable_program: The School of Computing offers programs
content: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NUCES houses three academic schools: the School of Computing, the School of Engineering, and the School of Management. The School of Computing offers programs in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, and Data Science. The School of Engineering covers Electrical Engineering, and the School of Management covers programs in Business Administration, Accounting and Finance, and Management Sciences.

title: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NUCES is led by a team of senior academic professionals
applicable_campus: The Islamabad campus
content: The Islamabad campus of FAST-NUCES is led by a team of senior academic professionals. The campus has a Director who oversees administrative and academic operations. Heads of Department and Deans lead individual schools and departments. Faculty members at the Islamabad campus include researchers with postdoctoral qualifications from international universities, contributing to both teaching and research activities.

title: Pre-medical students who studied FSc with Biology, Physics, and Chemistry without additional Mathematics are not eligibl
applicable_program: nal Mathematics are not eligible for admission in Computing programs
content: Pre-medical students who studied FSc with Biology, Physics, and Chemistry without additional Mathematics are not eligible for admission in Computing programs at FAST-NUCES through the NTS NAT route or any other route without additional Mathematics. Additional Mathematics is a mandatory requirement for admission into Computing programs. Pre-medical students who have passed or are awaiting results of an additional Mathematics examination may apply, but those without any Mathematics qualification are only eligible for BBA and business-related programs.

title: FAST-NUCES opens undergraduate admissions exclusively in the Fall semester
applicable_campus: There is no Spring intake for any undergraduate program at any campus
applicable_program: There is no Spring intake for any undergraduate program
content: FAST-NUCES opens undergraduate admissions exclusively in the Fall semester. There is no Spring intake for any undergraduate program at any campus. Students who wish to apply for BS, BBA, or any other undergraduate degree must wait for the Fall admission cycle. This policy applies uniformly across all FAST campuses including Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, Chiniot-Faisalabad, and Multan.

title: ECAT is accepted as an alternative admission route for engineering and computing programs at FAST-NUCES
applicable_campus: However, the number of seats allocated to ECAT applicants at each campus
applicable_program: n alternative admission route for engineering and computing programs
content: ECAT is accepted as an alternative admission route for engineering and computing programs at FAST-NUCES. However, the number of seats allocated to ECAT applicants at each campus is limited and significantly fewer than the seats available for FAST NU test applicants. Students who are serious about securing admission are advised to appear for the FAST NU test as their primary route, as it offers access to a larger pool of available seats and results in a stronger competitive position.

title: Both the Basic Mathematics and the Advanced Mathematics sections of the FAST admission test are important and neither sh
content: Both the Basic Mathematics and the Advanced Mathematics sections of the FAST admission test are important and neither should be neglected during preparation. However, the best starting point for preparation is the Advanced Mathematics section, as it carries approximately 50 percent of the total marks in the computing and engineering test. After building a strong foundation in Advanced Mathematics, candidates should give attention to Basic Mathematics, Analytical Skills, and English.

title: The content studied in FSc Part 1 and Part 2, commonly referred to as 11th and 12th grade mathematics, is sufficient pre
content: The content studied in FSc Part 1 and Part 2, commonly referred to as 11th and 12th grade mathematics, is sufficient preparation for the Advanced Mathematics section of the FAST entry test. Students do not need to study topics beyond the standard FSc mathematics curriculum. The same applies to candidates who have studied A-Level mathematics, as the content covered in A-Level mathematics aligns with the level tested in the Advanced Mathematics section.

title: The tuition fee at FAST-NUCES is charged on a per-credit-hour basis
content: The tuition fee at FAST-NUCES is charged on a per-credit-hour basis. For the academic year 2025 to 2026, the tuition fee is Rs 11,000 per credit hour. A standard semester typically involves 17 to 19 credit hours, making the approximate per-semester tuition cost between Rs 187,000 and Rs 209,000 depending on the number of registered credit hours. Students can calculate their exact semester fee by multiplying the total credit hours they register for by the current per-credit-hour rate. The latest per-credit-hour fee can always be verified on the official FAST-NUCES fee structure page at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/FeeStructure.

title: The one-time admission fee at FAST-NUCES is Rs 30,000
applicable_program: d again if the student is later admitted to a higher degree program
content: The one-time admission fee at FAST-NUCES is Rs 30,000. This fee is non-refundable and is paid only at the time of initial enrollment. It is not charged again if the student is later admitted to a higher degree program at the same university. The admission fee is separate from the per-semester tuition fee and the refundable security deposit.

title: The FAST-NUCES admission test is conducted at all campuses across Pakistan simultaneously during the announced testing w
applicable_campus: The FAST-NUCES admission test is conducted at all campuses
content: The FAST-NUCES admission test is conducted at all campuses across Pakistan simultaneously during the announced testing window. A candidate who applies for admission at Islamabad or Lahore campus can appear for the test at the Peshawar campus, or any other campus that is nearest or most convenient. The test result is the same regardless of which campus location the test was taken at. Campus preference is selected during registration and does not affect where the candidate physically sits the test.

title: A candidate who gives the FAST admission test at one campus can apply for and receive admission at any other campus, pro
applicable_campus: A candidate who gives the FAST admission test at one campus
content: A candidate who gives the FAST admission test at one campus can apply for and receive admission at any other campus, provided their merit qualifies for that campus. The test is universal and the result is used across all campus merit lists based on the preferences selected during the application. Students should ensure they have applied for all campuses they wish to be considered for, as results from one campus test application do not automatically transfer to unapplied campuses.

title: There is no official hostel facility at the FAST-NUCES Islamabad campus
applicable_campus: There is no official hostel facility at the FAST-NUCES Islamabad campus
content: There is no official hostel facility at the FAST-NUCES Islamabad campus. Students who gain admission to the Islamabad campus and require accommodation must arrange private housing in nearby areas of the city. Several private hostels are available in sectors such as G-10, G-11, I-8, and I-9, which are close to the campus in H-11/4. Students can also use the Islamabad Metro Bus Service and other public transport for commuting from these areas to the campus.

title: Documents required to bring on the day of the admission test are specified on the admit card that each applicant prints
content: Documents required to bring on the day of the admission test are specified on the admit card that each applicant prints after paying the application fee. Candidates should carefully read all instructions mentioned on their admit card before the test day. The admit card itself is the primary document required at the test center. The test dates for each admission cycle are announced on the official FAST-NUCES website at nu.edu.pk and the admissions portal at admissions.nu.edu.pk.

title: Students wishing to transfer between FAST-NUCES campuses must appear for the FAST entry test and qualify on the merit of
applicable_campus: Students wishing to transfer between FAST-NUCES campuses
content: Students wishing to transfer between FAST-NUCES campuses must appear for the FAST entry test and qualify on the merit of their desired destination campus. After qualifying, the student's previously completed courses are reviewed for credit transfer. Courses in which the student earned a grade of C or above are eligible for transfer. Courses with grades below C are not transferred and must be repeated at the new campus. Students planning a campus transfer should contact the admissions office of the destination campus for specific procedural guidance.

title: In certain situations, a student who has completed at least two semesters and was originally eligible for a specific pro
applicable_campus: t two semesters and was originally eligible for a specific program at a specific campus
applicable_program: st two semesters and was originally eligible for a specific program
content: In certain situations, a student who has completed at least two semesters and was originally eligible for a specific program at a specific campus may be able to transfer to that campus without retaking the admission test. This exception is subject to conditions and must be confirmed directly with the admissions office of the relevant campus. The general rule remains that a test is required for campus transfers, and the exception applies only to specific qualifying circumstances.

title: Merit scholarships at FAST-NUCES are awarded to the top three position holders of each provincial examination board in t
applicable_campus: to the top three position holders on the FAST-NUCES admission merit list of each campus
content: Merit scholarships at FAST-NUCES are awarded to the top three position holders of each provincial examination board in the year of admission. Scholarships are also offered to the top three position holders on the FAST-NUCES admission merit list of each campus. The merit scholarship covers the full tuition fee for four years of uninterrupted undergraduate studies, provided the student maintains a semester GPA of 3.0 or higher in every semester. The scholarship is not available for courses taken in summer semesters or for repeated courses.

title: FAST-NUCES also provides need-based financial assistance in the form of interest-free loans
content: FAST-NUCES also provides need-based financial assistance in the form of interest-free loans. This assistance is limited to tuition fees and is renewed every semester based on the student's academic performance and financial need. Students with a household financial situation that cannot be verified through formal salary slips, such as those with parents in informal employment or private businesses without documented income, can still apply for financial assistance. The assessment is conducted through an interview process and the financial aid committee evaluates each case individually. Complete details of the scholarship and financial assistance programs are available at nu.edu.pk/Admissions/Scholarship.

title: For preparation of the FAST admission test, the YouTube channel Mathflix by Hashim Zia is recommended for mathematics pr
content: For preparation of the FAST admission test, the YouTube channel Mathflix by Hashim Zia is recommended for mathematics preparation. For the English section, the IQ Series by Amin Education is a useful resource. Past papers and study material for the FAST entry test are also available through FAST Vault, which can be accessed at fastvault.vercel.app. Practicing with past papers helps candidates understand the question format, time pressure, and difficulty level of the actual test.

title: The difficulty level of the FAST admission test is moderate on average
content: The difficulty level of the FAST admission test is moderate on average. The perceived difficulty varies from student to student depending on how thoroughly they have studied the FSc or equivalent curriculum and how much practice they have done with test-format questions. Candidates who are well-prepared in the subjects of Advanced Mathematics, Basic Mathematics, and analytical reasoning typically find the test manageable within the given time. Consistent practice with timed past papers is the most effective way to build both speed and accuracy.

title: Candidates who are still appearing in their board examinations and have not yet completed their FSc should ideally begin
content: Candidates who are still appearing in their board examinations and have not yet completed their FSc should ideally begin test preparation early without waiting for board results. Balancing preparation for both board exams and the FAST entry test simultaneously is possible with a structured study schedule. Starting early gives candidates more time to cover the full Advanced Mathematics syllabus, which carries the highest weightage. Waiting until after board results to begin test preparation significantly reduces available preparation time, as the FAST test is typically held in July.

title: Students who have a strong SAT score and are weighing whether to apply through the SAT route or the FAST NU test route s
applicable_campus: r SAT applicants depend on the pool of SAT applicants for that specific year and campus
content: Students who have a strong SAT score and are weighing whether to apply through the SAT route or the FAST NU test route should be aware that closing merits for SAT applicants depend on the pool of SAT applicants for that specific year and campus. Historical merit trends for the SAT category at specific campuses are not officially published by FAST-NUCES. Students with high SAT scores and strong O-Level equivalence percentages can consider applying through both routes simultaneously by paying the applicable fees, and will then compete on whichever merit list yields the better outcome for them.

title: If a candidate does not make the merit for their first preference campus, they will be considered for their second prefe
applicable_campus: If a candidate does not make the merit for their first preference campus
content: If a candidate does not make the merit for their first preference campus, they will be considered for their second preference campus only if they applied for it and their merit qualifies under that campus's cutoff. First-preference applicants at each campus are given priority over second or third-preference applicants competing for the same seats. Whether a candidate qualifies for their second campus is determined independently by that campus's merit list and is not automatic.

title: The FAST-NUCES admission test is a computer-based test conducted at specialized computer laboratories at each campus
applicable_campus: is a computer-based test conducted at specialized computer laboratories at each campus
content: The FAST-NUCES admission test is a computer-based test conducted at specialized computer laboratories at each campus. Each candidate is assigned an individual computer terminal for the duration of the test. The interface is simple and straightforward, displaying the current question, the remaining time for the active section, and five answer options for each multiple-choice question. Candidates do not need any prior experience with specialized software to navigate the test interface.

title: The test interface displays a running timer that is clearly visible to the candidate throughout each section
content: The test interface displays a running timer that is clearly visible to the candidate throughout each section. The timer counts down the remaining time for the active section in real time. There is no separate warning bell or alarm, but since the timer is always visible on screen, candidates can monitor their remaining time and manage their pace accordingly throughout each section.

title: The FAST admission test is divided into sections, each with its own dedicated timer
content: The FAST admission test is divided into sections, each with its own dedicated timer. Once a section's timer runs out, the system automatically moves the candidate to the next section. Candidates cannot jump freely between sections during the test. The order in which sections appear is randomized for each candidate, so the sequence of sections may differ from one test-taker to another.

title: Within a single active section, candidates are allowed to skip a question and return to it later before the section time
content: Within a single active section, candidates are allowed to skip a question and return to it later before the section timer expires. A candidate who finds a question difficult, such as a challenging Advanced Mathematics problem, can move past it and come back to attempt it before the section ends. However, once the time for a section runs out, the candidate cannot return to that section under any circumstances.

title: There is no built-in on-screen calculator available during the FAST admission test
content: There is no built-in on-screen calculator available during the FAST admission test. All calculations must be performed manually without any digital or physical calculator aid. Bringing a personal calculator to the test center is not permitted. Candidates are expected to solve all mathematical problems including those in the Advanced Mathematics section entirely by hand.

title: The test center provides candidates with blank sheets for rough work during the test
content: The test center provides candidates with blank sheets for rough work during the test. Candidates are not permitted to bring their own stationery including personal blank paper, clipboards, pencils, or erasers. All materials needed for rough work are provided by the university on the day of the test. Only one blank sheet is provided per candidate, and additional sheets are not issued during the test, so candidates should use their rough work space efficiently.

title: If a mathematical equation or text appears small on the screen during the test, candidates are not expected to encounter
content: If a mathematical equation or text appears small on the screen during the test, candidates are not expected to encounter a significant readability issue as the interface is designed to display questions clearly. Candidates who face any technical difficulty during the test should immediately notify the invigilator present in the examination hall for assistance.

title: There is no advantage or disadvantage to taking the FAST admission test on any particular day within the testing window
content: There is no advantage or disadvantage to taking the FAST admission test on any particular day within the testing window. The question bank used in the test operates on a randomized but defined selection pattern. Questions are not exhausted or made more difficult based on the day or sequence of the test. Candidates appearing on the first day face the same difficulty distribution as those appearing on the last day of the testing window.

title: The difficulty level of the FAST admission test does not vary based on which campus a candidate has selected as their fi
applicable_campus: The difficulty level of the FAST admission test does not vary based on which campus
content: The difficulty level of the FAST admission test does not vary based on which campus a candidate has selected as their first preference. All candidates, regardless of campus preference, take the same standardized test with the same difficulty level and section structure. Campus preference only affects which merit list the candidate is placed on after results are announced.

title: Negative marking in the FAST admission test is applied only when a candidate selects an incorrect answer
content: Negative marking in the FAST admission test is applied only when a candidate selects an incorrect answer. If a candidate leaves a question completely unanswered, no marks are deducted for that question. A blank or skipped question receives zero marks. Candidates who are uncertain about an answer should consider leaving it blank rather than guessing, to avoid the negative marking penalty.

title: The negative marking rules differ between sections
content: The negative marking rules differ between sections. In the Advanced Mathematics, Basic Mathematics, and Analytical Skills and IQ sections, one mark is deducted for every four incorrect answers, meaning each wrong answer costs 0.25 marks. In the English section, one mark is deducted for every twelve incorrect answers, making the English penalty significantly lighter. Unanswered questions in all sections receive zero marks and no deduction.

title: The Basic Mathematics section of the FAST admission test focuses on formula-based and logical problem-solving
content: The Basic Mathematics section of the FAST admission test focuses on formula-based and logical problem-solving. Topics in Basic Mathematics include ratios, percentages, profit and loss, arithmetic, basic algebra, cost and price calculations, and other practical quantitative reasoning problems. These questions are more straightforward in nature and are based on concepts from secondary school level mathematics.

title: The Advanced Mathematics section of the FAST admission test covers topics from FSc Part 1 and Part 2 mathematics
content: The Advanced Mathematics section of the FAST admission test covers topics from FSc Part 1 and Part 2 mathematics. These include sets, matrices, integration, differentiation, trigonometry, permutation and combination, probability, analytic geometry, vectors, and complex numbers. The Advanced Mathematics section is distinct from Basic Mathematics in that it tests deep knowledge of college-level mathematical concepts rather than practical arithmetic reasoning.

title: The Advanced Mathematics section carries the highest number of questions in the computing and engineering version of the
content: The Advanced Mathematics section carries the highest number of questions in the computing and engineering version of the FAST admission test, with approximately 50 questions. This makes it the longest and most time-consuming section for most candidates. Time management within this section is critical. If a candidate becomes stuck on a difficult problem, it is advisable to skip it and move on rather than spending disproportionate time on a single question.

title: A strong time management strategy for the FAST admission test is to attempt easier and more familiar questions first wit
content: A strong time management strategy for the FAST admission test is to attempt easier and more familiar questions first within each section and return to harder ones before the section timer ends. Since each section has its own locked timer and candidates cannot revisit a section after it closes, the focus during each section should be entirely on completing as many questions as accurately as possible within the allocated time.

title: FAST-NUCES allows students from other accredited institutions to apply for credit transfer and exemption when seeking ad
applicable_program: transfer and exemption when seeking admission into a degree program
content: FAST-NUCES allows students from other accredited institutions to apply for credit transfer and exemption when seeking admission into a degree program. The transfer policy requires that the courses taken at the previous institution are relevant to and compatible with the curriculum at FAST-NUCES. The Head of Department reviews the transcript and recommends compatible courses for exemption to the Dean of Faculty, who holds the final authority to approve or deny the exemption.

title: Credit exemption at FAST-NUCES cannot exceed 50 percent of the total credit hours of the degree program
applicable_program: percent of the total credit hours of the degree program
content: Credit exemption at FAST-NUCES cannot exceed 50 percent of the total credit hours of the degree program. For a standard eight-semester undergraduate program, this means a maximum of four semesters worth of courses can be exempted. Any credits beyond this limit will not be recognised regardless of the academic record at the previous institution.

title: Students applying for credit transfer from another university must first apply for admission to FAST-NUCES through the s
applicable_program: ssions process and meet the merit criteria for their chosen program
content: Students applying for credit transfer from another university must first apply for admission to FAST-NUCES through the standard admissions process and meet the merit criteria for their chosen program. After gaining admission, the student may then apply for course exemption to their department. The process is handled by the central academics office upon submission of the student's previous transcript.

title: Students who have completed an Associate degree from another accredited institution may apply for admission at FAST-NUCE
content: Students who have completed an Associate degree from another accredited institution may apply for admission at FAST-NUCES and subsequently apply for credit transfer. The specific courses that are transferable are determined by the departmental committee. As with all credit transfer cases, no more than 50 percent of the degree credit hours can be transferred under HEC rules.

title: A student currently enrolled at FAST-NUCES who wishes to change their degree program is required to appear in the FAST a
applicable_program: ly enrolled at FAST-NUCES who wishes to change their degree program
content: A student currently enrolled at FAST-NUCES who wishes to change their degree program is required to appear in the FAST admission test for the new program and qualify based on merit. Upon qualifying, the student is registered as a new student in the new degree program. Only credits from relevant courses in which a grade of C or higher was obtained are transferable to the new program, and the CGPA earned in the previous program is not carried over.

title: Campus transfer between FAST-NUCES campuses is possible but requires a separate process
applicable_campus: Campus transfer between FAST-NUCES campuses
content: Campus transfer between FAST-NUCES campuses is possible but requires a separate process. A student wishing to transfer from one campus to another must reappear in the FAST entry test after completing at least two semesters at the current campus. Transfer is only possible if the student achieves the required merit for the destination campus. All previously passed courses must carry a grade of C or above; otherwise, the relevant courses must be repeated at the new campus.

title: When a student transfers from one campus to another within FAST-NUCES, their CGPA continues from their previous record
applicable_campus: When a student transfers from one campus
content: When a student transfers from one campus to another within FAST-NUCES, their CGPA continues from their previous record. The academic history and earned credit hours are preserved in the student's record. The student must meet all remaining degree requirements as defined by the destination campus upon joining.

title: Students applying to multiple campuses of FAST-NUCES during the admission process are required to pay an additional fee
applicable_campus: Students applying to multiple campuses
content: Students applying to multiple campuses of FAST-NUCES during the admission process are required to pay an additional fee of Rs 500 per additional campus beyond the primary choice. Candidates are evaluated only for the campus they have selected as their preferred campus during the admission test. Selection results for each campus are provided separately based on campus-specific merit.

title: FAST-NUCES does not officially publish closing merit or merit list cutoffs on its website or social media platforms
applicable_campus: Students who wish to know the closing aggregate for a particular campus
content: FAST-NUCES does not officially publish closing merit or merit list cutoffs on its website or social media platforms. Students who wish to know the closing aggregate for a particular campus and program are advised to directly contact the relevant campus admissions office. The campus will provide this information upon request.

title: Undergraduate admissions at FAST-NUCES are exclusively conducted during the Fall semester
applicable_campus: This policy is consistent across all campuses
applicable_program: There is no Spring intake for any undergraduate program
content: Undergraduate admissions at FAST-NUCES are exclusively conducted during the Fall semester. There is no Spring intake for any undergraduate program. Students who miss the Fall admission cycle must wait for the next academic year to apply. This policy is consistent across all campuses and all undergraduate programs.

title: A student whose application was not selected for one program at FAST-NUCES, such as BS Computer Science, is not automati
applicable_program: A student whose application was not selected for one program
content: A student whose application was not selected for one program at FAST-NUCES, such as BS Computer Science, is not automatically considered for a different program such as BS Electrical Engineering or BBA. Each program has its own separate merit list, and selection for one program does not confer eligibility for another. Students wishing to be considered for multiple programs must indicate this during the application process and pay the applicable fees.

title: FAST-NUCES has a policy on Generative AI use in academics that provides guidance for both students and faculty
content: FAST-NUCES has a policy on Generative AI use in academics that provides guidance for both students and faculty. The university acknowledges that AI tools can be used responsibly to support learning. However, using AI to complete assessments and presenting AI-generated work as one's own is treated as a form of academic dishonesty. The policy is designed to ensure that academic assessments reflect genuine student effort and understanding.

title: The Student Activity Fund at FAST-NUCES is a per-semester charge contributed by every enrolled student
applicable_campus: s fund supports extracurricular activities, student societies, events, and other campus
content: The Student Activity Fund at FAST-NUCES is a per-semester charge contributed by every enrolled student. This fund supports extracurricular activities, student societies, events, and other campus programs. The amount charged per student per semester for this fund is a fixed amount and is payable along with the tuition fee at the start of each semester.

title: FAST-NUCES is a non-profit organization that charges subsidized tuition fees to all enrolled students
applicable_campus: ncouraged to apply for this assistance at the start of each semester through the campus
content: FAST-NUCES is a non-profit organization that charges subsidized tuition fees to all enrolled students. The university recognizes that even subsidized fees may be unaffordable for some students and provides need-based financial assistance in the form of interest-free loans. Students in genuine financial need are encouraged to apply for this assistance at the start of each semester through the campus financial assistance process.

title: Merit scholarships are awarded to the top three position holders of each examination board in the year of admission
content: Merit scholarships are awarded to the top three position holders of each examination board in the year of admission. Scholarships are also offered to the top three position holders in the university admission merit list. These scholarships cover the full tuition fee for four years of uninterrupted studies with a full course load. Continuation of the merit scholarship is subject to maintaining a semester GPA of three point zero or higher.

title: The Higher Education Commission provides scholarships to students studying for MS and PhD degrees at the university
content: The Higher Education Commission provides scholarships to students studying for MS and PhD degrees at the university. These scholarships cover tuition fees, a monthly stipend, and a book allowance.

title: The Government of Punjab provides forty-two scholarships to indigent students having domicile of Punjab
applicable_campus: Newly admitted students in any campus
content: The Government of Punjab provides forty-two scholarships to indigent students having domicile of Punjab. Newly admitted students in any campus of the university can apply for this scholarship. It covers a portion of the tuition fee for four-year undergraduate studies. The remaining tuition fee can be provided by the university as Qarz-e-Hasna.

title: The Sindh Government offers scholarships to students of the Karachi campus on a need-cum-merit basis for both undergradu
applicable_campus: The Sindh Government offers scholarships to students of the Karachi campus
content: The Sindh Government offers scholarships to students of the Karachi campus on a need-cum-merit basis for both undergraduate and graduate studies. This scholarship covers the full tuition fee and is renewable annually.

title: The quota is sixty percent for students from the rural sector and forty percent for students from the urban sector
content: The quota is sixty percent for students from the rural sector and forty percent for students from the urban sector. Approximately twenty-five new scholarships are offered every year under this scheme.

title: The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa government offers scholarships to students of the Peshawar campus on a need-cum-merit basis for b
applicable_campus: he Khyber Pakhtunkhwa government offers scholarships to students of the Peshawar campus
content: The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa government offers scholarships to students of the Peshawar campus on a need-cum-merit basis for bachelor of science and master of science studies with an equal male-to-female ratio. The scholarship covers eighty percent of the tuition fees for the entire duration of the program.

title: Interested students can apply online on the foundation website after the annual call is circulated and will be interview
applicable_campus: oundation website after the annual call is circulated and will be interviewed on campus
content: Interested students can apply online on the foundation website after the annual call is circulated and will be interviewed on campus.

title: The Baluchistan Government offers scholarships to students of the Karachi campus on a need-cum-merit basis for both unde
applicable_campus: The Baluchistan Government offers scholarships to students of the Karachi campus
content: The Baluchistan Government offers scholarships to students of the Karachi campus on a need-cum-merit basis for both undergraduate and graduate studies. The scholarship covers the full tuition fee and is renewable annually.

title: The Old Students Association of FAST arranges financial assistance for students who cannot afford to pay their full fee
content: The Old Students Association of FAST arranges financial assistance for students who cannot afford to pay their full fee.

title: Various community-based scholarships are offered by committees and foundations such as the Memon Community, Bohra Commun
content: Various community-based scholarships are offered by committees and foundations such as the Memon Community, Bohra Community, INFAQ Foundation, and Agha Khan Foundation.

title: For the Karachi campus, the Alumni Association, Hindu Community, and Agha Khan Foundation also provide scholarships in l
applicable_campus: For the Karachi campus
content: For the Karachi campus, the Alumni Association, Hindu Community, and Agha Khan Foundation also provide scholarships in limited numbers.

title: The university may utilize the services of its students in different activities
content: The university may utilize the services of its students in different activities. Students are compensated for rendering these services through assistantships.

title: Commercial banks such as PICIC Commercial Bank, NIB Bank, Bank Alfalah, Bank Islami, and MCB offer loans to students
content: Commercial banks such as PICIC Commercial Bank, NIB Bank, Bank Alfalah, Bank Islami, and MCB offer loans to students.

title: FAST arranges financial assistance in the form of interest-free need-based study loans for bright indigent students
content: FAST arranges financial assistance in the form of interest-free need-based study loans for bright indigent students. This assistance is subject to renewal every semester based on the student's academic performance. Financial assistance is limited to tuition fees only and is discontinued if the student's CGPA falls below the minimum specified level. Loan recipients must take the full load of courses offered.

title: Applicants seeking a study loan should apply using the prescribed form available from all campuses or downloadable from
applicable_campus: s seeking a study loan should apply using the prescribed form available from all campuses
content: Applicants seeking a study loan should apply using the prescribed form available from all campuses or downloadable from the university website.

title: The repayment of the study loan starts three months after graduation or soon after getting employment whichever is earli
content: The repayment of the study loan starts three months after graduation or soon after getting employment whichever is earlier. The total amount has to be repaid within a period of four years after graduation. Students are required to sign a contract allowing future employers to deduct loan repayments at source and remit them to the university.
